MP/CÁC DẠNG CÂU HỎI TRONG ĐỀ THI/ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU/Bài 2 Luyện tập bài đọc 8 câu (Buổi 2).pdf
vocab_context_group | câu: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 | trang 1-2 | rule_high
[Page 1] LUYỆN TẬP ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU (BUỔI 2) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn tập cơ bản, tổng hợp các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following passage about dealing with problems and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 8. Problems are a part of life. Arguments can happen with friends and family over a variety of issues. What is important, though, is how you deal with problems. Arguments with friends can make you feel bad or even angry. It is important to explain your feelings without blaming the other person. Share how you feel by using "I" sentences. For example, don't say "You are always telling me what to do," but "I feel annoyed when you don't listen to me." To solve a problem, you must also listen to how the other person feels and accept that he or she may see a problem differently. Showing your understanding of the other person makes them want to solve the problem. In the end, what is important is to find a solution and become friends again. Families can also have problems. The generation gap can cause parents and children to see things differently, making both feel confused or disappointed. Remember that parents and older family members have years of life experience and want the best for you, so listen to what they say. When you have a problem, try to set a time to sit down and talk quietly. Breathe and count to ten if you start to feel angry or anxious. Be prepared not to get exactly what you want but to find a solution everyone can agree on. We value our families and can solve any problems through caring and communication. (Adapted from Bright)
Question 1. The word blaming in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______.   
A. complimenting 
B. helping 
C. accusing 
D. ignoring
Question 2. The word them in paragraph 2 refers to ______.   
A. family members 
B. friends 
C. the other person 
D. feelings
Question 3. The word disappointed in paragraph 3 could be best replaced by ______.   
A. hopeful 
B. upset 
C. delighted 
D. hesitant
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
A. It’s crucial to compromise for a solution that satisfies everyone involved.  
B. Always prioritise what you want and encourage others to agree with you. 
C. Insist on your preferences to ensure others find them agreeable. 
 
D. Ensure all parties get exactly what they desire for a fair solution.
Question 5. According to the passage, which of the following best helps in dealing with problems? 
A. Avoiding conversations about the generation gap 
 
B. Always trying to get what you want 
C. Setting aside time to talk calmly 
 
D. Ignoring family members’ advice 
 
 

[Page 2]
Question 6. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. The generation gap only causes confusion in families. 
 
B. Using “I” sentences is likely to worsen conflicts with others. 
C. Conflicts can arise in families because of different perspectives. 
 
D. Only by talking honestly can family conflicts be resolved.
Question 7. In which paragraph does the writer mention the impact of age on perspectives in family issues? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 8. In which paragraph does the writer illustrate a piece of advice by using examples? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
vocab_context_group | câu: 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 | trang 3-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage about global warming and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 9 to 16. From melting arctic ice caps to forest fires, global warming is heating up the planet and damaging the Earth's ecosystems. Global warming is the rise of the Earth's temperatures caused by an increase of CO2 and other greenhouse gas emissions. Global warming has increased very much in the past 100 years because people are using more and more electricity and driving more cars. We are also cutting down more trees that clean the air. Scientists have been studying the effects of global warming on different ecosystems for a long time and what they have found is quite worrying. The rising temperatures have caused many problems in ecosystems. Arctic ice is melting, causing sea levels to rise and floods in seaside areas. As a result, animals and plants are losing their homes. As oceans and rivers become warmer, the water temperatures keep changing. Some marine animals cannot live in these new conditions. Since the 1970s, populations of fish in lakes and rivers have decreased by 76 percent. Global warming is also causing extreme weather events. Other ecosystems, like deserts and grasslands, are experiencing terrible droughts and certain wildlife and plant species that depend on water are dying. Due to the high temperatures, wildfires are destroying whole ecosystems such as forests. There has been a serious rise in endangered species in recent years that could result in many animals becoming extinct. The loss of plants and animals causes problems for all living things in an ecosystem. We need to take action to stop global warming and save the Earth's ecosystems before it's too late. (Adapted from Bright)
Question 9. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a caused of global warming in paragraph 1? 
A. People are cleaning the air. 
B. People are consuming more electricity. 
C. People are using more cars for travel. 
D. People are cutting down more trees.
Question 10. The word worrying in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. promising   
B. confusing  
C. typical 
D. natural
Question 11. The word they in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. people   
B. greenhouse gases   C. scientists   
D. ecosystems
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
A. Wildfires in forests are the main cause of high temperatures in these ecosystems.  
B. Whole ecosystems, like forests, are being destroyed because of the high temperatures. 
C. The main result of high temperatures is the increase in forested areas in ecosystems. 
 
D. Wildfires are causing high temperatures to spread across ecosystems like forests. 
 
 
 

[Page 3]
Question 13. The phrase result in in paragraph 4 could be best replaced by ______.   
A. prevent 
B. focus on 
C. lead to 
D. change
Question 14. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Scientists have just begun studying the effects of global warming on ecosystems.  
B. Serious droughts and wildfires only occur in ecosystems like deserts and grasslands. 
C. Fish populations declined by 76 percent in lakes and rivers during the 1970s. 
 
D. The loss of plants and animals has a negative impact on all living things in an ecosystem.
Question 15. In which paragraph does the writer mention specific issues faced by seaside areas? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 16. In which paragraph does the writer call for prompt action? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

MP/CÁC DẠNG CÂU HỎI TRONG ĐỀ THI/ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU/Bài 3 Luyện tập bài đọc 8 câu (Buổi 3).pdf
arrangement_group | câu: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 | trang 1-2 | rule_high
[Page 1] LUYỆN TẬP ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU (BUỔI 3) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn tập cơ bản, tổng hợp các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following passage about learning disorders and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 8. Many people have learning disorders. They suffer in school: other children bully them or make fun of them and call them 'slow'. However, people can get over their learning disorders and have successful careers with the help and support of family, teachers, and doctors, as these examples prove. At school, Michael Phelps could not sit still and found it difficult to concentrate. But then he found his passion for sports. His reading improved when he read books about sports and when the problems reflected his interests. Through hard work and never missing a day's practice, Michael became an Olympic champion, winning twenty-eight medals, twenty-three of them gold. In the world of autism, Dr Temple Grandin is an important voice. Autistic herself, she is an expert on how to teach people with autism, and she has written six books about the condition. School was difficult for her because she didn't think in the same way as other children. At school, her science teacher became her mentor. With his help and support, Grandin became interested in animal science. She later became a professor of animal science at Colorado State University. Keira Knightley found out she had dyslexia at the age of six. She believes that her problems with reading and writing made her strong. Knightley struggled with reading and writing, but she dreamt of being an actress, so she had to read scripts. She had to work harder than other children to improve her literacy. Later she finished school with top grades. So don't worry if you are different - you never know where your differences could lead you. (Adapted from English Discovery)
Question 1. The word them in paragraph 1 refers to _______. 
A. children 
B. careers 
C. examples 
D. people
Question 2. The phrase get over in paragraph is OPPOSITE in meaning to ________. 
A. struggle with 
B. turn down 
C. deal with 
D. bring about
Question 3. According to the passage, what factor helps people overcome learning disorders?  
A. Achieving high grades 
B. A successful career   
C. Support from other people 
D. Reading books regularly
Question 4. The word reflected in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to ________. 
A. contrasted 
B. demonstrated 
C. encouraged 
D. affected
Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
A. She found school challenging as her way of thinking differed from that of other children. 
B. She found it difficult to think differently from her classmates during school.  
C. Unlike other children, she struggled in school due to learning differently from them.  
D. Her challenges in school were caused by not understanding other children's thinking styles. 
 
 
 
 

[Page 2]
Question 6. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Grandin’s science teacher inspired her to become a professor of animal science. 
B. Despite her dyslexia, Knightley tried to improve her literacy to pursue an acting career. 
C. Only by never missing a day’s practice could Michael become an Olympic champion. 
D. Grandin has published six books on teaching people with autism about animal science.
Question 7. In which paragraph does the writer mention a role model’s influence?  
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 8. In which paragraph does the writer explore how individual interests helped overcome learning 
challenges?  
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 5
arrangement_group | câu: 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 | trang 3-4 | rule_high
Read the following passage about Sapa and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 9 to 16. Sapa has become a favourite destination for eco-tourists. This remote part of Việt Nam is 315 km northwest of Hà Nội, near the Việt Nam-China border. Sapa is famous for the beautiful landscapes of Fansipan Mountain as well as rainforests and rice fields. The Hàm Rồng area of Sapa is 1,800 meters above sea level, so it is cool and full of fresh air. One thing that you should not miss in Sapa is the Hàm Rồng flower garden. It is full of bright-coloured flowers. You can take a 30-minute walk from it to the top of the mountain. Another place to see is Fansipan, known as "The Roof of Indochina". It is the highest mountain in the Hoàng Liên Sơn Range. The height of the mountain is 3,143 meters. Fansipan is only 9 km from Sapa town. You can join a trekking tour. It's not an easy trek, but the view from the top of the mountain is worth trying. Trekkers can see beautiful fields of flowers and rainforests. These wilderness areas have not been deforested, so they remain their natural beauty. Don't forget the locals in Sapa. There are interesting villagers with unique lifestyles. Therefore, spending time in the local villages on your trip is highly recommended. Please remember that Sapa is beautiful, and we want to keep it that way. So please follow the guidelines that your ecotour leader gives you. We want others to enjoy this special place too. (Adapted from English Discovery)
Question 9. Which of the following is NOT mentioned about Sapa in paragraph 1? 
A. It is famous for its beautiful landscapes. B. It is cool and full of fresh air. 
C. It is a tourist attraction. 
D. It is an isolated urban area.
Question 10. The word it in paragraph 2 refers to ________. 
A. the top of the mountain 
B. the Hàm Rồng flower garden 
C. the Việt Nam-China border 
D. Fansipan Mountain
Question 11. The word deforested in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to ________. 
A. cut down 
B. taken up 
C. turned down 
D. carried out
Question 12. The word unique in paragraph 4 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ________. 
A. special 
B. normal 
C. standard 
D. pleasant
Question 13. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 5? 
A. Sapa is beautiful, so we should all work to keep it looking more attractive.  
B. Sapa is beautiful, and we can protect its original charm at all costs. 
C. Since Sapa is beautiful, we should ensure that it stays this way.  
D. Keeping Sapa beautiful is important to make it even more appealing. 
 
 
 

[Page 3]
Question 14. Which of the following is NOT true according to the passage? 
A. Trekking tours to Fansipan are available but they are quite challenging. 
B. Bright-coloured flowers are a highlight of the Hàm Rồng flower garden. 
C. Spending time with the locals in Sapa can bring exciting experiences. 
D. To preserve the beauty of Sapa, it is vital to listen to the guidelines of the locals.
Question 15. In which paragraph does the writer discuss the geological features of Sapa? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 16. In which paragraph does the writer mention an activity that tourists to Sapa can try? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3  
D. Paragraph 4

[Page 1] LUYỆN TẬP ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU (BUỔI 4) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn tập cơ bản, tổng hợp các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following passage about the paleo diet and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 8. Modern supermarkets give us many choices. There are colourful fruits and vegetables, fresh fish and meat, many kinds of bread, eggs, yoghourt, and cheese. But at the same time, people in the modern world are suffering from health problems. Could the food we eat be causing some of these problems? One explanation for modern health problems is that they began when human beings became farmers about 12,000 years ago. Around that time, people started growing and eating crops, such as wheat and rice. Some people believe that returning to an earlier way of eating a "paleo diet" that includes only meat, fish, and fruits and vegetables-might be a solution to our modern health problems. A paleo diet is special because it doesn't include grains, dairy foods, or legumes, such as peanuts or beans. For many of us, it is not possible to prepare our favourite dishes without these ingredients. But people who follow a paleo diet only eat foods people ate before farming began. They believe this is a more natural and healthier way to eat. For Dr. Peter Ungar, the truth is not so simple. In human history, people have eaten a wide variety of foods. Early human beings lived in different places with different climates, so many kinds of food were available to our ancestors even before farming began. Dr. Ungar says our ability to eat in many different ways and to be healthy whether we live in the Arctic or in the tropics is the important thing. One thing is certain, though: If you do decide to try a paleo diet, remember that your meals might actually have less variety than the meals of your early ancestors. (Adapted from Explore New Worlds)
Question 1. The phrase suffering from in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ________. 
A. abandoning 
B. experiencing 
C. requiring 
D. accessing
Question 2. The word they in paragraph 2 refers to _________. 
A. farmers 
B. human beings 
C. crops 
D. health problems
Question 3. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an element in a paleo diet? 
A. fish 
B. dairy products 
C. meat 
D. fruits and vegetables
Question 4. The word solution in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ________. 
A. answer 
B. potential 
C. challenge 
D. growth
Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
They believe this is a more natural and healthier way to eat. 
A. They consider this approach a more original and nutritious diet.  
B. No other approach is seen as healthier and more natural than this diet.  
C. This is thought to be a healthier and more natural way of eating.  
D. Many regard this as the healthiest way to consume food. 
 
 

 

[Page 2]
Question 6. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. The paleo diet today contains more ingredients than that in the past. 
B. Preparing dishes that follow the paleo diet is impossible for everyone. 
C. Our ancestors had followed the paleo diet even before farming appeared.  
D. Dr. Ungar highlights the importance of maintaining health through diverse diets.
Question 7. In which paragraph does the writer discuss opinions held by a researcher? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 8. In which paragraph does the writer mention what sets the paleo diet apart from other diets? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
vocab_context_group | câu: 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 | trang 3-4 | rule_high
Read the following passage about streets for people and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 9 to 16. Walking is a great way to get around. For short trips, for exercise, or just for fun, walking can be better than driving or riding. But in many cities, walking can also be dangerous. Cars, trucks, and motorcycles are a danger to pedestrians, and sometimes there are accidents. David Engwicht, from Brisbane, Australia, wants to do something about this. His book, Reclaiming Our Cities and Towns, has a simple message: We need to take back our streets and make them better places for walking. In the past, Engwicht says, streets belonged to everybody. Children played there, and people walked to work or to stores. Now, however, most city engineers design streets for vehicles. People stay inside buildings to get away from the crowded sidewalks, the noisy streets, and the dangerous traffic. Unfortunately, this gives them less contact with their neighbours. Many cities are working to make their streets safer for pedestrians. There are new crosswalks on the streets and more traffic lights and bicycle lanes. The city of Florence, Italy, only allows cars and buses with special permits to drive on its historic city streets. In Boston, US, the Slow Streets Programme gives some neighbourhoods more stop signs and a 20 mph (32 kph) speed limit. Engwicht travels around the world, helping people think differently about pedestrians, streets, and neighbourhoods. Whether we live in a small town or a city with a population in the millions, Engwicht says we should think of streets as our "outdoor living room." Changing the traffic is just the beginning. In the future, streets may again be safe places for people, and walking will be an even better form of transportation than it is now. (Adapted from Explore New Worlds)
Question 9. According to paragraph 1, people walk for all of the following EXCEPT ______. 
A. long commute 
B. exercise 
 
C. fun  
 
D. short trips
Question 10. The word dangerous in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. scary 
 
B. occasional  
C. safe 
 
 
D. protective
Question 11. The word them in paragraph 1 refers to ________. 
A. places 
 
B. streets 
 
C. accidents  
D. cities
Question 12. The phrase get away from in paragraph 2 can be best replaced by ________. 
A. endure 
 
B. avoid 
 
C. prevent 
 
D. delay 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]
Question 13. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
The city of Florence, Italy, only allows cars and buses with special permits to drive on its historic 
city streets.  
A. Only vehicles with permits can access the historical streets of Florence, Italy.  
B. Florence’s historic streets are open to every car and bus, regardless of permits.  
C. Only permitted cars and buses are allowed to drive through Florence’s historic city streets.  
D. Florence, Italy, restricts most traffic in its historic streets, allowing only cars and buses.
Question 14. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Engwicht inspires others to think differently about streets through his book. 
B. The Slow Streets Programme imposes a speed limit on all neighbourhoods.  
C. Streets were only restricted to small children and shoppers in the past. 
D. Efforts are underway to turn streets into safer places for pedestrians.
Question 15. In which paragraph does the writer suggest a vision for the future? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 16. In which paragraph does the writer explore the drawbacks of current street designs? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

MP/CÁC DẠNG CÂU HỎI TRONG ĐỀ THI/ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU/Bài 5 Luyện tập bài đọc 8 câu (Buổi 5).pdf
vocab_context_group | câu: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 | trang 1-2 | rule_high
[Page 1] LUYỆN TẬP ĐỌC HIỂU 8 CÂU (BUỔI 5) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn tập cơ bản, tổng hợp các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following passage about a tale of two guys and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 8. When Guy Goma went for a job interview at the BBC, he was very astonished by the interviewer' questions. He'd applied for a job in the computing department. Why did the woman want him to answer questions about a legal case involving a famous music company? He was even more shocked when he found that he was answering these questions live on television. Mr Goma had been waiting in a reception room at Television Centre when an assistant came up and asked him if he was Guy. When he said ‘yes', she told him to follow her to a TV studio. The next thing he knew, he was on national television. At the same time, Guy Kewney, the editor of Newswireless.net, sat in another reception room wondering why he was waiting so long. Mr Goma did his best to answer 'very stressful’ questions from the BBC business journalist Karen Bowerman. When Karen asked him to talk about the result of the court case, he said that it was ‘a big surprise'. He then went on to say that more and more people were downloading music from the Internet because it was very easy. At that point Karen Bowerman appeared to realise something was wrong and very professionally ended the interview after only three questions. The video of Mr Goma was quickly uploaded onto YouTube, where it immediately went viral. Twenty minutes after his TV appearance, Mr Goma finally got to his real interview. Unfortunately, he did not get the job. (Adapted from THiNK)
Question 1. The word astonished in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to _______. 
A. calm 
B. delighted 
C. disappointed 
D. surprised
Question 2. As stated in paragraph 1, Guy Goma _______. 
A. decided to reject a job offer in the computing department 
B. was taken aback when asked to answer questions live on television  
C. didn’t go for a job interview at the BBC on time 
D. was an expert in settling legal cases involving music
Question 3. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
Mr Goma did his best to answer 'very stressful’ questions from the BBC business journalist Karen 
Bowerman.  
A. Mr. Goma made an effort to address the BBC business journalist Karen Bowerman’s difficult 
questions.  
B. Mr. Goma struggled to avoid answering the stressful questions posed by the BBC business journalist 
Karen Bowerman.  
C. Mr. Goma aimed to answer all of Karen Bowerman's questions, even though they were not particularly 
challenging.  
D. Mr. Goma was unable to handle the questions from BBC business journalist Karen Bowerman 
effectively. 

 

[Page 2]
Question 4. The word it in paragraph 3 refers to ________. 
A. the result of the court case 
B. downloading music from the Internet 
C. ending the interview  
D. a big surprise
Question 5. The phrase went viral in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ________. 
A. became popular 
 
B. received positive feedback 
C. failed to attract attention 
D. brought some profits
Question 6. Which of the following is NOT true according to the passage? 
A. Mr Goma became popular after his appearance on TV, which earned him a well-paid job.  
B. Guy Goma was mistaken for Guy Kewney who was supposed to be interviewed on TV.  
C. Karen Bowerman showed his professionalism when ending the interview with Mr Goma.  
D. Guy Goma expressed his surprise when asked about the result of the court case.
Question 7. In which paragraph does the writer mention a disappointing result? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
 D. Paragraph 4
Question 8. In which paragraph does the writer mention reaction from a BBC employee? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
 D. Paragraph 4
vocab_context_group | câu: 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 | trang 3-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage about Rachel Carson and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 9 to 16. Rachel Carson, an ecologist, a biologist, and a writer, was born in 1907 in Springdale, Pennsylvania. As a small girl, she was an avid reader and soon showed a keen interest in the natural world and writing. She had her first story about animals published in a magazine when she was in the fourth grade. She graduated from Pennsylvania College for Women (now Chatham University) in 1929, studied at the Woods Hole Marine Biological Laboratory, and received her MA in zoology from Johns Hopkins University in 1932. Initially, Rachel Carson worked for the US Bureau of Fisheries as a part-time science writer. Part of her job was to interest the public in marine and freshwater biology via radio programmes. She then spent the next few years serving as a marine scientist and editor for the US Fish and Wildlife Service. There, she became famous for her writings on environmental pollution and the natural history of the sea, in which she always concerned herself about the impact that humans made on the natural world. Climate change, rising sea-levels, melting Arctic glaciers, decreasing animal populations are part of her work. In her most influential book, Silent Spring (1962), Rachel Carson strongly disapproved of the widespread use of pesticides such as DDT. She warned the public about its negative long-term effects and called for new policies to protect humans and the environment. She then was criticised by the chemical industry, but never gave up. The book eventually prompted a change in national policy, leading to the enactment of a national ban of pesticides on agricultural farming. Additionally, it helped spark the environmental movement, resulting in the establishment of the United States Environmental Protection Agency. That's why she was called the mother of modern ecology. Rachel Carson died of breast cancer in 1964. However, her work continues to inspire new generations to protect all the living world. (Adapted from Friends Global) [Page 3]
Question 9. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
As a small girl, she was an avid reader and soon showed a keen interest in the natural world and 
writing.  
A. As a young girl, she loved reading and quickly developed a strong interest in nature and writing.  
B. As a young girl, she disliked reading but eventually showed interest in the natural world and writing.  
C. Even as a small girl, she was an avid reader, though her interest in the natural world took time to 
develop.  
D. As a young girl, her interest in reading and the natural world faded, but she began to enjoy writing.
Question 10. The word interest in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ________. 
A. persuade 
B. fascinate 
C. replace 
D. discourage
Question 11. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as part of Rachel Carson’s work? 
A. sea level rise 
 
B. decline in animal populations 
C. melting Arctic glaciers 
D. extreme weather conditions
Question 12. The word its in paragraph 3 refers to ________. 
A. the environment 
B. book 
C. DDT 
D. the chemistry group
Question 13. The word prompted in paragraph 3 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. prevented 
B. improved 
C. caused 
D. skipped
Question 14. Which of the following is NOT true about Rachel Carson according to the passage? 
A. She expressed her concern about the impact humans had on the natural world. 
B. She showed her strong opposition to the use of pesticides in Silent Spring. 
C. She remained determined despite facing criticism from the chemical industry. 
D. She herself published her first story about animals in a magazine when she was small.
Question 15. In which paragraph does the writer mention another name for Rachel Carson? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 16. In which paragraph does the writer mention a contrast relationship? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

Drive into the Future with the EcoMotion EV! Experience the thrill of innovation with the EcoMotion Electric Vehicle, designed to make your journeys (1) _________ and sustainable. (2) ________ a green lifestyle has never been easier with this eco-friendly car. (3) _________ traditional vehicles that pollute the environment, the EcoMotion EV produces zero emissions, helping you protect the planet. With a (4) _________ of features, including fast charging, a spacious interior, and advanced safety systems, this car is built for your comfort and convenience. When choosing your next vehicle, (5) _________ into account the long-term savings on fuel and maintenance. The EcoMotion EV not only saves you money but also supports a cleaner, greener world. Join the revolution in transportation today and drive an electric vehicle (6) __________ the way we travel. The future is electric, and it’s waiting for you!

Renewable energy is a type of energy replenished at a higher (7) _______ than it is used. It comes from natural sources like the sun, wind, and water. Unlike fossil fuels, renewable energy will not (8) _________. Using renewable energy is the key (9) ________ building a sustainable future. It helps reduce pollution and fight climate change. Solar panels and wind turbines are great examples of clean energy systems. (10) _________ energy sources, like hydropower and geothermal energy, also play an important role. We hope to encourage everyone (11) _________ renewable energy in their homes and businesses. Switching to these sources not only helps the planet but also saves money in the long run. Join the movement for a cleaner, greener energy future. Together, we can create a better tomorrow with (12) ___________!

A partner who offers (21) _________ with cleaning, cooking, or (22) ________ chores shows care and respect. It also helps reduce the burden on one person. Couples and families (23) _________ tasks evenly are more likely to feel satisfied and connected. A balanced approach to household work can strengthen relationships and make daily life easier. By working together, everyone can enjoy a clean and organised home without feeling (24) _________.

Read the following notice and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option 
that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 25 to 30. 
This Saturday, we will hold activities in response to Earth Hour, an event (25) ________ global action 
to protect the planet. Everyone is invited to join and make a difference!   
Our activities aim to (26) ________ awareness about energy conservation (27) ________ creating a 
sustainable future. There will be a tree-planting session, a workshop on eco-friendly practices, and a 
candlelight gathering to mark the event.   
(28) _________ participant will receive a small eco-friendly gift as a thank-you for their support. 
Together, we can (29) _________ a message of hope and responsibility to protect our Earth.   
Join us and (30) _________ part in this global movement for a greener tomorrow. Let’s turn off the 
lights and shine a light on climate action!

We must also encourage the protection of (5) ________ traditions, such as festivals and local customs. Preserving cultural heritage is not just about the past; it shapes our (6) ________ and builds a brighter future. Let’s protect it!

An Unforgettable Summer Vacation 
Susan Anna 
This summer, I had an unforgettable vacation with my best (19) 
_________, Mia. We planned a trip to the mountains and made sure 
to (20) _________ everything ready in advance. From packing 
clothes to booking a cabin, we wanted everything to go smoothly.   
When we arrived, the place (21) ________ to be even more 
beautiful than we expected. Imagine (22) ________ through green 
forests and discovering hidden waterfalls - it was magical!   
One funny moment happened when I slipped on a rock and got 
soaked. I felt so (23) _________, but Mia laughed and helped me 
up. It became one of our favourite memories.   
 
This trip was special because it reminded me (24) ________ the 
joy of adventure and friendship. I can’t wait to plan another trip like 
this!

Read the following article and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option 
that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 25 to 30. 
The Reality of Domestic Violence 
Domestic violence is a (25) _________ issue that affects people around the world. It can happen to 
anyone, (26) _________ age, gender, or background.   
Many victims feel trapped and unable to seek help. The abuse can include a wide (27) ________ of 
actions, such as physical harm, emotional manipulation, or financial control.   
Domestic violence doesn’t just harm the victim; it can have an impact (28) ________ their mental 
health, confidence, and even relationships with others. Children witnessing abuse are also at (29) 
_________ of long-term emotional scars.   
It’s important to (30) _________ and support those affected by domestic violence. Everyone deserves 
to live in safety and peace. If you or someone you know is in danger, reach out for help today. You are 
not alone.

Read the following article and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 13 to 18. What is Brainrot? The term "brainrot" is often used to refer (13) _________ the state of being overly obsessed with something, like a TV show, book, or game. It describes the feeling of (14) ________ much content about a specific interest, to the point where it takes over your thoughts. Recently, "brainrot" was chosen as the Oxford word of the year, (15) ________ its growing popularity. This (16) ________ questions about how our habits and hobbies influence our minds. Many people experience brainrot as a result of spending too much time on social media or binge- watching their favorite series. (17) __________ it can be fun, it’s important to balance interests with other parts of life. Understanding brainrot reminds us (18) _________ our passions without letting them eat our minds entirely. Balance is key!

It’s important to teach them how to (23) ________ these situations. Building self-confidence and learning to say no are key skills for handling peer pressure. Surrounding yourself with supportive friends can also (24) _________ a big difference. Remember, it’s okay to be yourself. Don’t let peer pressure lead to choices you’ll regret.

[Page 1] HẠ GỤC CÂU HỎI ĐỒNG NGHĨA – TRÁI NGHĨA THEO NGỮ CẢNH (BUỔI 1) Ôn luyện các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions. 1. Parent-teen relationships can be challenging but also rewarding. Parents often try to guide their teens, helping them make good choices. However, teens may feel misunderstood, which can lead to resentment. It’s important for both sides to communicate openly and listen to each other. Teens are learning to cope with new responsibilities and emotions, while parents are adjusting to their child’s growing independence. By showing patience and understanding, parents and teens can build trust and strengthen their bond.
Question 1. The word guide in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. prepare 
B. neglect 
C. focus 
D. remind
Question 2. The word resentment in the text is closest in meaning to  
. 
A. discussion 
B. hardship 
C. frustration 
D. competition
Question 3. The word strengthen in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. maintain 
B. oppose 
C. weaken 
D. disapprove
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
2. The rise of AI and robots is changing the world. These technologies play a key role in many industries, from healthcare to manufacturing. They can perform tasks precisely, often completing them faster than humans. For example, robots help assemble cars, while AI assists doctors in diagnosing diseases. However, this progress also raises concerns about job losses and ethical questions. Despite challenges, AI and robots have the potential to improve our lives by making work easier and solving complex problems. As we move forward, it’s important to use these tools responsibly and ensure they benefit everyone.
Question 4. The word precisely in the text is closest in meaning to  
. 
A. manually 
B. traditionally 
C. pleasantly 
D. accurately
Question 5. The word progress in the text can be best replaced by  
. 
A. advancement    B. awareness 
C. situation 
D. condition
Question 6. The word benefit in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. bore 
B. harm 
C. change 
D. force 
 

[Page 2]

3. 
Shanghai is making great efforts to become a green city. The city is planting more trees and creating parks 
to make use of green spaces effectively. To reduce pollution, Shanghai is expanding its public 
transportation system, including buses and subways. Many residents are also encouraged to adopt eco- 
friendly habits, like using reusable bags and biking instead of driving. Recycling programmes are 
growing, and buildings are using solar panels to save energy. These steps aim to make Shanghai cleaner 
and more sustainable. By working together, the city hopes to create a healthier environment for everyone 
and inspire other cities to follow its green example.
Question 7. The phrase make use of in the text can be best replaced by  
. 
A. utilise 
B. equip 
C. construct 
D. understand
Question 8. The word expanding in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. adjusting 
B. gathering 
C. restricting 
D. rebuilding
Question 9. The word steps in the text is closest in meaning to  
. 
A. abilities 
B. challenges 
C. measures 
D. requirements

4. Online friendships can be very fulfilling, offering emotional support and a sense of belonging. These connections often stem from shared interests, such as gaming, music, or learning new skills. For instance, someone might lend a hand by teaching you how to play a song or solve a problem. Over time, people value these relationships because they provide comfort and encouragement, even from a distance. While online friendships may not involve face-to-face interaction, they can still be meaningful and long-lasting. With trust and effort, they can become an important part of your life, enriching it in unique ways.

. 
A. honest 
B. satisfying 
C. mature 
D. available

B. defeating 
C. pushing 
D. ruining
vocab_context_group | câu: 14, 15, 16, 17 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
5. Hawaii is known for its magnificent beaches and clear blue waters, but plastic pollution is threatening its beauty. Tons of plastic waste wash up on the shores, poisoning marine life and harming the ecosystem. Many visitors and locals are taken aback when they see the amount of trash covering the sand. Efforts are being made to remedy the situation, such as beach clean-ups and banning single-use plastics. However, more action is needed to protect Hawaii’s natural wonders. By reducing plastic use and raising awareness, we can help keep Hawaii’s environment clean and safe for future generations.
Question 14. The word magnificent in the text is closest in meaning to  
. 
A. affordable 
B. ancient 
C. accessible 
D. impressive
Question 15. The word poisoning in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. benefiting 
B. polluting 
C. clarifying 
D. calming

D. worsen
vocab_context_group | câu: 18, 19, 20, 21 | trang - | rule_high
6. Global warming is causing glaciers to melt at an alarming rate. This puts many species at risk, as they lose their natural habitats. Animals like polar bears, which thrive in cold environments, are struggling to survive. Melting ice also makes coastal communities more vulnerable to rising sea levels and flooding. Additionally, warmer temperatures facilitate the spread of some diseases, such as malaria, as mosquitoes can survive in new areas. The effects of global warming are far-reaching, impacting both nature and human life. To protect our planet, we must take action to reduce carbon emissions and slow down climate change.
Question 18. The phrase at risk in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. protective 
B. unspoilt 
C. secure 
D. generous

. 
A. pause 
B. consider 
C. adapt 
D. suffer

. 
A. accept

C. encourage 
D. inspire

. 
A. serious 
B. limited 
C. negative 
D. extensive
vocab_context_group | câu: 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-6 | rule_high
7. Vocational education has many advantages. It helps students learn skills that are useful for jobs. Many programmes provide hands-on training, so students can practice real tasks instead of just reading about them. This makes learning more effective. Students also acquire important knowledge in their chosen field. They study topics that prepare them for work. Vocational education can lay the foundation for a successful career. It gives students the experience and confidence they need to find a good job. Many employers look favourably on workers who already have skills, so vocational training can lead to better job opportunities.
Question 22. The word hands-on in the text can be best replaced by  
. 
A. continuous 
B. practical 
C. updated 
D. initial
Question 23. The word acquire in the text mostly means  
. 
A. need 
B. fit 
C. gain 
D. meet
Question 24. The phrase lay the foundation for in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. prevent 
B. disturb 
C. bother 
D. control
Question 25. The phrase look favourably on in the text mostly means  
. 
A. regard 
B. prefer 
C. expect 
D. convince 
 

[Page 4]

8. 
Vietnamese New Year, or Tết, is a time for family and tradition. People customarily clean their houses 
before the holiday to remove bad luck from the past year. They also avoid breaking things, as this is 
believed to bring misfortune. Many superstitions are associated with Tết. For example, the first visitor 
to a home is thought to affect the family's fortune for the whole year. People often invite a prestigious 
person to be their first guest, hoping for success and happiness. Some believe that wearing black or white 
brings bad luck, so they choose bright colours instead. However, some argue that superstitions have no 
place in modern society. They believe success depends on hard work, not traditions or beliefs.
Question 26. The word customarily in the text can be best replaced by  
. 
A. unusually 
B. preferably 
C. traditionally 
D. responsibly
Question 27. The word misfortune in the text is opposite in meaning to  
. 
A. mystery 
B. luck 
C. progress 
D. need
Question 28. The word associated in the text is closest in meaning to  
. 
A. caused 
B. linked 
C. gained 
D. evolved
Question 29. The word prestigious in the text mostly means  
. 
A. miserable 
B. decisive 
C. ordinary 
D. respected
Question 30. The word place in the text can be best replaced by  
. 
A. demand 
B. challenge 
C. value 
D. habit

MP/CÁC DẠNG CÂU HỎI TRONG ĐỀ THI/ĐỒNG NGHĨA - TRÁI NGHĨA THEO NGỮ CẢNH/Hạ gục câu hỏi Đồng nghĩa - Trái nghĩa theo ngữ cảnh (Buổi 2).pdf

[Page 1] ĐỒNG NGHĨA – TRÁI NGHĨA THEO NGỮ CẢNH (BUỔI 2) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn luyện các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions. 1. Digital learning is becoming more popular. It allows students to employ technology to study anytime and anywhere. Many online courses provide videos, quizzes, and interactive lessons to help students learn better. One great benefit of digital learning is that it can suit various learning styles. Some people learn best by watching videos, while others prefer reading or practising exercises. Another advantage is that learning materials are easily accessible. Students can find many free resources online, making education more convenient for everyone.
Question 1. The word employ in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. gain 
B. hire 
C. cause 
D. use
Question 2. The word suit in the text can be replaced by _________. 
A. skip 
B. fit 
C. stop 
D. fix
Question 3. The word accessible in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. unusual 
B. unpleasant 
C. limited 
D. available
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
2. Robot classroom assistants are becoming more common in schools. They can help teachers by providing assistance with lessons and activities. This allows teachers to focus more on guiding students. Robots can also engage students in learning. They make lessons more interactive and fun, which helps students stay interested. Using robots in classrooms can be beneficial. They can answer simple questions, give feedback, and support students with different learning needs. This makes learning easier and more enjoyable for everyone.
Question 4. The word assistance in the text can be replaced by _________. 
A. focus 
B. influence 
C. support 
D. demand
Question 5. The word engage in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. match 
B. meet 
C. view 
D. bore
Question 6. The word beneficial in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. useful 
B. challenging 
C. personal 
D. active 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

[Page 2]

 

 
3. 
Ecotourism helps protect nature while allowing people to explore beautiful places. It teaches visitors to 
appreciate wildlife and natural landscapes. This makes people more aware of the need to protect the 
environment. One big benefit of ecotourism is that it can lessen the negative impact of travel. It encourages 
responsible behaviour, such as using fewer resources and reducing waste. Many ecotourism activities 
focus on reducing pollution. For example, eco-friendly hotels do not discharge harmful chemicals into 
rivers or oceans, helping to keep nature clean.
Question 7. The word appreciate in the text mostly means _________. 
A. realise 
B. follow 
C. value 
D. access
Question 8. The word lessen in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. minimise 
B. reject 
C. increase 
D. establish
Question 9. The word discharge in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. collect 
B. release 
C. reduce 
D. generate
vocab_context_group | câu: 10, 11, 12, 13 | trang - | rule_high
4. Gender inequality is still a big problem in many places. Some people face bias at work or school, making it harder for them to succeed. They may not have the same chances as others, even when they have the same skills. In some countries, there are restrictions on what women can do. They may not be allowed to study certain subjects or have important jobs. Society needs to help people get over these challenges. Everyone should have the right to pursue their dreams without unfair treatment.
Question 10. The word bias in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. disrespect 
B. opposition 
C. fairness 
D. duty
Question 11. The word restrictions in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. conflicts 
B. limitations 
C. requirements 
D. responsibilities
Question 12. The phrase get over in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. overcome 
B. understand 
C. assess 
D. investigate
Question 13. The word pursue in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. achieve 
B. disturb 
C. abandon 
D. adjust
vocab_context_group | câu: 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
5. Plastic pollution is a serious problem that harms the environment. When plastic waste is not disposed of properly, it can contaminate water, soil, and even the air. Many people get rid of plastic in rivers and oceans, causing great harm to marine life. This pollution is destructive because it takes a heavy toll on ecosystems and takes hundreds of years to break down. To protect nature, we must address this issue by reducing plastic use and recycling more. Small actions can help create a cleaner and healthier planet for the future.
Question 14. The word contaminate in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. recycle 
B. purify 
C. confuse 
D. manage
Question 15. The phrase get rid of in the text is closest in meaning to __________. 
A. replace 
B. neglect 
C. reuse 
D. dump
Question 16. The phrase takes a heavy toll on in the text mostly means ________. 
A. raises some doubt about 
B. causes serious damage to 
C. comes into conflict with 
D. creates a sense of duty to
Question 17. The word address in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. handle 
B. reveal 
C. ignore 
D. recognise 
 

[Page 3]

 

 
6. 
A new recycling initiative is helping communities reduce waste and protect the environment. Many people 
advocate for better recycling programmes to cut down on pollution. This initiative encourages a shift in 
daily habits, such as using reusable bags and separating waste properly. When creating new policies, 
leaders must take into account the needs of both people and the environment. Without action, plastic and 
other waste can cause severe damage to nature. Recycling is a simple but powerful way to make a positive 
change. By working together, we can create a cleaner and healthier future.
Question 18. The word advocate in the text can be best replaced by ________. 
A. oppose strongly 
B. ignore completely 
C. question openly 
D. support publicly
Question 19. The word shift in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. need 
B. change 
C. chance 
D. right
Question 20. The phrase take into account in the text can be best replaced by ________. 
A. accept 
B. supervise 
C. consider 
D. realise
Question 21. The word severe in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. balanced 
B. minimal 
C. harmful 
D. natural
arrangement_group | câu: 22, 23, 24, 25 | trang - | rule_high
7. A smart city uses technology to improve daily life. Many systems are in place to make transportation, energy, and public services more efficient. With smart traffic lights and digital payments, everything runs smoothly, reducing delays and inconvenience. Good internet and strong infrastructure make communication more reliable for businesses and residents. To keep up with modern needs, the city must upgrade its technology regularly. A smart city creates a better environment by saving energy, reducing waste, and improving safety. With careful planning, people can enjoy a more comfortable and sustainable life.
Question 22. The phrase in place in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. being operated 
B. being revised 
C. being arranged 
D. being accepted
Question 23. The word smoothly in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. conveniently 
B. ineffectively 
C. illegally 
D. irresponsibly
Question 24. The word reliable in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. simple 
B. independent 
C. common 
D. challenging
Question 25. The word upgrade in the text can be best replaced by __________. 
A. maintain 
B. promote 
C. improve 
D. encourage
vocab_context_group | câu: 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-6 | rule_high
8. Nick had always been confident, but one day, he faced body shaming at school. Some classmates made objectionable comments about his weight, making him feel uncomfortable. It was an unusual experience for him because he had never been judged for his appearance before. Their words hurt, and he struggled to figure out why people cared so much about his looks. The situation did not blow over quickly, and he kept thinking about their remarks. However, the incident prompted him to focus on self-acceptance. He realised that his worth was not based on his body. With support from friends and family, he regained his confidence. Nick learned that negative comments say more about the people who make them than about the person they target. He decided to stand up for himself and others facing similar situations. [Page 4]
Question 26. The word objectionable in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. regular 
B. impolite 
C. insincere 
D. pleasant
Question 27. The word unusual in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. shared 
B. normal 
C. strange 
D. extreme
Question 28. The phrase blow over in the text mostly means _________. 
A. impact 
B. disappear 
C. continue 
D. spread
Question 29. The word prompted in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. inspired 
B. exposed 
C. discouraged 
D. confused
Question 30. The phrase stand up for in the text mostly means _________. 
A. treat 
B. defend 
C. handle 
D. manage

[Page 1] ĐỒNG NGHĨA – TRÁI NGHĨA THEO NGỮ CẢNH (BUỔI 3) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn luyện các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions. 1. Parents carefully determine a name for their child. They think about meanings, family traditions, and sometimes even lucky numbers. A name is a means to show love and hope for the child’s future. Some parents choose names from history or books, while others create unique ones. No matter the choice, parents treasure the name they give, believing it will bring happiness and success to their child.
Question 1. The word determine in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. assess 
B. reject 
C. decide 
D. require
Question 2. The word means in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. purpose 
B. habit 
C. duty 
D. way
Question 3. The word treasure in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. disregard 
B. support 
C. remind 
D. prevent
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6 | trang - | rule_high
2. The kind of music people enjoy can reveal their personality. Some love calm and slow songs, while others prefer fast and exciting beats. A curious person might explore different types of music to learn about new cultures and sounds. People who are sociable often like music they can dance or sing along to with friends. No matter what style someone enjoys, music is a special part of life that brings joy and meaning.
Question 4. The word reveal in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. share 
B. hide 
C. view 
D. face
Question 5. The word curious in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. enthusiastic 
B. immature 
C. indifferent 
D. irresponsible
Question 6. The word sociable in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. ashamed 
B. confused 
C. outgoing 
D. delighted
vocab_context_group | câu: 7, 8, 9 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
3. Young people are full of energy and dreams. They want to stand on their own feet and make their own choices. Many are bold, ready to take risks and try new things. Life can be tough, but they come up with creative ideas to solve problems. They work hard to build a better future. With passion and determination, they inspire others. Young people are the hope of tomorrow, always learning and growing. Their courage and fresh perspectives help shape the world.
Question 7. The phrase stand on their own feet in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. become reliable 
B. argue with others 
C. give their voice 
D. depend on others
Question 8. The word bold in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. fearful 
B. anxious 
C. loyal 
D. brave 
 
 

[Page 2]
Question 9. The word inspire in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. motivate 
B. accept 
C. remind 
D. calm
vocab_context_group | câu: 10, 11, 12, 13 | trang - | rule_high
4. Self-driving cars may become mainstream in the future. Many companies are working to improve this technology. These cars can enable people to travel safely without driving. They use sensors and AI to avoid accidents and follow traffic rules. It will take time to attain full safety and trust, but experts believe it is possible. The idea of cars driving themselves is extraordinary and could change the way people travel. In the future, self-driving cars might make roads safer and life easier for everyone.
Question 10. The word mainstream in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. traditional 
B. existent 
C. normal 
D. convenient
Question 11. The word enable in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. discourage 
B. allow 
C. confuse 
D. disappoint
Question 12. The word attain in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. follow 
B. achieve 
C. inform 
D. reduce
Question 13. The word extraordinary in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. realistic 
B. unusual 
C. promising 
D. unsurprising
vocab_context_group | câu: 14, 15, 16, 17 | trang - | rule_high
5. Taking a gap year can appeal to many young people. It is a chance to explore the world. During this time, they can acquire skills that are not taught in school. Some students use the year to work, while others travel. For those with wealthy parents, the trip might be subsidised to make it easier. A gap year can enrich their lives by exposing them to different cultures and ideas. It helps them grow and understand themselves better. This break can also prepare them for future challenges, making it a valuable choice.
Question 14. The phrase appeal to in the text can be best replaced by __________. 
A. prevent 
B. evaluate 
C. attract 
D. surprise
Question 15. The word subsidised in the text mostly means _________. 
A. applied for 
B. paid for 
C. made for 
D. used for
Question 16. The word enrich in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. normalise 
B. prepare 
C. assign 
D. disrupt
Question 17. The word valuable in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. useless 
B. endless 
C. lifeless 
D. careless
vocab_context_group | câu: 18, 19, 20, 21 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
6. Family life is built on trust and love. When everyone works together, it helps create harmony at home. Small acts of kindness, like helping with chores, can do the trick to keep everyone happy. Families forge strong bonds by spending time together and sharing experiences. These moments make relationships deeper and more meaningful. Even during tough times, supporting each other strengthens the family. A happy home is where everyone feels valued and understood. By showing care and respect, families can overcome challenges and enjoy a peaceful life together. Family is the foundation of our lives.
Question 18. The word harmony in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. influence 
B. confusion 
C. surprise 
D. conflict
Question 19. The phrase do the trick in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. serve 
B. fight 
C. turn 
D. fail 
 
 

[Page 3]
Question 20. The word forge in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. upset 
B. interrupt 
C. break 
D. change
Question 21. The word valued in the text can be best replaced by __________. 
A. respected 
B. recognised 
C. approached 
D. attended
vocab_context_group | câu: 22, 23, 24, 25 | trang - | rule_high
7. A cashless society is becoming a reality in many places. Digital payments are fast and secure, making life easier for many people. However, some question whether this change is good for everyone. They object to losing the option of using physical money. This shift is like a revolution in how we handle transactions. While it brings convenience, it also raises concerns about privacy and access. Not everyone has the tools to join this new system. A cashless world has its benefits, but it’s important to ensure no one is left behind in the process.
Question 22. The word secure in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. clear 
B. safe 
C. full 
D. sharp
Question 23. The word question in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. doubt 
B. share 
C. mind 
D. boost
Question 24. The word revolution in the text mostly means __________. 
A. a perfect example  
B. an excellent model 
C. an important change 
D. a new way of life
Question 25. The phrase left behind in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. bothered 
B. employed 
C. equipped 
D. supported
vocab_context_group | câu: 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-6 | rule_high
8. Parent-teen relationships can be challenging. Teens want independence, while parents worry about their choices. This sometimes leads to clashes over rules, school, or friendships. When parents are too strict, teens may feel resentment and refuse to listen. However, too much freedom can have adverse effects, leading to bad decisions. Instead of placing blame, both sides should try to understand each other. Good communication helps parents become a pillar of strength for their children. With patience and respect, families can build strong relationships and create a supportive home where everyone feels valued.
Question 26. The word clashes in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. worries 
B. benefits 
C. mysteries 
D. arguments
Question 27. The word resentment in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. confidence 
B. pleasure 
C. respect 
D. belief
Question 28. The word adverse in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. beneficial 
B. minimal 
C. negative 
D. temporary
Question 29. The word blame in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. responsibility 
B. demand 
C. compliment 
D. communication
Question 30. The phrase a pillar of strength in the text mostly means __________. 
A. a means of communication 
B. a source of support 
C. a source of inspiration 
D. a way of thinking

MP/CÁC DẠNG CÂU HỎI TRONG ĐỀ THI/ĐỒNG NGHĨA - TRÁI NGHĨA THEO NGỮ CẢNH/Hạ gục câu hỏi Đồng nghĩa - Trái nghĩa theo ngữ cảnh (Buổi 4).pdf

[Page 1] ĐỒNG NGHĨA – TRÁI NGHĨA THEO NGỮ CẢNH (BUỔI 4) Dành riêng cho khóa học: Ôn luyện các dạng câu hỏi trong đề thi Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the following questions. 1. Many students regard taking a gap year as a great way to gain experience before university. They can travel, work, or learn new skills. However, some parents worry that their children will lose focus on their studies. Some students try to get round their parents by showing the benefits of a gap year, such as learning independence and responsibility. Others may feel unsure because financial problems or fear of falling behind may deter them from taking a break. In the end, choosing a gap year depends on personal goals and careful planning.
Question 1. The word regard in the text is closest in meaning to __________. 
A. promise 
B. involve 
C. consider 
D. advise
Question 2. The phrase get round in the text mostly means __________. 
A. permit 
B. persuade 
C. offer 
D. prepare
Question 3. The word deter in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. disallow 
B. attract 
C. protect 
D. encourage
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
2. Printed media and digital media both have advantages. Many people prefer digital media because it provides instant access to news and information. It is fast, convenient, and easy to update. However, some online sources can be misleading, spreading false information. Printed media, like newspapers and magazines, is more reliable but not as quick. Some people still enjoy reading printed materials once in a while because they like the feel of paper and the lack of distractions. Both types of media are important, and choosing between them depends on personal preference.
Question 4. The word instant in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. independent 
B. immediate 
C. affordable 
D. equal
Question 5. The word misleading in the text is opposite in meaning to _________. 
A. credible 
B. loyal 
C. friendly 
D. enthusiastic
Question 6. The phrase once in a while in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. regularly  
B. attentively  
C. occasionally  
D. selectively 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

[Page 2]

 

 
3. 
There is an ongoing debate about what should be compulsory in the school curriculum. Some believe that 
subjects like maths and science are the most important, while others think that arts and life skills should 
also be included. A diverse curriculum helps students learn different skills and prepares them for the 
future. Schools should not only focus on knowledge but also nurture creativity, critical thinking, and 
social skills. Finding the right balance is important to ensure that students develop into well-rounded 
individuals who are ready for the challenges of life.
Question 7. The word diverse in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. standard 
B. available 
C. uniform 
D. accidental
Question 8. The word nurture in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. impress 
B. recognise 
C. establish 
D. foster
Question 9. The word well-rounded in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. experienced 
B. incompetent 
C. various 
D. impatient
vocab_context_group | câu: 10, 11, 12, 13 | trang - | rule_high
4. Bangkok, the capital of Thailand, is a popular tourist destination. Visitors are often struck by its mix of modern buildings and ancient temples. The Grand Palace is one of the city’s most famous landmarks, attracting thousands of tourists each year. Bangkok has been featured in a few films because of its beautiful scenery and lively streets. The city is bustling with markets, street vendors, and cultural events. Tourists can savour traditional dishes like Pad Thai and Tom Yum soup at local restaurants. With its rich culture and exciting atmosphere, Bangkok is a great place to explore.
Question 10. The word struck in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. attacked 
B. impressed 
C. delighted 
D. annoyed
Question 11. The phrase been featured in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. collected 
B. created 
C. appeared 
D. succeeded
Question 12. The word bustling in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. crowded 
B. quiet 
C. ancient 
D. unknown
Question 13. The word savour in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. enjoy 
B. prepare 
C. witness 
D. imagine
vocab_context_group | câu: 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
5. Ecotourism is a great alternative to conventional tourism for people who care about nature. It allows travellers to explore beautiful places while protecting the environment. Many eco-friendly tours are designed to meet the needs of visitors without harming local wildlife or culture. Unlike mass tourism, which can spoil natural areas, ecotourism focuses on sustainability. The impact on nature is negligible because visitors follow strict rules to reduce waste and pollution. By choosing ecotourism, people can enjoy unique experiences while helping to preserve the planet for future generations.
Question 14. The word conventional in the text mostly means __________. 
A. modern 
B. strange 
C. frightening 
D. traditional
Question 15. The word meet in the text can be best replaced by __________. 
A. satisfy 
B. generate 
C. eliminate 
D. establish
Question 16. The word spoil in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. introduce 
B. preserve 
C. damage 
D. inform
Question 17. The word negligible in the text is opposite in meaning to ________. 
A. minimal 
B. harmful 
C. beneficial 
D. significant 
 

[Page 3]

 

 
6. 
The teacherless learning model is akin to self-study, where students learn through technology and online 
resources instead of traditional classrooms. This approach can make way for more flexible and 
personalised education. With access to digital tools, students feel empowered to take control of their own 
learning. However, some argue that a lack of direct guidance from teachers may be challenging. To make 
up for this, interactive platforms and AI tutors can provide support. While this model is still developing, 
it offers new opportunities for students to learn at their own pace.
Question 18. The word akin in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. common 
B. pleasant 
C. similar 
D. random
Question 19. The phrase make way for in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. consider 
B. offer 
C. enhance 
D. ensure
Question 20. The word empowered in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. discouraged 
B. respected 
C. supported 
D. reminded
Question 21. The phrase make up for in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. acknowledge 
B. prepare 
C. prevent 
D. disclose
vocab_context_group | câu: 22, 23, 24, 25 | trang - | rule_high
7. Cambridge University is planning to axe traditional written exams and switch to digital assessments. This decision aims to bring an end to handwriting issues and make exams more efficient. However, some worry that students' writing skills may deteriorate if they no longer practice handwriting. Others believe that using computers will help students complete exams faster and more comfortably. The university still wants to preserve high academic standards while adapting to modern technology. This change has sparked debate about the best way to test students' knowledge in the future.
Question 22. The word axe in the text is closest in meaning to _________. 
A. transform 
B. abolish 
C. discourage 
D. revise
Question 23. The phrase bring an end to in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. prevent 
B. discontinue 
C. control 
D. encourage
Question 24. The word deteriorate in the text is opposite in meaning to __________. 
A. remain unchanged  
B. become more important 
C. get worse and worse 
D. turn into a serious issue
Question 25. The word sparked in the text can be best replaced by __________. 
A. closed 
B. prompted 
C. continued 
D. won
vocab_context_group | câu: 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-7 | rule_high
8. The Covid-19 pandemic led to a debate about online education. Some people saw it as an innovative way to continue learning, while others found it challenging. Many deem online classes effective because they allow students to study from home. However, online learning invariably creates difficulties, such as a lack of interaction and technical issues. Teachers had to keep watch on students’ progress through digital tools, which was not always easy. The pandemic also highlighted the divide between students with good internet access and those without. While online education has benefits, it is not a perfect solution for everyone.
Question 26. The word innovative in the text is closest in meaning to __________. 
A. rewarding 
B. creative 
C. sustainable 
D. risky
Question 27. The word deem in the text can be best replaced by _________. 
A. select 
B. affect 
C. inspire 
D. consider  
 

[Page 4]

D. doubtfully

D. violate

C. shift
