[Page 1] CHUYÊN ĐỀ: CÂU HỎI ĐUÔI Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh trọn đời Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương 1. The sky today is clear, _______? A. does it B. is it C. doesn’t it D. isn't it 2. Both your sons can swim well, _______? A. can't they B. are they C. can they D. is he 3. We don't have enough time to wait for the bus, _______? A. did we B. don’t we C. do we D. aren’t we 4. We ought to leave as soon as possible, _______? A. shouldn't we B. ought we C. ought we not D. had we 5. Your friend Jane will come to the party, _______? A. will he B. won't she C. won’t it D. is she 6. Peter won the cooking competition last month, _______? A. was he B. didn't he C. hasn’t he D. isn’t he 7. Let’s go to the beach together tomorrow, _______? A. will we B. shall we C. aren’t we D. don’t we 8. Your parents had been living there for 10 years then, _______? A. aren’t they B. were they C. hadn't they D. weren’t they 9. The babies weren't sleeping at that time, _______? A. are they B. did they C. aren’t they D. were they 10. We might buy some groceries on our way to the station, _______? A. don’t we B. may we C. might not we D. mightn't we 11. All attendees are required to be on time, _______? A. are they B. aren’t they C did they D. weren’t they 12. Freddy won't have any trouble finding the address, _______? A. does he B. shall he C. will he D. is he 13. We should reserve a table, ______? A. shouldn’t we B. don’t we C. aren’t we D. haven’t we 14. I am not the only Asian here, _______? A. do I B. are I C. aren’t I D. am I 15. Your siblings don't really enjoy spicy food, _______? A. did they B. will they C. are they D. do they 16. You haven't finished your homework yet, _______? A. had you B. do you C. have you D. did you 17. You had finished your work before yesterday, _______? A. hadn't you B. haven’t you C. didn’t you D. do you cho người khác! [Page 2] 18. You have understood the instructions we provided, _______? A. don’t you B. didn’t you C. hadn’t you D. haven't you 19. They went on a road trip last summer vacation, _______? A. don’t they B. didn’t they C. aren’t they D. haven’t they 20. She is practising English in her room, _______? A. isn't she B. doesn’t she C. won’t she D. didn’t she 21. It is not easy for under-5-year-old kids to solve this puzzle, _______? ...

A thousand is ten times more than…......hundred. 4. ….....eulogy is usually a speech about someone who has died. 5. …...unknown person stole a TV from my mother’s home. 6. C. A – the  
 
D. Ø – the
Question 7. He is _____ fastest player on the volleyball team. 
A. a 
 
 
B. an  
 
C. the  
 
D. Ø (no article)
Question 8. The bakery is on _____ Rose Lane, next to the flower shop. 
A. a 
 
 
B. an  
 
C. the  
 
D. Ø (no article)
Question 9. Nick is not a master at playing _____ drums. 
A. a 
 
 
B. an  
 
C. the  
 
D. Ø (no article)
Question 10. Do you believe that there is _____ life on Mars? 
A. a 
 
 
B. an  
 
C. the  
 
D. Ø (no article)
Question 11. She is doing _____ master's degree at _____ University of Sydney. 
A. the – the 
 
B. a – the  
 
C. a – Ø  
 
D. a – a
Question 12. _____ Lake Baikal is _____ deepest freshwater lake in the world. 
A. The – a  
 
B. Ø – the  
 
C. Ø – a  
 
D. Ø – the

[Page 1] CHUYÊN ĐỀ : TỪ LOẠI Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Xác định các danh từ được gạch chân sau là danh từ số ít, số nhiều hay không đếm được: Life is full of choices. Should you buy the latest smartphone, or put your money in a savings account instead? Do you really need that phone, or is it a luxury you can live without? These are questions that are asked every day, and for many people, the answer is to reduce the amount of money they spend in order to increase their happiness. (p. 76, Explore New Worlds 11) 1. Các danh từ ‘money’, ‘happiness’ là danh từ . A. Số ít B. Số nhiều C. Không đếm được 2. Các danh từ ‘choices’, ‘questions’ là danh từ . A. Số ít B. Số nhiều C. Không đếm được 3. Các danh từ ‘phone’, ‘luxury’ là danh từ . A. Số ít B. Số nhiều C. Không đếm được Dựa vào hậu tố, chọn đáp án chứa từ loại là danh từ:

D. dependently

C. careless 
D. careful

[Page 1] CHUYÊN ĐỀ : TỪ LOẠI Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Xác định từ loại nào là tính từ trong các từ gạch chân ở mỗi câu trong đoạn văn sau: Setting achievable goals is also important for lifelong learning. Goals give direction and motivation to your learning journey. First, identify areas of interest and choose specific skills or knowledge that you want to develop. Second, break your goals down into smaller, more manageable tasks and track your progress. Finally, celebrate every accomplishment along the way to stay motivated. (p. 96, Bright 12, adjusted) 1. Từ nào là tính từ? A. goals B. important C. learning 2. Từ nào là tính từ? A. specific B. knowledge C. develop 3. Từ nào là tính từ? A. Finally B. every C. accomplishment Dựa vào hậu tố, chọn đáp án chứa từ loại là tính từ:

B. empowerment 
C. powerful   
D. empower

C. reliance 
 
D. reliable

B. care 
 
 
C. carefulness 
 
D. carelessness

B. considerations  
C. considerate  
D. consideration

D. nationality

B. bored

B. disappointed
Question 8. A. interesting

Many still argue that if we had not sacrificed the environment, we (2) _______ the modern comforts we enjoy today. However, development together with conservation is absolutely possible. If we (3) _______ action today, the Earth will soon become an unsafe place for future generations.
Question 1. A. is 
B. had been 
C. would be  
D. will be
Question 2. A. could have 
B. will not have 
C. would have had 
D. would not have
Question 3. A. do not take  
B. would have taken 
C. can take 
D. had taken

If you remain friends until after graduation, you (6) _______ able to date again once you are old enough to make your own decisions.

B. may express  
C. would express  
D. should express
Question 5. A. didn’t forbid 
B. forbid  
C. forbade 
D. have forbidden
Question 6. A. are 
B. would have been 
C. will be

3. Family, like any other institution, needs rules to function well. If there are clear family rules, everything (7) _______ in order. For example, if parents set the rule from the start that nobody is allowed to come home after 10 p.m., their children (8) _______ what time they should return. On the contrary, if no rules (9) _______ at all, each member may do things in their own way, and disagreements are more likely to happen.
Question 7. A. had stayed  
B. stays  
C. would stay

Chọn đáp án đúng để hoàn thiện đoạn văn: 
1. Carbon dioxide is the gas that traps heat. (10) _______, the Earth overheats. However, CO₂ is not 
completely harmful. If it did not exist, (11) _______, and humans would have no oxygen to breathe. In 
the past, (12) _______, they would not have released such a huge amount of greenhouse gases into the 
air. Therefore, we must understand the double role of CO₂: it’s essential for life, but too much of it is also 
dangerous.
Question 10. 
A. If heat doesn’t escape from the atmosphere 
B. If the atmosphere did not let the heat go away 
C. When the heat was escaping outside 
D. Supposing heat didn’t manage to leave the atmosphere
Question 11.  
A. trees didn’t have the ability to make food 
B. plants were unable to do photosynthesis 
C. trees cannot perform the process of photosynthesis 
D. trees would not be able to carry out photosynthesis
Question 12. 
A. if humans behave responsibly 
B. if people were taking care of the environment 
C. if people had been more responsible 
D. people do not show enough responsibility
cloze_group | câu: 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
2. People often talk a lot about the negative sides of peer pressure. However, if there were no such pressure, (13) _______. This is because pressure leads to competition. And if people compete, (14) _______. For instance, if a person always feels satisfied with his job regardless of the fact that his colleagues earn higher salaries, (15) _______. On the contrary, if he realises the difference, he may try to work harder, gain more skills, and eventually get promoted.
Question 13. 
A. people had lost their motivation to work harder 
B. people would lose part of their motivation to strive harder 
C. we may not have any motivation for striving harder 
D. people would not lose their motivation to try harder
Question 14. 
A. progress was made 
B. improvement will have occurred 
C. development takes place 
D. advancement is happening
Question 15. 
A. he will not make any effort to improve himself 
B. he would make efforts to better himself 
C. he wouldn’t making any effort for self-improvement 
D. that man would not have made any effort to improve 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
3. In the long run, you should stay with those whom you really want to be with. An unpopular group 
should not be a reason for you to leave. If you are good enough, (16) _______. On the other hand, think 
about (17) _______ if you decided not to hang out with your current people and chose other suitable ones 
instead. Perhaps you might just lose some expensive and forgettable trips. Life is full of choices, so make 
the right one. Do not let yourself regret later by saying, “If I had chosen differently that day, (18) 
________.”
Question 16.  
A. you would have turned the group more popular 
B. you would be able to make that group friendly 
C. you can even make that group more popular yourself 
D. it was possible for you to create a more popular group
Question 17. 
A. how your life would be 
B. what your life will become 
C. the way your life changes 
D. how your life was different
Question 18. 
A. I can be much happier today 
B. I would be much happier now 
C. my life will be happier soon 
D. I was much happier at the moment 
 
Chọn đáp án chứa câu diễn đạt lại tốt nhất với các câu được gạch chân trong đoạn: 
1. English is widely regarded as the language of business. Studying in the UK helps you learn the language 
quickly, through your studies, friends and everyday life. If you need any additional support, there are 
lots of English language classes across the UK. 
(Adapted from English Workbook 11)
Question 19. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. English language classes in the UK are available, but only for advanced learners. 
B. There are many English courses across the UK that can give you extra support. 
C. The UK offers English classes helping you, though mainly concentrated in large cities. 
D. Additional help is limited to a few English programs in certain regions of the UK.
reading_group | câu: 20, 21 | trang 4-4 | rule_low
2. As a country industrialises, the number of people living in urban areas tends to increase. The UK and many other MEDCs urbanised during the 18th and 19th centuries. People migrated from rural areas to urban areas where there was employment in the new factories. (Adapted from English Workbook 12)
Question 20. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. Urban areas will expand if a country goes through industrialisation. 
B. If a country industrialises, all rural residents move to urban areas. 
C. If industrialisation does not happen, cities will still become more crowded. 
D. If a country industrialises, more people are likely to live in cities.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
3. Coral reefs are often called the “rainforests of the sea” because they provide shelter and food for 
thousands of marine species. If coral reefs did not exist, many species of fish would have no place to 
reproduce. However, coral reefs are now under serious threat from pollution, overfishing, and global 
warming. This would break the food chain and damage the entire ocean ecosystem.
Question 21. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. Without coral reefs, numerous kinds of fish would lose their breeding grounds.  
B. Many fish species could still reproduce elsewhere if coral reefs did not exist. 
C. If coral reefs disappeared, fish populations would immediately vanish from the oceans. 
D. Fish would still have plenty of breeding places even if coral reefs did not exist.
reading_group | câu: 22 | trang - | rule_low
4. Some research suggests that teenagers need more sleep than adults; however, it's often the case that they get less than they need. With homework and social obligations, you can be staying up late but still have to get up early to get to classes, work or other activities. To function properly, your body and mind need a minimum of eight hours of sleep each night. (Adapted from English Workbook 11)
Question 22. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. If you sleep less than eight hours a night, your body and mind will still function well. 
B. Unless you rest for more than eight hours during the day, you cannot stay healthy. 
C. Your body and mind will work properly if you get at least eight hours of sleep every night.  
D. If you don’t sleep at all every night, your body and mind will still be fine.
reading_group | câu: 23 | trang - | rule_low
5. Using plants to cover walls and rooftops can also keep cities cooler in the summer. Buildings and roads absorb the sun's heat and hold it, causing a building or neighbourhood to stay warmer longer. Plants, on the other hand, provide an enormous amount of shade. There is evidence that growing a roof or wall garden can lower a building's energy costs. (Adapted from English Workbook 12)
Question 23. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. If vegetation is placed inside buildings, urban areas will stay cooler in the summer months. 
B. If plants are grown on walls and rooftops, cities can have lower temperatures in summer. 
C. If rooftops have no greenery, the whole city will still feel cooler during hot weather. 
D. If plants exist only in city parks, urban areas will remain cooler throughout the summer.
vocab_context_group | câu: 24, 25 | trang 5-8 | rule_high
6. For decades, human activities have had a harmful impact on the atmosphere. Our emissions over many years have weakened the ozone layer’s ability to protect us from ultraviolet rays. This dangerous change has increased the risk of skin cancer, eye damage, and other health problems for people all over the world.
Question 24. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. The ozone layer would have been weakened if humans had not emitted so much for decades. 
B. If we had reduced our emissions over many years, the ozone layer would have protected us better from 
ultraviolet rays. 
C. The ozone layer would never have lost any of its protective ability if emissions had been lower. 
D. If people had avoided releasing gases for years, ultraviolet radiation would not have existed at all. 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
7. Social media algorithms have a powerful influence on the way people use online platforms. The more 
time we spend scrolling, the more content the algorithm shows us that matches our interests. As a 
result, users often fall into a cycle where they cannot stop browsing because every post seems relevant 
and attractive.
Question 25. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. If we shorten our scrolling time, the algorithm tends to supply even more content aligned with what 
we enjoy. 
B. If we spend more time scrolling, the algorithm will present random content unrelated to our preferences. 
C. If we reduce the time spent scrolling, the algorithm will still continue giving us more of the things we 
like. 
D. If we keep scrolling longer, the algorithm will generate a greater amount of content tailored to our 
interests.  

 

[Page 6]

MP/NGỮ PHÁP ỨNG DỤNG/CHUYÊN ĐỀ 11 - ĐỘNG TỪ KHUYẾT THIẾU, CÂU DỰ ĐOÁN TRONG QUÁ KHỨ

files=2
11 Thi Online Động từ khuyết thiếu, câu dự đoán trong quá khứ.pdf segments=10 | question_blocks=30 | status=ok
MP/NGỮ PHÁP ỨNG DỤNG/CHUYÊN ĐỀ 11 - ĐỘNG TỪ KHUYẾT THIẾU, CÂU DỰ ĐOÁN TRONG QUÁ KHỨ/11 Thi Online Động từ khuyết thiếu, câu dự đoán trong quá khứ.pdf
cloze_group | câu: 1, 2, 3 | trang 1-1 | rule_high
[Page 1] TRONG QUÁ KHỨ THI ONLINE Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. In the future, citizens (1) _______ wait for long in traffic jams, because public transportation will be faster and more efficient. There (2) _______ also be flying taxis or underground highways that make travelling even easier. With these improvements, daily life will be more convenient, and people (3) _______ have more free time to enjoy life.
Question 1. A. cannot 
B. must 
C. won’t have to  
D. shouldn’t
Question 2. A. shouldn’t 
B. may  
C. dare  
D. needn’t
Question 3. A. have to 
B. should 
C. couldn’t 
D. will
cloze_group | câu: 4, 5, 6 | trang - | rule_high
2. Each of us (4) _______ help protect marine life by changing our daily habits. For example, we (5) _______ throw plastic waste into rivers, beaches, or the ocean, because even a small bottle or bag can harm fish, turtles, or seabirds. Moreover, we (6) _______ reduce the use of single-use plastics, such as straws or plastic cups, and switch to reusable items. By making these small choices, every person can contribute to saving precious creatures.
Question 4. A. dare 
B. will 
C. would 
D. can
Question 5. A. could not 
B. must not  
C. should 
D. need
Question 6. A. might 
B. don’t have to 
C. had better  
D. cannot
cloze_group | câu: 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
3. When someone makes a mistake, we (7) _______ give them gentle advice instead of making them feel embarrassed. For example, if your friend is late, you can say, ‘You ought to leave earlier next time,’ rather than ‘You (8) ________ earlier.’ You can also show sympathy. For instance, ‘If I were you, I (9) _______ have set an alarm to make sure I arrive on time.’ Such words help your friend improve without feeling discouraged.
Question 7. A. may 
B. ought to  
C. could  
D. must not
Question 8. A. will leave 
B. should have left  
C. must not leave 
D. have to leave
Question 9. A. would  
B. must  
C. can 
D. dare  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 2]

 

 
4. Our school has just updated its regulations. First, from now on, all students (10) _______ wear the 
school uniform every day. Second, students (11) _______ bring food and drinks into the school, but they 
must throw their trash in the right place to keep the campus clean. Finally, mobile phones are not allowed 
inside the school. Therefore, students (12) _______ leave their phones at home or keep them turned off 
during school hours.
Question 10. A. can  
B. have to 
C. mustn’t  
D. would
Question 11. A. might  
B. should 
C. can  
D. couldn’t
Question 12. A. had better  
B. can  
C. dare 
D. shouldn’t
cloze_group | câu: 13, 14, 15, 16 | trang - | rule_high
5. When Lionel Messi was a child, he (13) _______ play football with amazing skills. He (14) _______ practise for hours every day, running, dribbling, and shooting until he improved his technique. Coaches realised that this little boy (15) _______ become a great player if he had the right training. Even though his health was a problem, he never gave up. Later, when he joined FC Barcelona, Messi showed that talent alone was not enough; he (16) _______ work extremely hard to overcome his difficulties and succeed.
Question 13. A. should 
B. must 
C. could 
D. can
Question 14. A. used to  
B. should  
C. had better 
D. needn’t
Question 15. A. couldn’t 
B. might  
C. didn’t have to 
D. dare
Question 16. A. can 
B. didn’t have to 
C. had to  
D. ought to
cloze_group | câu: 17, 18, 19, 20 | trang - | rule_high
6. In American culture, communication is direct and friendly. Unlike the British, who may use formal wishes like ‘(17) _______ you have a wonderful year,’ Americans often give short greetings such as ‘Have a great day!’ or ‘Good luck!’ They (18) _______ smile and look directly into your eyes, as this shows confidence and respect. In fact, you (19) _______ follow too many strict or traditional rules, since simplicity is highly valued. If someone ever feels offended, you (20) _______ something rude or too personal, as most Americans usually don’t get upset over simple, casual words.
Question 17. A. Will 
B. May  
C. Can 
D. Should
Question 18. A. ought to 
B. must 
C. could 
D. would rather
Question 19. A. needn’t  
B. have to 
C. may  
D. can
Question 20. A. must say 
B. might have said  
C. should have said 
D. may say
cloze_group | câu: 21, 22, 23, 24, 25 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
7. In the past, Tet was much simpler but very meaningful. Families (21) _______ prepare everything by hand, from making ‘bánh chưng’ to decorating the house. Children (22) _______ wear new clothes only on the first days of Tet, and they eagerly waited for ‘lì xì’ from adults. Nowadays, Tet has changed. People (23) _______ buy almost everything they need at supermarkets. Many young people (24) _______ travel during the holiday instead of staying at home. However, they (25) _______ still practise the traditional values of Tet and spend time with their families. In this way, Tet today combines both modern convenience and cultural heritage.
Question 21. A. used to  
B. didn’t have to 
C. might not 
D. should
Question 22. A. shouldn’t 
B. must 
C. will 
D. would
Question 23. A. couldn’t 
B. must not 
C. can  
D. ought to
Question 24. A. dare 
B. may  
C. might not 
D. shouldn’t
Question 25. A. must not 
B. don’t have to 
C. cannot  
D. should  
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
Read the following passage and choose the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the option that best 
paraphrases each of the underlined sentences. 
1. Many people prefer buying trendy and cheap clothes to purchasing high-quality items. This habit 
creates convenience and excitement, but it also causes serious problems. Fast fashion often leads to 
environmental pollution, waste of resources, and unfair labour practices.
standalone_mcq | câu: 26 | trang - | rule_high
Question 26. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. Numerous shoppers don’t have to select premium items over budget, stylish clothes. 
B. A lot of people should buy stylish, low-cost clothes instead of quality ones. 
C. Many would rather purchase affordable, fashionable clothes than buy quality ones.  
D. Plenty of consumers might not get inexpensive trendy clothes instead of quality ones.
reading_group | câu: 27 | trang - | rule_low
2. Singapore is a small country, but it has become one of the most developed nations in Asia. It is famous for modern buildings, excellent transport, and a very clean environment. The government enforces strict rules to protect public spaces. Buying chewing gum in this country is impossible because the law completely bans it.
Question 27. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence? 
A. Citizens cannot purchase chewing gum here since the law totally prohibits it. 
B. Shoppers should buy chewing gum here even though the law bans it. 
C. Consumers don’t have to avoid chewing gum because the law permits it. 
D. Individuals might buy chewing gum here, although the law makes it illegal.
... còn 2 segment trong file này

When you wake up feeling tired in the morning, you (1) _______ have had a restless night. Several factors affect your sleep. Now, I regret it. I (6) _______ have used my time better back then.

To prevent these possible consequences, both governments and people (9) _______ take action. One effective solution is raising public awareness of poaching problems. For example, schools (10) _______ educate students about the importance of wild animals and the dangers of poaching. (p. 97, Explore New Worlds 11)

4. In the future, we (11) _______ witness even more dramatic changes in education. With the integration 
of technology, students (12) _______ attend virtual classes and store materials digitally. They (13) 
_______ carry heavy notebooks anymore, since tablets allow them to write, read, and submit work online. 
However, this convenience also brings challenges we (14) _______ address. Constant notifications and 
online distractions, for example, reduce focus. Thus, schools (15) _______ also teach digital discipline 
alongside technical skills.

B. should 
C. must 
D. would

D. could

B. don’t have to

Chọn đáp án gần nghĩa nhất với các câu sau: 
1.  Modern technology has made distance interviews possible in the past decade. No longer is distance 
an obstacle to an interview participant. Nowadays, interviews can be conducted via telephone or with 
video calls. 
(p. 63, Friends Global 12) 
A. Without modern technology, distance interviews could have happened more easily. 
B. Modern technology might not prevent people from doing interviews far from each other. 
C. We have been able to interview remotely over the past ten years with modern technology.  
D. People could only attend interviews in person before technology improved recently. 
 
2. Home to 226 species of plants, U Minh Thuong National Park is the region with the richest biodiversity 
in the Mekong River Delta. It used to be one of the largest habitats for water birds in the area. But 
their home was badly damaged by forest fires in 2002. 
(p. 113, Global Success 11) 
 
A. It was once among the biggest places where water birds lived in the region. 
B. It is currently the only place where water birds can be found in the region. 
C. It might have been too small to support many water birds in the past. 
D. Water birds used to avoid this place because it lacked proper habitats. 
 
3. Gary Hall Jr. is a special person in regard to all of the obstacles he overcame on his path to glory. With 
his determination, he achieved great success. From his story, we can all learn an important life lesson: To 
be successful, you must try your best and believe in what you can do. 
(p. 16, Bright 12) 
A. Success comes naturally if you are confident without putting in much effort. 
B. It is recommended to work hard and believe in yourself to achieve success. 
C....

The truth is, (2) _______ their diet and lifestyle choices affect the results. For example, working out regularly is helpful, but consuming too many high-calorie snacks can cancel the benefits. For effective weight loss, people must pay attention not only to physical activity (3) _______ to nutrition.
Question 1. A. or  
B. yet  
C. and 
D. so

B. while 
C. neither 
D. both
Question 3. A. so that 
B. but also  
C. even though 
D. only if

(5) ______ you go to Japan, you should be careful when writing. It's taboo to use a red pen to write somebody's name (6) _______ it is thought to be very unlucky. (p. 68, iLearn Smart World 12)

B. until

D. yet

B. Since

He has been passionate about singing (8) _______ he was five years old and has achieved great success as a professional singer. However, he faced a lot of challenges during his early life. When Arnel was thirteen, his mother died, (9) _______ his family had a lot of financial problems. (p. 22, Bright 12)

B. and

B. when

Chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất để hoàn thành đoạn văn: 
1. Taylor Swift is an American singer and songwriter. (10) _______, she became interested in musical 
theatre and performed in a youth theatre. Later, she became more interested in country music, (11) 
_______. At age 14, her family moved to Nashville, where she became a songwriter of country songs. 
Her songs made headlines, (12) _______.  
(p. 13, English Discovery 12)
Question 10.  
A. When she was only 9 years old 
B. Since she was only 9 years old 
C. Because she was only 9 years old 
D. If she was only 9 years old
Question 11.  
A. although she avoided public appearances 
B. whereas she had interest in country songs 
C. because she stopped singing completely 
D. so she started performing at local events
Question 12.  
A. but they didn’t attract any attention 
B. if her music had stayed completely unknown 
C. and people noticed how talented she was 
D. because she never released anything public
cloze_group | câu: 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
2. (13) _______, we have damaged the planet more than ever before. (14) _______, we continue to exploit natural resources for short-term gain. Every day, (15) _______, the environment, wildlife, and ecosystems are quietly suffering. (16) _______, the planet will keep moving closer to irreversible destruction.
Question 13.  
A. Unless the Industrial Revolution never happened 
B. As the Industrial Revolution was a turning point in history 
C. Since the Industrial Revolution began  
D. In case the Industrial Revolution changed everything instantly
Question 14.  
A. Even though its negative effects are widely known 
B. Because its benefits were still being discovered 
C. If people pay more attention to the environmental warnings 
D. So industries are encouraged to expand further
Question 15.  
A. while we are chasing convenience and profit 
B. because we want to save the planet together 
C. as long as we take care of every single species 
D. when we stop using natural resources
Question 16.  
A. As soon as we’ve done everything necessary 
B. Until we change our mindset and take real action  
C. Provided that nature can recover without help 
D. Because people are starting to care more 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
3. A single offensive remark or even a negative comment can be considered cyberbullying. (17) _______, 
step away immediately. You can block or report the person (18) _______ the toxic interaction. (19) 
_______, stay offline for a while to protect your mental health. Treat every online interaction (20) 
_______ and speaking directly to your face. Take this seriously.
Question 17.  
A. Provided that you ignore the early signs 
B. Even though you recognize the signs 
C. Unless you notice the signs immediately 
D. As soon as you notice the signs
Question 18.  
A. whereas you might still feel upset by  
B. in order that you no longer stay exposed to 
C. so you continue getting involved in 
D. because you may already be protected from
Question 19.  
A. In case it affects your mood or focus 
B. So that it boosts your energy levels 
C. Unless it improves your concentration 
D. Because it makes you feel more productive
Question 20.  
A. as if the person were standing right in front of you 
B. because the person is just texting anonymously 
C. before the person is far away and irrelevant to you 
D. because the person actually appears polite online

[Page 1] BÀI TẬP ỨNG DỤNG (BUỔI 2) Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Chọn đáp án đúng để hoàn thành đoạn văn: 1. (1) _______ I started recycling, I made the mistake of putting some plastic takeaway containers with some leftover food into the recycling bin. The next day, the whole bin was contaminated, (2) _______ all the recyclables were taken to the landfill instead. So now I always rinse out containers (3) _______ I recycle them. (p. 36, Global Success 12)
Question 1. A. So  
B. Since 
C. When  
D. Because
Question 2. A. nor 
B. for 
C. yet 
D. so
Question 3. A. before  
B. after 
C. though 
D. until

2. Many college students work part-time, (4) _______ they want to gain experience and money. It's good to make use of their free time after class, (5) _______ they often lose balance between work and study. (6) _______ part-time jobs may offer practical benefits, students should not let them interfere with their education, which must stay the top priority in university life.
Question 4. A. so  
B. for  
C. and 
D. but
Question 5. A. yet  
B. and 
C. so 
D. or
Question 6. A. Once 
B. So that  
C. Because  
D. Although
cloze_group | câu: 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
3. (7) _______ I was a child, computers have always fascinated me. (8) _______ I grew up, I started to take more interest in working with computers and using them. I began by making simple quiz apps and programmes for taking notes, (9) _______ then I went to university and completed a course in software development when I finished high school. (p. 24, Bright 12)
Question 7. A. For 
B. When 
C. Since  
D. So
Question 8. A. As  
B. Since 
C. Though 
D. If
Question 9. A. because  
B. as if 
C. yet 
D. and  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 2]

 

 
Chọn đáp án phù hợp nhất để hoàn thành đoạn văn: 
1. Many secondary school students dream of studying abroad (10) _______. However, (11) _______ such 
as the US, students may experience culture shock. One of the best ways to deal with culture shock is to 
research the local culture in advance. For example, (12) _______, reading about British culture can be 
helpful. 
(p. 30, Global Success 12)
Question 10.  
A. if they find good schools in their own country 
B. as they believe it might be too challenging 
C. though they think it’s their only path to success 
D. because they think it is a wonderful opportunity
Question 11.  
A. while studying in a foreign country 
B. because life abroad is always simple and familiar 
C. in case students never leave their hometown 
D. so students can avoid making new friends
Question 12.  
A. even though you plan to study in your hometown 
B. if you're attending a UK university  
C. unless you choose a British college instead 
D. so you won’t need to learn anything after arriving
cloze_group | câu: 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 | trang 3-4 | rule_high
2. (13) _______, people had to write letters or meet in person to communicate. (14) _______, they often created deeper and more meaningful connections. (15) _______, we communicate more frequently but often less meaningfully. Many feel more disconnected despite being constantly online, (16) _______ and endless scrolling.
Question 13.  
A. Before smartphones existed 
B. So phones had never been invented 
C. Though smartphones were not common 
D. In case smartphones were invented
Question 14.  
A. Whereas these methods were slower and less convenient 
B. Because these methods were faster and more modern 
C. So these methods were rarely used for communication 
D. If these methods had nothing to do with human bonding
Question 15.  
A. While smartphones remained unpopular 
B. Although smartphones are no longer in use 
C. Unless smartphones had improved communication 
D. Now that smartphones are everywhere
Question 16.  
A. as real conversations are replaced by short texts 
B. even if people talk more deeply face to face 
C. since meaningful dialogue happens more online 
D. so everyone enjoys better attention spans 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
3. Technology can exert its power on almost every aspect of our lives, (17) _______. Social media links 
us all together, (18) _______. It is time we realised that the power of connections is built from our own 
efforts. (19) _______, we have to spend a considerable amount of time doing activities together, listening, 
understanding and sharing feelings. This not only helps strengthen our mind and body (20) _______. 
(p. 18, Friends Global 12)
Question 17.  
A. so personal relationships remain unaffected 
B. if personal relationships are not influenced at all 
C. and personal relationships are no exception  
D. because personal relationships are kept separate
Question 18.  
A. though we believe they improve emotional communication 
B. if we assume they are better than in-person interaction 
C. but we mistake them for a perfect replacement for relationships  
D. but we think they strengthen friendships
Question 19.  
A. Whether we choose to stay technologically or socially connected 
B. Even though we refuse to engage in digital communication 
C. Unless we ignore both technology and human contact 
D. Because we decide not to connect with anyone at all
Question 20.  
A. so that it leads to more anxiety and misunderstanding 
B. but also keeps us away from isolation and loneliness  
C. while it pushes us further into social media addiction 
D. although it limits our ability to be truly alone

D. so that

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. (21) _______, zero-waste shops are becoming a good alternative to traditional ones. They encourage people to buy only what they need and avoid unnecessary packaging. Customers bring their own containers, refill essentials, and purchase in bulk, (22) _______. Although prices can sometimes be higher, the long-term benefits outweigh the costs. (23) _______, the movement could grow rapidly.
Question 21.  
A. Although plastic pollution is harming oceans and wildlife 
B. When plastic pollution has no effect on oceans and wildlife 
C. Even if plastic pollution were improving oceans and wildlife 
D. Considering that plastic pollution is harming oceans and wildlife
Question 22.  
A. yet waste decreases at the start 
B. as if waste had disappeared 
C. so waste is reduced at the source 
D. if waste becomes serious at the source
Question 23.  
A. Unless governments try to prevent these businesses with penalties 
B. Considering that governments ignore these businesses entirely 
C. If governments support these businesses with incentives 
D. Until governments discourage these businesses with restrictions 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
2. (24) _______, they not only express pride but also keep traditions alive for future generations. Such 
clothing often carries symbols, patterns, or colours that tell stories, (25) _______. Although globalisation 
encourages modern fashion, many people still wear traditional dress on festivals or weddings. (26) 
_______, cultural identity will remain strong over time; otherwise, it risks fading away.
Question 24.  
A. As individuals selected clothing that celebrated foreign heritage 
B. When individuals wear garments that reflect their heritage 
C. Since people choose garments that have no link to their heritage 
D. So that individuals put on outfits that represent their heritage
Question 25.  
A. as if it only copied designs from unrelated cultures 
B. because it tells no story of the community’s traditions 
C. so it is a living record of a community’s values 
D. yet it becomes a display without cultural value
Question 26.  
A. If these practices are maintained 
B. Once such customs vanish in daily life 
C. As if these practices were replaced 
D. Though these traditions are not forgotten
arrangement_group | câu: 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-6 | rule_high
3. On September 11, 2001, (27) _______, firefighters in New York City displayed extraordinary bravery. (28) _______, they rushed into the burning buildings to rescue those trapped inside. Many continued climbing the stairs (29) _______. They carried heavy equipment (30) _______. Tragically, hundreds lost their lives because the towers collapsed before everyone could be saved. Their sacrifices were not only acts of courage but also symbols of selfless service.
Question 27.  
A. or they assumed the towers would never collapse 
B. as though nothing dangerous could happen to them 
C. though they knew their lives were at great risk 
D. because they believed their mission was safe
Question 28.  
A. Even if the Twin Towers were secure 
B. So the Twin Towers were untouched 
C. Since the Twin Towers were stable 
D. As the Twin Towers were struck
Question 29.  
A. before thousands of people had refused to evacuate 
B. even if nobody was leaving the buildings 
C. while thousands of people were trying to escape 
D. but people had not already reached safety
Question 30.  
A. as it slowed them down and endangered more people 
B. because they wanted to appear stronger 
C. though carrying equipment was only for ceremonial display 
D. in order to fight the fires and guide survivors to safety

Language exchange apps connect learners and allow them to share culture and ideas, (1) _______ they make learning a language more interesting. For example, (2) _______ people finish a lesson, they can open these apps to chat with native speakers, which helps them apply theory in a natural context. Users can choose partners freely, whether they are beginners (3) _______ advanced speakers, which makes the experience flexible and inclusive.
Question 1.  A. as  
B. if 
C. when 
D. so

B. while 
C. because 
D. as if
Question 3.  A. and 
B. or  
C. yet 
D. so

3. (7) _______ noise pollution seriously affects health and quality of life in cities, it is often ignored. (8) _______ traffic, construction, and industrial activities in urban areas never stop, many residents suffer from constant noise. Noise disturbs sleep and concentration, (9) _______ productivity also declines. (10) _______ people have been exposed to loud sounds for long periods, hearing loss may occur, and stress levels rise. The problem continues to grow while urban areas expand rapidly.

B. Once 
C. Although  
D. If

B. Until 
C. As 
D. So

C. but 
D. as

D. Before  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 2]

 

 
4. The gig economy has grown quickly, (11) _______ technology now allows people to connect instantly 
with customers. Many workers choose this path (12) _______ it offers flexible schedules. They drive for 
Uber or deliver food so that they can increase their incomes. It is (13) _______ a convenient system that 
thousands of students and part-time workers join every month. Although the work seems attractive, it also 
has challenges. Drivers may earn well when demand is high, (14) _______ their income drops during 
quiet hours. (15) _______ they often work long shifts, they still lack benefits such as health insurance.
Question 11. A. and 
B. so 
C. before 
D. for
Question 12. A. because 
B. though 
C. if 
D. so

B. such  
C. while 
D. if
Question 14. A. until 
B. and  
C. yet 
D. so that
Question 15. A. Before  
B. When 
C. Because  
D. Whereas

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. (16) _______, migrants settle in overcrowded areas, which soon turn into slums. These neighbourhoods expand quickly, (17) _______. Since slums are often located near economic centres, they also provide labour that sustains the city. Yet, in case living standards are ignored, problems like crime and disease will spread. Therefore, the growth of slums (18) _______ that urban development must include everyone.
Question 16.  
A. Unless governments fail to provide cheap housing 
B. Because governments always guarantee affordable housing 
C. When governments fail to provide affordable housing 
D. As if governments had already solved the housing shortage
Question 17.  
A. as if they completely lacked water, sanitation, and healthcare 
B. because they enjoy an excess of water, sanitation, and healthcare 
C. even though they already met every need for sanitation and healthcare 
D. whereas they lack needs such as water, sanitation, and healthcare
Question 18.  
A. is required for urban development 
B. is either humans' fault or cities' incapability 
C. tells that we should exclude some groups 
D. is both a challenge and a reminder

2. Malala Yousafzai, a Pakistani girl born in 1997, was a global symbol of courage. (19) ________, Malala continued attending school and speaking out for equality. She wrote a blog under a pseudonym to describe life under the Taliban, (20) _______. In 2012, she was attacked by extremists, (21) _______. (22) _______, Malala gave speeches around the world, urging leaders to invest in girls’ education. She co- founded the Malala Fund, which supports schools in many countries.
Question 19.  
A. While many girls in her region were forced to stay home 
B. Because all girls in her region were free to attend school 
C. Even if every girl in her region had equal access to school 
D. As though girls in her region never faced any restrictions 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]
Question 20.  
A. so that the world could understand the situation 
B. though the world did not understand the situation 
C. because the world had no interest in the situation 
D. and her words could never reveal the situation
Question 21.  
A. because she did not survive the attack 
B. so the attack had silenced her forever 
C. yet she survived and grew even stronger 
D. though she gave up all her efforts afterwards
Question 22.  
A. If she recovered from the attack 
B. After she had recovered from the attack 
C. Unless she recovers after the attack 
D. As if she had remained too weak to act

3. The blending of customs creates challenges, (23) _______, so multicultural families often become more adaptable. (24) _______, the household often becomes a place of cultural exchange. Children raised in such families may speak two or more languages, celebrate multiple holidays, and learn to respect different values. However, parents should remain open-minded for the family to develop harmony; (25) _______. In the end, mixed marriages not only unite two individuals (26) _______. They prove that diversity can be a source of strength rather than division.
Question 23.  
A. because it disturbs daily life 
B. though it had no impact on life 
C. as soon as it removes all joy 
D. yet it also enriches daily life
Question 24.  
A. Because each partner carries distinct traditions 
B. Although both partners share identical traditions 
C. Only if neither partner brought any tradition at all 
D. While both partners rejected their own traditions
Question 25.  
A. or misunderstandings will never appear 
B. but harmony could be guaranteed 
C. otherwise, misunderstandings can arise 
D. so communication problems were possible
Question 26.  
A. and they separated communities instead 
B. but also build bridges between communities 
C. because they increased divisions 
D. yet they break connections between groups

4. The “Meatless Monday” encourages individuals to avoid meat once a week, so that they can reduce carbon emissions and improve their diets. The idea spread quickly (27) _______. The collective impact of the movement is significant, (28) _______. (29) _______, the reduction in demand can influence farming practices. Many families enjoy experimenting with plant-based recipes, while others see it as an opportunity to save money. (30) _______, the movement will still spark conversation about responsible eating.
Question 27.  
A. as long as many educational groups refused to support it 
B. so schools, restaurants, and celebrities ignored it 
C. after schools, restaurants, and celebrities began to support it 
D. as though influencers and celebrities discouraged people from it 
 

 

[Page 4]

B. if skipping meat for one day were completely pointless 
C. even though skipping meat for one day increases emissions 
D. though skipping meat for just one day may seem small

A. If millions of people join the effort 
B. Until only a handful of people could join 
C. Because nobody joins the effort 
D. Even if no one was willing to join the effort
Question 30.

D. Even if not everyone participates regularly

When people lose their jobs, they not only face financial difficulties (21) _______. In many cases, this situation may push some individuals to commit illegal acts such as robbery or drug dealing, (22) _______. To reduce crime, governments should create more job opportunities, improve education, and support poor families. (23) _______, they will have less motivation to break the law.
Question 21.  
A. so they end up feeling despair and having no hope ahead 
B. because they become discouraged and lose faith in tomorrow 
C. but also feel frustrated and hopeless about their future 
D. if they grow upset and cannot see a future for themselves
Question 22.  
A. but they sometimes turn to violence just to get by 
B. or they may even resort to violence as a way to survive 
C. when they choose violent means simply to make ends meet 
D. because they might rely on violent acts in order to survive
Question 23.  
A. If people can earn a stable income 
B. Unless people are able to earn a steady living 
C. Though they manage to secure earnings 
 
D. So people obtain consistent sources of income

2. Renewable energy is becoming important while fossil fuels are running out. (24) _______, future 
generations will face serious environmental problems. (25) _______, we reduce greenhouse gas emissions, 
and we also protect natural resources. Renewable energy can replace fossil fuels in many areas, (26) 
_______. Before it is too late, humans must change their lifestyle. Renewable energy may look expensive 
now, but it is the only way to build a sustainable future.
Question 24.  
A. Because countries invest in solar, wind, and hydro power 
B. If countries rely only on solar, wind, and hydro energy 
C. Unless countries invest in solar, wind, and hydro power 
D. Although nations put money into solar, wind, and hydro energy
Question 25.  
A. When we use renewable energy 
B. So that we can depend on renewable energy  
C. If we only consume fossil fuels 
D. Although we choose renewable energy
Question 26.  
A. whereas technology continues to develop 
 
B. even though technology continues to advance 
C. when technology stops making progress 
 
D. provided that technology keeps developing

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. Tet is the most important festival in Vietnam. (16) _______, families always prepare carefully. People clean their houses as well as decorate them with peach blossoms or kumquat trees with red details (17) _______. Although people spend a lot of money, they are happy to do so since Tet happens only once a year. When Tet begins, relatives visit one another and give ‘li xi’ to children. Everyone feels joyful (18) _______.
Question 16.  
A. After the festival has arrived 
B. Despite the festival's beginning 
C. Although the festival is coming soon 
D. Before the festival really comes
Question 17.  
A. despite prosperity and happiness 
B. though they hope to attract fortune and joy 
C. unless they wish to receive luck and cheerful spirits 
D. so that they can welcome luck and happiness
Question 18.  
A. because Tet is not only a holiday but also a time for reunion 
B. when Tet serves as both a celebration and a chance to gather 
C. in case Tet functions not only as a holiday but as a family reunion 
D. as if Tet is regarded as both a festival and a reunion time 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
2. (19) _______, it also creates waste that is very hard to recycle. Electronic waste has become a serious 
environmental problem (20) _______. E-waste contains dangerous chemicals, (21) _______ that people’s 
health is at risk. Some countries export e-waste to poorer regions, whereas local communities have to 
suffer from pollution. (22) _______, they release toxic substances, and these poisons remain for a long 
time.
Question 19.  
A. Although technology makes life easier 
B. Unless devices make modern life comfortable 
C. Because technology brings convenience 
D. Before technology provides ease for life
Question 20.  
A. until consumers replace their gadgets yearly and dispose of the old models 
B. though individuals purchase new electronics annually and abandon the old ones 
C. since people buy new devices every year and throw away the old ones 
D. unless users keep upgrading to newer devices while discarding the outdated ones
Question 21.  
A. so that it results in harmful effects on soil and water 
B. as long as it leads to heavy land and water contamination 
C. while it is capable of polluting the ground and water sources 
D. so it can cause such serious damage to soil and water
Question 22.  
A. When devices are dumped in landfills 
B. So machines are discarded into landfills 
C. Unless electronics are in garbage areas 
D. So as to reduce stress on dumping grounds
arrangement_group | câu: 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-7 | rule_high
3. Social media is very popular, but it is also a risky place where many users lose their privacy. (23) _______, it may be difficult to stay safe. When your account gets hacked, your private photos and messages can be exposed. You should always check your settings carefully (24) _______, and avoid posting details like your phone number or address. Also, be careful with friend requests, (25) _______ to approach you. (26) _______, we must protect our information more seriously.
Question 23.  
A. Though people can learn about you on the internet 
B. If someone knows too much about you online 
C. As if others found out a lot about you online 
D. Because strangers may not discover your life on the web
Question 24.  
A. when you do not share private details 
B. before you share personal information 
C. unless you reveal your personal data 
D. despite your plan to post updates
Question 25.  
A. so cybercriminals might act like familiar friends 
B. while online thieves try to pose as someone you know 
C. so that attackers disguise themselves as acquaintances 
D. as hackers may pretend to be people you know
Question 26.  
A. Though social media is linking millions worldwide 
B. Unless social platforms are bringing users together 
C. Now that social media connects millions of people 
D. In order that online communities are connecting people 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
4. (27) _______, they face many challenges in adapting to a new environment. Some quickly follow the 
local lifestyle (28) _______, whereas others try to keep their cultural identity. While learning new customs 
is useful, forgetting one’s roots can make people feel lost. Immigrants should practice their own festivals 
and food (29) _______. (30) _______, they will live more confidently.
Question 27.  
A. Until people immigrate 
B. Whereas people settle in another country 
C. As people go abroad to live 
D. Unless individuals move overseas
Question 28.  
A. once they behave just like natives 
B. despite acting like members of that culture  
C. unless they live like people around 
D. as though they were born in that culture
Question 29.  
A. so as to remember their traditions where they come from 
B. in order that they can recall the customs of the new culture 
C. as long as they integrate themselves into the new culture 
D. lest they remember the cultural habits of their home country
Question 30.  
A. Provided that they respect both local culture and their own 
B. When they forget not only the host culture but also their own 
C. If they disrespect the traditions of the new society and their homeland 
D. Although they honour both the local way of life and their own heritage 

 

[Page 5]

MP/NGỮ PHÁP ỨNG DỤNG/CHUYÊN ĐỀ 13 - TRẠNG TỪ LIÊN KẾT/13 Trạng từ liên kết (Bài tập Ứng dụng).pdf
cloze_group | câu: 1, 2, 3 | trang 1-1 | rule_high
[Page 1] TRẠNG TỪ LIÊN KẾT - BÀI TẬP ỨNG DỤNG Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Chọn đáp án đúng để hoàn thành đoạn văn: 1. (1) _______, healthy eating, such as using fresh, natural ingredients, provides more nutrients. (2) _______, home-cooked meals are often cheaper and safer. (3) _______, fast food and packaged meals are usually high in fat, sugar, and preservatives. So why do people still choose the worse option?
Question 1. A. However 
B. Afterwards 
C. Meanwhile 
D. Clearly
Question 2. A. Furthermore 
B. Notwithstanding 
C. In practice 
D. Ultimately
Question 3. A. What’s more 
B. Meanwhile 
C. To conclude 
D. Accordingly
cloze_group | câu: 4, 5, 6 | trang - | rule_high
2. (4) _______, having a job that matches your passion is the best. (5) _______, in reality, few people get that chance due to financial or social pressure. Many settle for stable but unexciting jobs. (6) _______, they treat their passion as a hobby – something they do after work, like how students used to draw or play music after school.
Question 4. A. Otherwise 
B. Thus 
C. Additionally 
D. Undoubtedly
Question 5. A. Yet 
B. Hence 
C. For example 
D. In theory
Question 6. A. All in all 
B. At the same time 
C. Importantly 
D. At last
cloze_group | câu: 7, 8, 9 | trang - | rule_high
3. Today, people can access massive amounts of information anytime, anywhere. (7) _______, AI tools are generating even more content at an unprecedented speed. (8) _______, information spreads even more rapidly – including false or misleading news. We must learn to verify what we read. (9) _______, misinformation will continue to confuse, mislead, and overwhelm the public.
Question 7. A. Namely 
B. Subsequently 
C. At the same time 
D. In brief
Question 8. A. To conclude 
B. As a consequence 
C. Clearly 
D. In contrast
Question 9. A. In addition 
B. Again 
C. Therefore 
D. Otherwise
cloze_group | câu: 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 | trang 2-2 | rule_high
4. Fast food chains and international restaurants have become popular. (10) _______, here in Viet Nam, we can enjoy Italian pizza or Japanese sushi, in addition to our traditional food. (11) _______, some international dishes use local ingredients, which makes them unique and more suitable to local tastes. (12) _______, Vietnamese specialities, such as pho and banh mi, are also gaining popularity worldwide. (p. 24, Global Success 12)
Question 10. A. Similarly 
B. On the whole 
C. Additionally 
D. For example
Question 11. A. In addition 
B. Otherwise 
C. In theory 
D. In brief
Question 12. A. In summary 
B. For instance 
C. On the other hand 
D. All in all 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 2]

 

 
5. Renewable energy refers to power sources that naturally replenish over time. (13) _______, solar energy 
is produced from sunlight, while geothermal energy comes from the Earth’s internal heat. (14) _______, 
this energy is actually clean and sustainable; (15) _______, it often requires large infrastructure and 
depends on environmental conditions. Despite such challenges, many countries are shifting toward 
renewables for their environmental benefits. (16) _______, it offers a long-term solution to reduce 
emissions and combat climate change.
Question 13. A. Still 
B. Besides 
C. Consequently 
D. Namely
Question 14. A. In practice 
B. To summarize 
C. Afterwards 
D. Similarly
Question 15. A. all in all 
B. nonetheless 
C. hence 
D. what’s more
Question 16. A. Afterwards 
B. At the same time 
C. In the same way 
D. On the whole
cloze_group | câu: 17, 18, 19, 20 | trang - | rule_high
6. There are many push factors which force people away from rural life. (21) _______, rural residents have fewer employment options because there aren't many large companies nearby. (22) _______, rural jobs are mainly related to agriculture, so crop failure due to natural disasters can make it difficult to earn a good living. (23) _______, there are pull factors that attract people to urban life. Urban areas have more industries. (24) _______, people can easily get good jobs and achieve a higher standard of living. (p. 58, Bright 12)
Question 17. A. Otherwise 
B. For example 
C. Furthermore 
D. Consequently
Question 18. A. After all 
B. Thus 
C. Nevertheless 
D. What's more
Question 19. A. In contrast 
B. In addition 
C. To conclude 
D. Subsequently
Question 20. A. Otherwise 
B. Actually 
C. As a result 
D. Afterwards

7. How can something so nice be so bad for us? (21) _______, naturally occurring sugar, for example, in fruit, is considered a healthy sugar. (22) _______, refined sugar is not healthy and is added to many processed foods to make them taste better. (23) _______, refined sugar has no nutritional value and therefore offers 'empty' calories. (24) _______, it has no fibre, unlike the natural sugar in fruit, and therefore is easy to eat a lot of. (25) _______, excess calories cause weight gain. Too much sugar also encourages childhood tooth decay and diabetes in later life. (p. 81, Friends Global 12)
Question 21. A. Otherwise 
B. In the same way  
C. Obviously 
D. Consequently
Question 22. A. However 
B. In theory 
C. In particular 
D. To summarize
Question 23. A. Hence 
B. Moreover 
C. Nonetheless 
D. Still
Question 24. A. In brief 
B. Notwithstanding 
C. Also 
D. Thus
Question 25. A. Namely 
B. Similarly 
C. Meanwhile 
D. Consequently

[Page 1] THI ONLINE – ĐỀ SỐ 1 Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 30. 1. Perseverance plays a vital role in every success story. (1) _______, many great inventors failed countless times before achieving their goals. Thomas Edison, (2) _______, made thousands of attempts before creating a working light bulb. (3) _______, he never gave up, and his persistence led to inventions that changed the world forever.
Question 1.  A. To conclude 
B. In fact 
C. At the same time 
D. In contrast
Question 2.  A. therefore 
B. in addition 
C. then 
D. for example
Question 3.  A. Nevertheless 
B. As a result 
C. For example 
D. Again

2. Hard skills help employees perform technical tasks efficiently; (4) _______, soft skills such as teamwork, leadership, and communication are increasingly recognised as equally important. (5) _______, many companies are now seeking candidates who not only have strong professional knowledge but also possess strong interpersonal abilities. (6) _______, workers with both sets of skills can adapt better to challenges and contribute more effectively.

B. for example 
C. after that  
D. meanwhile
Question 5.  A. As a result

C. In brief 
D. On the contrary
Question 6.  A. Similarly 
B. Still 
C. However 
D. Indeed

(8) _______, climate change makes survival even harder for vulnerable species. (9) _______, polar bears are losing sea ice, while sea turtles struggle with plastic waste. Protecting endangered species not only saves animals but also helps keep ecosystems in balance.

B. Consequently 
C. Subsequently 
D. As a result

B. Moreover

lose weight, eat less than you spend. (15) _______, to gain weight, consume more than you burn. 
Controlling calorie intake requires careful attention to portion sizes. (16) _______, regular exercise is 
necessary to balance energy and improve fitness. (17) _______, only healthy and sustainable methods can 
help you manage weight effectively in the long run.

C. Moreover  
D. In addition

6. Each family has different income levels, goals, and opportunities. (18) _______, the wealth gap arises from these differences. In large cities, wealthy families enjoy modern houses, good schools, and advanced healthcare. (19) _______, the poor may live in slums with little access to services. Beyond basic needs, many rich households can afford luxuries such as travel and entertainment. (20) _______, others still struggle every day just to survive. (21) _______, the wealth gap is unavoidable, and it reflects both economic inequality and social division.

(23) _______, these devices often contain dangerous substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium that can leak into the soil and water. (24) _______, the environment suffers, as toxic chemicals destroy farmland and pollute rivers. (25) _______, local communities face serious health risks, including respiratory problems and even cancer. This is a growing problem as technology continues to develop year by year.

[Page 1] THI ONLINE – LÝ THUYẾT (ĐỀ SỐ 1) Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. Bangkok is famous for its busy lifestyle and crowded streets. Every day, thousands of drivers (1) _______ travel to work or school spend hours stuck in traffic. The roads, (2) _______ are often full of cars, buses, and motorbikes, make the situation worse. There are families (3) _______ daily routines are affected by the long delays.
Question 1.  A. which 
B. who  
C. what 
D. whom
Question 2.  A. that 
B. of which 
C. which  
D. whose
Question 3.  A. which 
B. whom  
C. who 
D. whose

2. Diwali is a traditional festival (4) _______ is celebrated in many parts of India. It is a time for families to gather together to share love and happiness. During the festival, lamps, (5) _______ people use to decorate their houses, make the streets look bright and beautiful. Children enjoy fireworks (6) _______ light up the night sky. It is also a festival of gratitude where people remember unity and peace.
Question 4.  A. whom 
B. of which  
C. that  
D. who
Question 5.  A. which  
B. that 
C. who 
D. whom
Question 6.  A. whose 
B. who  
C. what 
D. that

3. Nick Vujicic, (7) _______ was born without arms and legs, never gave up on life. He grew up with his parents, (8) _______ encouragement gave him the courage to face challenges. At school, he was often bullied, but he found strength in his faith. Later, he became a motivational speaker and travelled around the world to share his story. He also founded an organisation (9) _______ helps people overcome difficulties and find hope. His life is an example for anyone (10) _______ wants to believe that nothing is impossible.
Question 7.  A. which 
B. that 
C. who  
D. whom
Question 8.  A. whom 
B. whose  
C. who  
D. of which

B. whose  
C. which  
D. who

B. whom  
C. whose

4. In many rural areas of Africa, there are villages (11) _______ do not have access to electricity. People 
(12) _______ live in these places often rely on candles or kerosene lamps, which are unsafe and expensive. 
Solar panels are a new solution (13) _______ brings clean and renewable energy to these communities. 
They are installed by organisations and engineers (14) _______ want to improve people’s lives. Families 
(15) _______ homes once stayed in darkness can finally enjoy light and safety. Children can now study 
at night with lamps which are powered by solar energy.

B. that  
C. of which

cloze_group | câu: 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is a huge area of the ocean (16) _______. Scientists studying the ocean warn that this problem is becoming more dangerous every year. It affects many sea animals (17) _______. There are also fishermen whose work is threatened by polluted waters. The garbage patch is a symbol of human activities that harm the environment. It reminds people (18) _______ that urgent action must be taken to protect our planet.
Question 16.  
A. who travel by ship every day 
 B. contains thousands of small islands 
C. water is always clean and blue 
 D. that is filled with plastic waste
Question 17.  
A. who live safely in polluted areas 
 B. whom people try to protect carefully 
C. which mistake plastic for food 
 D. whose habitats free from danger
Question 18.  
A. which usually ignore environmental issues 
B. who care about the environment and the ocean  
C. whose only interests in economic growth 
D. where nobody wants to take responsibility 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
2. Ho Chi Minh City, (19) _______, is developing at a very fast pace. Every year, thousands of people 
(20) _______ move here to look for better opportunities. The city has many skyscrapers and modern 
facilities, which show its modern lifestyle. However, there are also old neighbourhoods, (21) _______. 
Heavy traffic and pollution are also problems (22) _______. The government is trying to improve 
infrastructure by building new roads, bridges, and metro lines.
Question 19.  
A. whose size among the top cities of Vietnam 
 
B. where is one of the biggest cities in Vietnam 
C. that is regarded as one of the most important cities   
D. which is one of the largest cities in Vietnam
Question 20.  
A. who originally live in rural areas and other cities 
B. that comes mainly from countryside regions 
C. whose homes once in villages and smaller cities 
D. of which originates from rural places and provincial areas
Question 21.  
A. whose loss of traditional beauty and culture 
B. of which the traditional charm is slowly disappearing  
C. that the ancient features are fading with time 
D. in that many of the old values are vanishing little by little
Question 22.  
A. where suffer from difficulties each day 
B. of which impact on people’s everyday routines 
C. that affect the daily lives of residents  
D. whose huge influence on local people’s lives
arrangement_group | câu: 23, 24, 25, 26 | trang 4-4 | rule_high
3. The COVID-19 pandemic changed the way people work in countries that were heavily affected by the crisis. At that time, millions of employees (23) _______ suddenly had to work from home. Companies also provided online tools (24) _______. Many businesses, which realised the benefits of cutting costs, decided to keep remote work even after the pandemic. People (25) _______ will have more chances to succeed in this new working environment. There are workers (26) _______, giving them more time with their families.
Question 23.  
A. whose time for long office hours 
B. which spent hours working in offices 
C. who once spent hours in offices  
D. where they spent their day in offices
Question 24.  
A. at that staff could join virtual meetings and cooperate with colleagues 
B. who made it possible for collaboration and online discussions 
C. which allowed meetings and teamwork to continue  
D. whose purpose of keeping communication among teams active
Question 25.  
A. whose ability to use updated software 
B. which are able to adjust to modern tools 
C. that cannot deal with digital platforms 
D. who can adapt to new technology  
 
 

 

[Page 4]
Question 26.  
A. where the daily routines are arranged with greater freedom 
B. which now enjoy working hours easier to manage 
C. whose schedules have become more flexible  
D. whose flexible timetables to balance life better
cloze_group | câu: 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 5-6 | rule_high
4. ChatGPT is an advanced AI tool (27) _______. It can help students who need explanations or guidance in their studies. Many teachers also create lessons with the support of ChatGPT. In workplaces, ChatGPT assists employees (28) _______. Since its advant, it has been considered a technology (29) _______. However, there are challenges which remind users to apply critical thinking instead of depending fully on AI. In short, ChatGPT is a powerful assistant (30) _______.
Question 27. 
A. whose popularity across almost every country 
B. on that millions of users across the globe rely 
C. that is used by millions of people around the world  
D. who many individuals access AI support daily
Question 28. 
A. who want to write reports, emails, or creative content 
B. which need to prepare documents and written tasks 
C. of which work requires composing texts and official papers 
D. whom often produce letters, essays, or professional writing
Question 29. 
A. whose function and efficiency in daily work 
B. who helps finish tasks faster and more effectively 
C. that saves time and increases productivity  
D. of which users can get results quickly and easily
Question 30. 
A. will influence how schools and companies develop 
B. that is shaping the future of both education and work  
C. whose great impact on classrooms and offices alike 
D. where the direction of learning and jobs is being changed

[Page 1] THI ONLINE – LÝ THUYẾT (ĐỀ SỐ 2) Ngữ pháp Tiếng Anh Ứng dụng Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 1. Gender inequality in the workplace is an issue that affects both career development and personal confidence. There used to be times (1) _______ women faced discrimination. And, in fact, today, there are still offices (2) _______ women are paid less than men for the same job. Gender equality is an important goal (3) _______ ensures men and women have the same opportunities at work.
Question 1.  A. who 
B. when  
C. where 
D. which
Question 2.  A. of which  
B. what 
C. who 
D. where
Question 3.  A. that 
B. when  
C. who 
D. whose

Deepfakes are a risk that reminds us to be careful with everything we see online.

C. which  
D. who

On this platform, users can create their own videos, many of (8) _______ can go viral and influence fashion, music, and even language. However, there are concerns about safety and privacy, (9) _______ remind parents and educators to guide young users. There are also periods (10) _______ critics argue that TikTok distracts students from their studies, but its impact continues to grow.

D. when

Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C or D on your answer sheet to indicate the option 
that best fits each of the numbered blanks. 
1. Rhino poaching is a crime that threatens the survival of these endangered animals. They are killed for 
their horns, (11) _______. Africa, which is home to the largest rhino population in the world, has become 
the centre of this fight. There are reserves (12) _______ to track poachers. Conservation groups, (13) 
_______, raise awareness among local communities.
standalone_mcq | câu: 11, 12, 13 | trang - | rule_low
Question 11.  
A. many of whose value is determined by illegal traders 
B. many of which are sold illegally on the black market  
C. many of that are traded secretly in black markets 
D. many of who the price is extremely high in illegal sales
Question 12.  
A. where technology such as drones and cameras is used 
B. which tools like drones and advanced cameras are applied 
C. of which system with modern equipment to follow hunters 
D. who rely on drones and cameras to support protection work
Question 13.  
A. whose the main goal of saving animals 
B. aim at protecting wild animals and nature 
C. whose mission is to save wildlife  
D. that try to keep endangered species safe
cloze_group | câu: 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
2. Many adults (14) _______ face more difficulties than children. Most learners' motivation comes from their jobs, since English is a skill that helps them get better opportunities. Often, they attend evening classes. Some learners join online communities, (15) _______. Regarding hardships, adults often encounter grammar and pronunciation problems, (16) _______. They sometimes feel lazy or frustrated, (17) _______.
Question 14.  
A. whose choice to study English while working 
B. which start learning English at an old age 
C. who decide to learn English later in life  
D. where many people begin studying English much later
Question 15.  
A. which allowing them to communicate with learners worldwide 
B. where they can practice with people from different countries  
C. that give opportunities to practice English with foreigners 
D. whose members and English learners from many nations
Question 16.  
A. many of who improve after practice 
B. that rules require years to fully understand 
C. many of that are challenging to control 
D. many of which take a long time to master
Question 17.  
A. which can hinder their learning progress 
B. that slow down their improvement 
C. whose effect on their overall motivation 
D. where learners lose energy and interest  
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
3. Online platforms like Coursera and Khan Academy provide free or affordable education. Thanks to 
these platforms, many learners (18) _______ can now study at their own pace. They offer courses (19) 
_______. There are online classrooms (20) _______ through videos and discussions. These platforms also 
give certificates when learners complete courses, which motivates them to finish learning. Online learning 
has broken barriers of distance, time and cost, (21) _______.
Question 18.  
A. who may not have access to traditional universities 
B. which may not reach formal higher education 
C. whose limited chance to attend regular universities  
D. where students cannot enter typical university systems
Question 19.  
A. whose contents such as math and physics 
B. who include topics from math to history 
C. which cover subjects from science to art  
D. when learners find materials in some fields
Question 20.  
A. allow discussions between students and their tutors 
B. why they let learners communicate with others 
C. where students interact with teachers and peers  
D. whose design for cooperation among members
Question 21.  
A. that helps bring learning opportunities to more people 
B. which makes education more accessible and inclusive  
C. where knowledge becomes easier for everyone to reach 
D. whose role is to expand equal chances for education
cloze_group | câu: 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 | trang 4-6 | rule_high
4. Teachers who choose to work in rural areas often face many difficulties. They teach in schools (22) _______. There are remote villages (23) _______. Young teachers sometimes struggle with low salaries and poor living conditions. There are moments (24) _______, yet they still have to continue their mission. However, their efforts change the lives of children (25) _______. Their journey is inspiring and demonstrates the importance of education and devotion.
Question 22.  
A. whose classrooms without enough resources for students 
B. when basic facilities like books and power are missing 
C. which may lack books, technology, and even electricity  
D. who are short of learning materials and modern equipment
Question 23.  
A. where they must walk several kilometres just to attend class 
B. why they require students to travel long distances to study 
C. of which location far from schools and difficult to reach 
D. who make learners spend hours walking to classrooms
Question 24.  
A. whose frustration and isolation are filled 
B. where bring them disappointment 
C. when they feel lonely and discouraged  
D. cause teachers to experience despair
Question 25.  
A. who need hope the most 
B. which require encouragement 
C. whose guidance and education 
D. wait for chances and better futures 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
5. Cristiano Ronaldo, (26) _______, began his football journey at a young age. He moved to Lisbon, (27) 
_______. His talent quickly impressed scouts from Manchester United. There were many matches (28) 
_______. He scored goals, many of which became unforgettable moments for fans. He won trophies with 
Manchester United. Later, he continued his journey across Europe, (29) _______. Ronaldo’s career is an 
inspiring story (30) _______.
Question 26.  
A. who was born on the island of Madeira 
B. which was born on the island of Madeira 
C. whose birthplace the island of Madeira 
D. that was originally from the island of Madeira
Question 27.  
A. whose academy where he grows as a young footballer 
B. which he entered Sporting CP’s academy to improve his talent 
C. where he joined Sporting CP’s academy and developed his skills  
D. that supported his early training at Sporting CP
Question 28.  
A. when their atmosphere allowed him to display his great effort 
B. highlighted his energy, pace, and strong mentality 
C. where Ronaldo showed his speed, strength, and determination  
D. who revealed his power, pace, and ambition on the pitch
Question 29.  
A. which helped build the image of one of the greatest players in history 
B. that contributed to creating his reputation as a football legend 
C. whose influence shaped him into one of football’s iconic figures 
D. why his career was rising to be considered among the very best
Question 30.  
A. whose message about how effort can eventually lead to success 
B. who shows the importance of persistence and love for football 
C. that teaches us why dedication and passion matter most  
D. when his fans learn the value of hard work and determination
