Question 1. The word “applying” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. recovering 
B. adapting 
C. breeding 
D. adopting
Question 2. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the green activities? 
A. join beach clean-ups 
B. go on eco-tours  
C. learn about local wildlife 
D. visit organic farms
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Bali only focuses on beaches and culture in tourism. 
B. The local government in Bali banned single-use plastic in 2022. 
C. Tourists in Bali can enjoy the island while still protecting the environment. 
D. Green tourism in Bali has reduced the number of tourists visiting the island.
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. The giant panda is one of the most famous and loved animals in the world. It lives mainly in the mountains of Sichuan, China. Known for its black and white fur, the panda has become a symbol of wildlife protection. Although the panda’s population has improved, it is still considered a vulnerable species. The main threat to pandas is habitat loss. As forests are cut down for farming and development, pandas lose their homes and food sources. They rely heavily on bamboo, which only grows in certain areas. Climate change also affects bamboo growth, making survival even harder for the species. To protect pandas, the Chinese government has created large nature reserves. Breeding programs and education efforts have also helped raise awareness. Tourists can visit panda centres to learn more and support conservation. Giant pandas are more than just a cute animal. They represent the need to protect other endangered species and their environments. Saving the panda means saving the forests, which benefits many other animals as well. [Page 5]
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“Although the panda’s population has improved, it is still considered a vulnerable species.” 
A. The number of pandas is growing fast and they are no longer in danger. 
B. Even though there are more pandas now, they still face the risk of extinction. 
C. Pandas are completely safe thanks to the efforts of the Chinese government. 
D. Because their population is high, pandas are not considered vulnerable anymore.
Question 5. The word “survival” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. development  
B. living  
C. existence 
D. death
Question 6. The word “their” in paragraph 4 refers to _______. 
A. giant pandas 
B. environments 
C. the forests 
D. other endangered species
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Giant pandas can survive in any climate as long as bamboo is available. 
B. The panda population is growing so fast that it is no longer a concern. 
C. The loss of forest land makes it harder for pandas to find food and shelter. 
D. Tourists are banned from visiting panda centres to protect the species.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 6-6 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Ecotourism is a form of travel that focuses on exploring natural areas while protecting the environment and supporting local communities. Unlike mass tourism, which often leads to pollution and overdevelopment, ecotourism encourages responsible behaviour. In recent years, it has become a growing trend as more travellers seek meaningful experiences and care about nature. People are now more aware of climate change and want to reduce their impact while travelling. Ecotourism offers many different activities. These include hiking through forests, bird-watching, camping in national parks, snorkelling in coral reefs, and visiting wildlife sanctuaries. It also promotes staying in eco-lodges that use renewable energy and local materials. Tourists often take part in environmental education, tree planting, or cultural exchanges with local people. All these activities are designed to be low-impact and educational. A great example of successful ecotourism is Costa Rica. The country protects over 25% of its land through national parks and reserves. Visitors can enjoy rainforests, waterfalls, and volcanoes while learning about biodiversity. Local people benefit from jobs in guiding, hospitality, and conservation projects. However, ecotourism still has some negative sides. If not managed carefully, too many tourists can harm wildlife or damage ecosystems. Some companies may use the term ‘eco’ just for profit. That’s why true ecotourism needs planning, honesty, and long-term thinking.
Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“People are now more aware of climate change and want to reduce their impact while travelling.” 
A. Travellers today are less interested in natural beauty and more focused on comfort. 
B. More and more tourists now avoid travel completely because of its effect on the climate. 
C. Most travellers still ignore climate change when making travel decisions. 
D. Travellers today understand climate change better and try to lessen their travel footprint.
Question 9. The word “It” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. Wildlife  
B. Snorkelling  
C. Ecotourism 
D. Bird-watching 

 

[Page 6]
Question 10. The word “conservation” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______. 
A. threat 
B. protection 
C. consumption 
D. creation
Question 11. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the places visitors can 
enjoy in Costa Rica? 
A. eco-lodges  
B. rainforests 
C. waterfalls 
D. volcanoes
Question 12. The word “damage” in paragraph 4 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. destroy 
B. generate 
C. release 
D. repair
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Ecotourism needs proper management to avoid harming the environment.  
B. Tourists in Costa Rica are not allowed to enter national parks and reserves. 
C. All companies that promote ecotourism always follow eco-friendly practices. 
D. Cultural exchanges are not considered part of ecotourism activities.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore the drawbacks of ecotourism? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author compare ecotourism to another kind of tourism? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

6. Nikola Tesla was a visionary (18) ______ whose work still influences our lives today. Born in 1856, he was fascinated by electricity and devoted his life to (19) _______ its possibilities. Tesla developed the alternating current (AC) system, which made it possible to (20) _______ power over long distances, lighting up cities worldwide. He also experimented with wireless (21) _______ and radio waves far ahead of his time. Brilliant yet humble, Tesla’s imagination and dedication continue to inspire scientists, engineers, and dreamers everywhere.
Question 18. A. discovery  
B. sensor  
C. inventor  
D. performance
Question 19. A. exploring  
B. featuring 
C. inventing 
D. adjusting
Question 20. A. launch  
B. transmit  
C. combine  
D. invent
Question 21. A. command  
B. inventor  
C. performance  
D. communication

Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the advantages of 
driverless vehicles? 
A. reduce accidents  
B. choose the best routes  
C. save fuel and money 
D. follow traffic regulations
Question 2. The word “expensive” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. outdated 
B. modern 
C. costly 
D. affordable
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Weather conditions have no effect on the performance of driverless cars. 
B. Driverless cars cost less than traditional vehicles. 
C. Advanced sensors help improve the safety of driverless cars.  
D. Professional drivers will not be affected by the rise of driverless vehicles.
reading_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. Virtual Reality (VR) is a computer-generated simulation that allows users to experience and interact with a three-dimensional environment as if they were physically present. By wearing a VR headset, users can look around, move, and engage with virtual objects in real time. This technology creates an immersive and realistic sense of presence. In education, VR offers students a chance to explore concepts beyond the limits of the classroom. For example, they can take virtual field trips to historical sites, dive into the human body to study anatomy, or conduct science experiments in a safe virtual lab. Such experiences make learning more engaging and help students understand complex ideas through direct interaction. VR can improve retention, enhance motivation, and bridge the gap between theory and practice. It also makes education more accessible, allowing learners from different locations to share the same virtual space. As VR technology becomes more affordable and advanced, its role in education is expected to grow.
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“In education, VR offers students a chance to explore concepts beyond the limits of the classroom.”  
A. VR allows learners to study ideas and topics outside classroom boundaries. 
B. With VR, students’ understanding is confined to the classroom environment. 
C. VR replaces all classroom activities by offering only virtual experiences. 
D. VR prevents students from interacting with educational content outside school settings. 

 

[Page 5]
Question 5. The word “allows” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. encourages 
 B. hinders  
C. enables 
D. hesitates
Question 6. The word “It” in paragraph 3 refers to _______. 
A. VR  
B. retention 
C. motivation 
D. theory
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Virtual field trips replace all traditional classroom lessons. 
B. Wearing a VR headset lets users interact with objects in real time.  
C. VR only helps with science subjects, not history or anatomy. 
D. The cost of VR technology is currently too high for schools.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 6-6 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. The earliest stage of mass communication was dominated by print media. Newspapers, magazines, and books allowed information to be recorded, stored, and shared widely. The printing press revolutionised literacy and public awareness, enabling ideas to spread across distances faster than word of mouth. This period laid the foundation for modern journalism and public discourse. The invention of radio and later television transformed communication into a real-time, audio-visual experience. Radio brought news, music, and entertainment into homes instantly, while the other added moving images that engaged audiences more deeply. These media created shared national moments, from presidential speeches to major sporting events, and connected people emotionally. The arrival of the internet marked a turning point, allowing instant global communication. Emails replaced letters, websites offered unlimited information, and online news became available 24/7. Social media began to emerge, giving individuals the power to share their own voices alongside traditional media outlets. Today, interactive platforms dominate. People can stream live events, join online discussions, and access personalised content anytime. Media is no longer just consumed—it is created and shaped by users. This stage continues to evolve, pushing communication toward greater speed, personalisation, and global interconnection.
Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“The earliest stage of mass communication was dominated by print media.” 
A. Print media was not widely used during the earliest stage of mass communication. 
B. Print media was the primary means of mass communication in its earliest phase.  
C. The latter mass communication mostly relied on print media forms. 
D. The earliest mass communication happened before print media existed.
Question 9. The phrase “the other” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. radio  
B. television  
C. communication  
D. experience
Question 10. The word “arrival” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______. 
A. risk 
B. destination 
C. advent 
D. disappearance
Question 11. The word “unlimited” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. vital 
B. infinite 
C. large 
D. restricted
Question 12. According to paragraph 4, today, thanks to interactive platforms, people can do all of the 
following, EXCEPT _______. 
A. joining discussions online  
B. accessing personalised content 
C. speeding up communication 
D. streaming live events 

 

[Page 6]
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. People only consume media and do not create content themselves today. 
B. Television was the first technology to bring information instantly to homes. 
C. Social media existed before the invention of the Internet. 
D. Newspapers helped spread information faster than spoken communication.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore experience relating to hearing and watching? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention the creation of media by users? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

/məˌʃiːn ˈlɜː.nɪŋ/ B2 28 man-made nhân tạo adj /ˌmænˈmeɪd/ B1 artificial natural 29 monitor giám sát v /ˈmɒn.ɪ.tər/ B2 observe, track 30 natural language processing xử lý ngôn ngữ tự nhiên n. phr /ˌnætʃ.ər.əl ˈlæŋ.ɡwɪdʒ ˈprəʊ.ses.ɪŋ/ B2 31 personalise / personalize được cá nhân hóa adj /ˈpɜː.sən.ə.laɪzd/ B2 generic, standard customized, tailo...

/ˌeɪ.aɪ ˈpaʊ.əd/ B2 AI-driven manual 2 diagnosis sự chẩn đoán n /ˌdaɪ.əɡˈnəʊ.sɪs/ C1 3 recognition sự nhận diện n /ˌrek.əɡˈnɪʃ.ən/ B2 identification 4 recovery sự hồi phục n /rɪˈkʌv.ər.i/ B2 5 appointment cuộc hẹn n /əˈpɔɪnt.mənt/ B1 6 predictive có tính dự đoán adj /prɪˈdɪk.tɪv/ B2 7 human-centred lấy con người làm trung tâm adj /ˌhjuː.mən ˈsen.təd/ B2 people-oriented 8 manual thủ công adj /ˈmæn.ju.əl/ C1 hand-operated automatic 9 labour lao động n /ˈleɪ.bər/ B2 10 data analysis phân tích dữ liệu n. phr /ˈdeɪ.tə əˈnæl.ə.sɪs/ B2 11 digital kỹ thuật số adj /ˈdɪdʒ.ɪ.təl/ A2 12 platform n /ˈplæt.fɔːm/ B2 13 irrigation sự tưới tiêu n /ˌɪr.ɪˈɡeɪ.ʃən/ C1 watering 14 accurately chính xác adv /ˈæk.jʊ.rət.li/ B2 precisely inaccurately 15 combine kết hợp v /kəmˈbaɪn/ B1 merge separate 16 humidity độ ẩm n /hjuːˈmɪd.ə.ti/ B2 dampness dryness 17 crop yield năng suất mùa màng n. phr /ˌkrɒp ˈjiːld/ B2 18 production sự sản xuất n /prəˈdʌk.ʃən/ B1 manufacturing 19 food shortage sự thiếu hụt lương thực n. phr /ˌfuːd ˈʃɔː.tɪdʒ/ B2 [Page 2] 20 agricultural thuộc về nông nghiệp adj /ˌæɡ.rɪˈkʌl.tʃər.əl/ C1 farming 21 simulate mô phỏng v /ˈsɪm.jʊ.leɪt/ C1 imitate real 22 integrate tích hợp v /ˈɪn.tɪ.ɡreɪt/ B2 incorporate separate 23 accessible có thể tiếp cận adj /əkˈses.ə.bəl/ C1 reachable inaccessible 24 efficiency hiệu quả n /ɪˈfɪʃ.ən.si/ C1 productivity inefficiency 25 customer service dịch vụ khách hàng n. phr /ˈkʌs.tə.mər ˈsɜː.vɪs/ B1 26 translation sự dịch thuật n /trænzˈleɪʃən/ B1 interpretation 27 misunderstand hiểu lầm v /ˌmɪs.ʌn.dəˈstænd/ B2 misinterpret understand 28 genuine chân thật adj /ˈdʒen.ju.ɪn/ B2 authentic fake 29 empathy sự thấu cảm n /ˈem.pə.θi/ C1 compassion indifference 30 replacement sự thay thế n /rɪˈpleɪs.mənt/ C1 substitute 31 decision-making việc ra quyết định n. phr /dɪˈsɪʒ.ən ˌmeɪ.kɪŋ/ C1 32 critical thinking tư duy phản biện n. phr /ˌkrɪt.ɪ.kəl ˈθɪŋ.kɪŋ/ B2 33 brainw...
Question 1. The word “accurate” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. automated 
B. remarkable 
C. wrong 
D. precise
Question 2. According to paragraph 2, AI helps hospitals manage work by all of the following, EXCEPT 
_______. 
A. scheduling appointments 
B. answering patients’ questions 
C. cutting costs 
D. planning treatments
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. AI completely replaces doctors in diagnosing diseases. 
B. AI can only be used in hospitals for scheduling appointments. 
C. Image recognition programs can detect cancer signs faster than doctors. 
D. Privacy and cost are no longer concerns in applying AI to healthcare.
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 4-4 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. Modern agriculture is no longer only about manual labour and traditional methods. Farmers today rely on advanced machines, data analysis, and digital platforms to increase productivity. Smart systems monitor soil quality, control irrigation, and predict weather patterns more accurately than humans. By combining technology with farming, agriculture becomes more sustainable and efficient. One of the most useful innovations is precision farming. Sensors placed in the field measure temperature, humidity, and nutrient levels, while drones capture images of crops from above. These tools help farmers decide when to water, fertilise, or harvest. Artificial intelligence supports this process by turning raw data into clear suggestions, saving time and resources while improving crop yield. The benefits go beyond higher production. Smarter farming methods also reduce waste, use less water, and limit harmful chemicals, which protects the environment. In developing countries, such technology can help communities fight food shortages and adapt to climate change. Although AI is important, the main goal remains to build a stronger and more sustainable agricultural system for the future. [Page 4]
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“By combining technology with farming, agriculture becomes more sustainable and efficient.” 
A. Mixing farming with technology increases sustainability but lowers productivity. 
B. The use of technology in farming helps improve efficiency but reduces sustainability. 
C. Applying technology in farming makes agriculture more eco-friendly and productive.  
D. Technology in agriculture keeps it sustainable while maintaining the same efficiency.
Question 5. The phrase “These tools” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. useful innovations 
 
B. images of crops 
 
C. nutrient levels 
 
D. sensors and drones
Question 6. The word “shortages” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. abundances 
B. limitations 
C. abilities 
D. products
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Modern agriculture depends largely on manual labour and traditional methods. 
B. Precision farming uses sensors and drones to help improve crop yield.  
C. Farmers use drones only to water their crops. 
D. The main goal of AI in farming is to replace farmers and make it sustainable.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. An AI chatbot is a computer program designed to simulate human conversation through text or voice. Using natural language processing and machine learning, it can understand questions, provide answers, and even maintain long discussions. Chatbots are now integrated into websites, messaging apps, and digital platforms, making communication between humans and machines more natural and accessible. The most obvious advantage of this technology is efficiency. In customer service, a chatbot can respond instantly to thousands of users at the same time, solving basic problems without human staff. In education, it supports students with explanations and practice tasks. Even in daily life, people rely on chatbots for translation, scheduling, or quick information. This makes services faster, cheaper, and more convenient. However, chatbots are far from perfect. They sometimes misunderstand context, provide inaccurate information, or sound unnatural in sensitive conversations. Unlike humans, they cannot show genuine empathy or creativity. Overreliance on pre-trained data also limits their ability to deal with unexpected questions. These weaknesses remind us that chatbots are tools, not replacements for human intelligence. A more serious problem is overuse or misuse. Some people depend too heavily on chatbots for homework, writing, or decision-making, which may reduce critical thinking skills. Others worry about “brainwashing,” since users can be influenced by biased or manipulated responses. Therefore, while chatbots bring many benefits, society must use them responsibly to avoid dependency and maintain human judgment.
Question 8. The word “it” in paragraph 1 refers to ______. 
A. machine learning 
 
B. an AI chatbot 
 
C. text or voice 
 
D. human conversation
Question 9. Which of the following is NOT mentioned as one of the abilities of AI chatbots in paragraph 
1? 
A. learn foreign languages 
B. keep long discussions 
C. give answers 
D. understand questions 

 

[Page 5]
Question 10. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
“Overreliance on pre-trained data also limits their ability to deal with unexpected questions.” 
A. Relying too much on pre-trained data makes them less effective with unfamiliar questions. 
B. Heavy use of pre-trained data makes chatbots better at managing predictable data. 
C. Depending on pre-trained data allows them to answer unexpected questions more accurately. 
D. Reliance on data improves efficiency but weakens responses to unfamiliar questions.
Question 11. The word “biased” in paragraph 4 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. fair 
B. wrong 
C. right 
D. unequal
Question 12. According to the passage, what role do chatbots play in education? 
A. They fully replace teachers by designing entire curricula. 
B. They mainly provide explanations and practice tasks for students. 
C. They organise school schedules and manage teachers’ workloads. 
D. They decide which subjects students should focus on.
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Customer service no longer requires human employees as problems are solved by chatbots. 
B. In education, students always receive perfect answers and full creativity from chatbots. 
C. Services become faster and more convenient when chatbots handle basic tasks. 
D. Sensitive conversations sound more natural with chatbots than with human beings.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author mention problems with thinking ability? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention some fields AI chatbots can help? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the aspects making the 
three cities known? 
A. modern architecture 
B. multiculturalism 
C. developed economies 
D. major coastal areas
Question 2. The word “combine” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. integrate 
B. construct 
C. replace 
D. decide
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Urbanisation in Australia happens mostly in inland areas. 
B. Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane are small cities with little international influence. 
C. Australian cities aim to balance modern development with nature. 
D. Green spaces are often destroyed to make room for tall buildings.
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 4-4 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. A city often whispers of opportunities: the chance to study in universities that shape the future, or to walk into hospitals where the latest technology can save lives. Therefore, people have always been drawn to cities, not only because they are larger and brighter, but also because they seem to promise something more. For many families, these possibilities feel like doors opening to a better tomorrow. Work, too, pulls people into the urban rhythm. In cities, offices light up late into the night, factories hum with energy, and cafés are full of quiet conversations about new projects. In cities, jobs are not just about survival; they offer a path to ambition. The chance to earn more, to dream bigger, becomes part of everyday life. And beyond work and study, cities hold a special charm in their culture. A theatre performance, a street festival, a late-night stroll through neon markets—these moments make life richer. People from different places meet, share stories, and create communities that could never exist in isolation. In this way, cities become more than concrete and glass; they become living spaces where energy, diversity, and creativity thrive.
Question 4. The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______. 
A. cities 
B. people 
C. lives 
D. universities  
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]
Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“In cities, jobs are not just about survival; they offer a path to ambition.” 
A. City jobs mainly help people cover their daily living expenses. 
B. Work in cities provides opportunities for people to achieve higher goals. 
C. Employment in cities ensures survival without room for bigger aspirations. 
D. Jobs in cities ensure both living and the possibility to aim for more.
Question 6. The word “isolation” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. loneliness 
B. limitation 
C. separation 
D. contact
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. People move to cities only because they are larger and brighter. 
B. Cities provide opportunities for education, work, and cultural experiences. 
C. Jobs in cities are focused only on survival, not ambition. 
D. Urban life prevents people from building diverse communities.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Urbanisation is the shift of people from rural areas into cities, and with that movement comes both hope and challenge. Cities rise higher, roads stretch farther, and lights burn brighter, but behind these changes lie questions about how people will live together in limited space. For many, urbanisation has been a symbol of progress. In Australia, for example, cities such as Melbourne and Sydney have become centres of opportunity where education, healthcare, and employment are within reach. Families who once lived far from schools or hospitals now find themselves surrounded by services that can transform their lives. In this sense, cities are magnets, drawing people with the promise of higher living standards and more diverse experiences. Yet urbanisation also reveals its darker side. The same growth that fills skylines with glass towers can also create slums at their base. Dhaka, one of the most densely populated cities in the world, struggles daily with overcrowding, pollution, and shortages of housing. Traffic jams steal hours from workers, and rising living costs make survival difficult for the poor. Without balance, the dream of the city easily turns into frustration. That is why planning becomes so important. If governments invest in affordable housing, efficient transport, and green spaces, cities can grow without suffocating their people. Sustainable design, like Singapore’s vertical gardens or Copenhagen’s cycling culture, shows that urbanisation does not need to destroy quality of life. Instead, with thoughtful planning, it can create places where ambition and well- being walk hand in hand.
Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“Urbanisation is the shift of people from rural areas into cities, and with that movement comes both 
hope and challenge.” 
A. Urbanisation refers to people being forced to abandon city life and return permanently to the rural areas 
they once left behind. 
B. Urbanisation means that cities expand into rural lands and, in doing so, remove every problem that 
once existed there. 
C. Urbanisation is a force that provides only advantages for people and never creates any social or 
economic challenges. 
D. Urbanisation is the process of people moving from the countryside into cities, bringing both 
opportunities and difficulties.  

 

[Page 5]
Question 9. According to paragraph 2, living in cities means all of the following are within reach, 
EXCEPT _______. 
A. education 
B. entertainment 
C. healthcare 
D. employment
Question 10. The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. families  
B. schools  
C. hospitals 
D. services
Question 11. The word “reveals” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. alters 
B. releases 
C. hides 
D. shows
Question 12. The word “suffocating” in paragraph 4 is CLOSEST in meaning to ______. 
A. refines 
B. hampers 
C. stifles 
D. fosters
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Urbanisation guarantees a perfect life for everyone who moves to the city. 
B. Cities like Dhaka prove that rapid growth always results in comfort and prosperity. 
C. Services such as education or healthcare become unavailable in modern cities. 
D. Sustainable planning shows that cities can support both advancement and life quality.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author suggest some solutions? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention a waste of time due to traffic? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word/Phrase Pronunciation Meaning 1 anxious (adj) /ˈæŋk.ʃəs/ lo lắng 2 calm (v) /kɑːm/ làm giảm nhẹ, làm bình tĩnh 3 worry (v/n) /ˈwʌr.i/ lo lắng/ sự lo lắng 4 appearance (n) /əˈpɪə.rəns/ ngoại hình, diện mạo 5 relaxed (adj) /rɪˈlækst/ thư giãn 6 mind (n) /maɪnd/ tâm trí 7 harmless (adj) /ˈhɑːm.ləs/ vô hại 8 restless (adj) /ˈrest.ləs/ không ngủ được, bồn chồn 9 confuse (v) /kənˈfjuːz/ làm xáo trộn 10 remedy (n) /ˈrem.ə.di/ phương thuốc, cách chữa trị 11 symptom (n) /ˈsɪmp.təm/ triệu chứng 12 cure (v/n) /kjʊər/ chữa khỏi/ cách chữa 13 effective (adj) /ɪˈfek.tɪv/ hiệu quả 14 fat (n) /fæt/ chất béo 15 digestion (n) /daɪˈdʒes.tʃən/ sự tiêu hóa 16 consumption (n) /kənˈsʌmp.ʃən/ sự tiêu thụ 17 unhealthy (adj) /ʌnˈhel.θi/ không lành mạnh 18 exercise (v/n) /ˈek.sə.saɪz/ tập thể dục/ bài tập 19 muscle (n) /ˈmʌs.əl/ cơ bắp 20 mood (n) /muːd/ tâm trạng 21 workout (n) /ˈwɜː.kaʊt/ buổi tập luyện 22 mental (adj) /ˈmen.təl/ thuộc về tinh thần 23 body (n) /ˈbɒd.i/ cơ thể 24 avoid (v) /əˈvɔɪd/ tránh 25 take care of (phr) /teɪk keə əv/ chăm sóc 26 diet (n) /ˈdaɪ.ət/ chế độ ăn uống 27 ingredient (n) /ɪnˈɡriː.di.ənt/ thành phần 28 nutrient (n) /ˈnjuː.tri.ənt/ chất dinh dưỡng 29 recover (v) /rɪˈkʌv.ər/ hồi phục 30 life expectancy (n) /laɪf ɪkˈspek.tən.si/ tuổi thọ [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 30. 1. You might feel (1) _______ before an exam – your hands sweat, your stomach feels tight, and your mind jumps from thought to thought. This is anxiety. To (2) _______ it, try breathing in for four seconds, holding for four, and breathing out slowly. Preparing early, sleeping well, and sharing your (3) _______ with a friend can also make a real difference.
Question 1. A. anxious 
B. relaxed  
C. effective  
D. unhealthy
Question 2. A. exercise 
B. take 
C. confuse  
D. calm
Question 3. A. workouts 
B. nutrients 
C. worries  
D. muscles

vocab_context_group | câu: 1, 2, 3 | trang 1-2 | rule_high
[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word/ Phrase Pronunciation Meaning 1 lose weight (v) /luːz weɪt/ giảm cân 2 misunderstanding (n) /ˌmɪsʌndəˈstændɪŋ/ sự hiểu lầm 3 support (v) /səˈpɔːt/ ủng hộ, hỗ trợ 4 replace (v) /rɪˈpleɪs/ thay thế 5 combine (v) /kəmˈbaɪn/ kết hợp 6 whole grain (n) /həʊl ɡreɪn/ ngũ cốc nguyên hạt 7 sugary (adj) /ˈʃʊɡəri/ có đường, ngọt 8 control (v) /kənˈtrəʊl/ kiểm soát 9 overall (adj/adv) /ˌəʊvərˈɔːl/ tổng thể 10 immune system (n) /ɪˈmjuːn ˌsɪstəm/ hệ miễn dịch 11 deprivation (n) /ˌdeprɪˈveɪʃn/ sự thiếu hụt, mất mát 12 concentrate (v) /ˈkɒnsntreɪt/ tập trung 13 anxiety (n) /æŋˈzaɪəti/ sự lo lắng 14 depression (n) /dɪˈpreʃn/ trầm cảm 15 angry (adj) /ˈæŋɡri/ tức giận 16 upset (adj) /ʌpˈset/ buồn, khó chịu 17 check-up (n) /ˈtʃek ʌp/ cuộc kiểm tra sức khỏe 18 disease (n) /dɪˈziːz/ bệnh 19 protect (v) /prəˈtekt/ bảo vệ 20 improve (v) /ɪmˈpruːv/ cải thiện BẢNG CẤU TRÚC STT Cấu trúc Nghĩa 1 help somebody do something giúp ai đó làm việc gì 2 make a decision đưa ra quyết định 3 lead to something dẫn đến điều gì đó 4 take care of chăm sóc 5 talk to somebody nói chuyện với ai đó [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 3. Many people believe that exercise alone can help them lose weight, but that is a common misunderstanding. Exercise supports weight loss, but it cannot replace a healthy diet. Physical activities like walking, running, or swimming help burn calories, build muscles, and keep the body strong. However, without eating properly, exercise won't be very effective. For best results, people should combine regular workouts with a healthy eating plan. A balanced diet plays the most important role in weight loss. It means eating the right kinds of food in the right amounts. A good diet includes vegetables, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, and healthy fats. Avoiding fast food, sugary snacks, and soft drinks is also necessary. When people eat healthy and control their portions, their bodies receive the nutrients they need without extra calories. This helps reduce fat and improve overall health.
Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the benefits of physical 
activities? 
A. burn calories 
 
B. replace a healthy diet 
C. build muscles 
 
D. keep the body strong
Question 2. The word “supports” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. prevent 
B. assist 
C. respect 
D. improve
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Exercise is more important than eating healthy for losing weight. 
B. People can lose weight by exercising alone without changing their diet. 
C. A combination of regular exercise and a balanced diet is best for weight loss. 
D. Eating fast food in small amounts is encouraged for a healthy lifestyle.
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 3-3 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. Lack of sleep is a serious problem that affects both physical and mental health. When people don’t get enough rest, their bodies cannot recover properly. This can lead to headaches, tiredness, and a weakened immune system, making it easier to get sick. In addition to physical health, sleep deprivation also harms mental performance. It becomes harder to concentrate, remember things, and make good decisions. Students who do not sleep enough may find it difficult to study and perform poorly on exams. Over time, it can even lead to stress, anxiety, or depression. Lack of sleep causes many troubles in daily life. People who don’t sleep enough often feel tired and have no energy to do their tasks. They may forget important things or make mistakes at work or school. Sleepy people also get angry or upset more easily, which can lead to problems in relationships. In serious cases, lack of sleep can cause accidents, especially when driving or using machines. That is why getting enough rest every night is very important.
Question 4. The word “weakened” in paragraph 1 is opposite in meaning to _______. 
A. famous 
B. important 
C. crowded 
D. enhanced
Question 5. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the mental problems 
caused by a lack of sleep?  
A. stress 
B. depression 
C. tiredness 
D. anxiety 
 

 

[Page 3]
Question 6. The word “They” in paragraph 3 refers to _______. 
A. Many troubles  
B. People  
C. Tasks 
D. Important things
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Sleep deprivation improves students’ performance on exams. 
B. People who don’t sleep enough may have problems in their relationships. 
C. Lack of sleep makes the immune system stronger. 
D. Sleepy people are more likely to stay focused and make better decisions.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 4-4 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Being healthy does not only mean having a strong body but also having a clear and positive mind. Both physical and mental health are important, and they work together to help us live well, study better, and enjoy life. Good physical health means your body is working properly. To stay physically healthy, people should eat a balanced diet, drink enough water, get regular exercise, and sleep well. It is also important to have regular health check-ups. These habits help the body grow stronger, prevent diseases, and give us energy for daily activities. Mental health is about our thoughts, feelings, and how we handle stress. Good mental health means feeling calm, confident, and in control. People can protect their mental health by talking to others, relaxing, avoiding too much stress, and doing things they enjoy. Sometimes, even small things like listening to music or walking in nature can improve your mood. In conclusion, both physical and mental health are important. If you are physically strong but feel sad or stressed all the time, you cannot enjoy life. On the other hand, if your mind is happy but your body is weak or sick, you may not have the energy to do anything. So, we must take care of both to live a full and healthy life.
Question 8. The word “regular” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. huge 
B. hard 
C. easy 
D. rare
Question 9. The word “they” in paragraph 3 refers to ______. 
A. people  
B. others 
C. things  
D. feelings
Question 10. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the ways to protect 
mental health? 
A. relaxing 
 
B. talking to others 
C. avoiding too much stress  
D. working when you like
Question 11. The word “handle” in paragraph 3 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. lose 
B. address 
C. consider 
D. join
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
“Good physical health means your body is working properly.” 
A. A healthy mind can keep the body from getting tired. 
B. Having a healthy body means you are always happy and confident. 
C. Physical health depends only on how strong your muscles are. 
D. Being physically healthy means your body is functioning as it should.  
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. People with weak bodies cannot enjoy life even if they feel happy. 
B. Regular check-ups are not necessary for good physical or mental health. 
C. Physical health has no effect on how we study or enjoy daily life. 
D. Being mentally healthy means people never feel sad or overwhelmed again.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author describe what life is like without either physical or 
mental health? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author explain mental health?  
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

/ˈpræk.tɪ.kəl/ thực tiễn, thiết thực 3 occupation (n) /ˌɒk.jʊˈpeɪ.ʃən/ nghề nghiệp 4 hospitality (n) /ˌhɒs.pɪˈtæl.ə.ti/ ngành dịch vụ nhà hàng, khách sạn 5 graduate (n) /ˈɡrædʒ.u.ət/ người tốt nghiệp 6 economics (n) /ˌiː.kəˈnɒm.ɪks/ kinh tế học 7 engineering (n) /ˌen.dʒɪˈnɪə.rɪŋ/ ngành kỹ thuật 8 qualification (n) /ˌkwɒ.lɪ.fɪˈkeɪ.ʃən/ bằng cấp, trình độ 9 career planning (n) /kəˈrɪə ˈplæn.ɪŋ/ lập kế hoạch nghề nghiệp 10 attend (v) /əˈtend/ tham dự 11 pressure (n) /ˈpreʃ.ər/ áp lực 12 preparation (n) /ˌprep.ərˈeɪ.ʃən/ sự chuẩn bị 13 extra class (n) /ˈek.strə klɑːs/ lớp học thêm 14 cover (v) /ˈkʌv.ər/ bao gồm 15 combine (v) /kəmˈbaɪn/ kết hợp 16 fairness (n) /ˈfeə.nəs/ sự công bằng 17 standardisation (n) /ˌstæn.də.daɪˈzeɪ.ʃən/ sự tiêu chuẩn hóa 18 memorisation (n) /ˌmem.ə.raɪˈzeɪ.ʃən/ sự ghi nhớ, học thuộc 19 aptitude test (n) /ˈæp.tɪ.tʃuːd test/ bài kiểm tra năng lực 20 competition (n) /ˌkɒm.pəˈtɪʃ.ən/ sự cạnh tranh 21 expectation (n) /ˌek.spekˈteɪ.ʃən/ sự kỳ vọng 22 platform (n) /ˈplæt.fɔːm/ 23 at one’s own pace (phr) /ət wʌnz əʊn peɪs/ theo tốc độ riêng 24 flexible (adj) /ˈflek.sə.bəl/ linh hoạt 25 technical (adj) /ˈtek.nɪ.kəl/ thuộc về kỹ thuật 26 face-to-face (adj) /ˌfeɪs.təˈfeɪs/ trực tiếp, đối mặt 27 discussion (n) /dɪˈskʌʃ.ən/ cuộc thảo luận 28 interact (v) /ˌɪn.təˈrækt/ tương tác 29 feedback (n) /ˈfiːd.bæk/ phản hồi 30 communication (n) /kəˌmjuː.nɪˈkeɪ.ʃən/ sự giao tiếp 31 teamwork (n) /ˈtiːm.wɜːk/ làm việc nhóm 32 fixed (adj) /fɪkst/ cố định 33 resource (n) /ˈriː.sɔːs/ tài nguyên [Page 2] BẢNG CẤU TRÚC STT Cấu trúc Nghĩa 1 lead to dẫn đến, gây ra 2 be in high demand có nhu cầu cao, được săn đón nhiều 3 depend on phụ thuộc vào 4 present challenges đặt ra thách thức, gây ra khó khăn 5 hesitate to do something do dự, ngần ngại làm gì 6 speak up phát biểu ý kiến, lên tiếng PRACTICE Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 3. After high school, students can choose between vocational training and higher education. Vocational programs often emphasise practical skills related to...
Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the examples of specific 
occupations? 
A. hospitality  
B. beauty services 
C. humanities 
D. automotive repair
Question 2. The word “covers” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. includes 
B. widens 
C. approaches 
D. improves
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Vocational training always takes longer than university education. 
B. University graduates are more in demand than vocational graduates in all industries. 
C. Vocational programs often lead to faster employment. 
D. University education is cheap and suitable for everyone, regardless of career goals. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 3]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
In Vietnam, the university entrance exam plays an important role in the education system. Each 
year, almost all students take this exam after finishing Grade 12. It is often considered a major turning 
point, as the results decide which universities or colleges students can attend. The pressure surrounding 
the exam is high, with months of preparation, extra classes, and practice tests. 
The exam usually covers core subjects like Math, Literature, English, and sometimes Science or 
Social Studies, depending on the student’s chosen group. The format combines multiple-choice questions 
and written responses. The exam aims to ensure fairness and standardisation. However, some argue that 
it focuses too much on memorisation rather than critical thinking or creativity. 
Recently, some changes have been introduced to improve the process. Universities are allowed to 
use multiple admission methods, including high school GPA and aptitude tests. However, the traditional 
exam remains central for most students. The intense competition and high expectations continue to make 
it one of the most challenging experiences in Vietnamese student life.
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 4-4 | rule_high
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
Each year, almost all students take this exam after finishing Grade 12.  
A. Nearly all students sit the exam after completing Grade 12 every year. 
B. Students take the exam many times before they finish Grade 12. 
C. Only a small number of students take the exam after Grade 12. 
D. Students are required to take the exam to graduate from high school.
Question 5. The word it in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. fairness  
B. the exam  
C. standardisation 
D. memorisation
Question 6. The word challenging in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. dangerous 
 B. complex  
C. difficult 
D. easy
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. All students are accepted into universities without taking any exam. 
B. The entrance exam in Vietnam no longer plays a major role in university admission. 
C. The exam system has completely changed to focus on creativity. 
D. Many students experience pressure and spend months preparing for the exam. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
 
Online learning has become more popular in recent years, especially after the pandemic. It allows 
students to attend classes from anywhere, saving time and travel costs. Online platforms often provide 
recorded lessons, which help learners review at their own pace. This method also supports flexible 
schedules, which is useful for those with other commitments. 
 
However, online learning has several drawbacks. Students may find it harder to focus without a 
classroom setting. Technical issues, such as unstable internet or device problems, can interrupt lessons. 
In addition, a lack of face-to-face contact may reduce motivation and make it difficult to ask questions or 
join discussions actively. 
 
In contrast, traditional learning provides a structured environment. Teachers and students interact 
directly, which helps build relationships and encourages immediate feedback. Group work and in-class 
activities often improve communication and teamwork skills. For many learners, this setting creates a 
stronger sense of discipline and responsibility. 
 
Still, face-to-face learning also presents challenges. Fixed timetables may not suit students with 
part-time jobs or other duties. Long commutes can be tiring and reduce pers...

Question 8. The word “saving” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. reducing 
B. tracking 
C. slowing 
D. wasting
Question 9. The word “their” in paragraph 1 refers to ______. 
A. platforms  
B. learners  
C. travel costs 
D. recorded lessons
Question 10. The word “focus” in paragraph 2 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. access 
B. distract 
C. consider 
D. concentrate
Question 11. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the problems of online 
learning? 
A. technical issues 
 
B. difficulty focusing 
C. joining discussions actively 
D. a lack of face-to-face contact
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
“Fixed timetables may not suit students with part-time jobs or other duties.” 
A. Students working part-time or with other duties may find fixed schedules inconvenient. 
B. All students with family duties might prefer flexible schedules. 
C. Having a fixed timetable can make it difficult for students to manage their time. 
D. Students who have other responsibilities do not attend in-person classes.
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Online learning always provides more motivation than traditional learning. 
B. Students in face-to-face classes never experience stress or anxiety. 
C. Traditional learning offers direct interaction, which helps build relationships. 
D. Online learning eliminates the need for group work or discussions.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author mention improved soft skills? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention a rise in a learning type?  
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word Pronunciation Meaning 1 growing (adj) /ˈɡrəʊ.ɪŋ/ đang gia tăng 2 negative (adj) /ˈneɡ.ə.tɪv/ tiêu cực 3 mental health (n. phr) /ˈmen.təl helθ/ sức khỏe tinh thần 4 natural disaster (n. phr) /ˈnætʃ.ər.əl dɪˈzɑː.stər/ thảm họa thiên nhiên 5 life-threatening (adj) /ˈlaɪfˌθret.ən.ɪŋ/ đe dọa tính mạng 6 shelter (n) /ˈʃel.tər/ nơi trú ẩn 7 damage (n/v) /ˈdæm.ɪdʒ/ thiệt hại / làm hư hại 8 hunger (n) /ˈhʌŋ.ɡər/ nạn đói 9 nutrition (n) /njuːˈtrɪʃ.ən/ dinh dưỡng 10 racism (n) /ˈreɪ.sɪ.zəm/ phân biệt chủng tộc 11 treat (v) /triːt/ đối xử 12 discrimination (n) /dɪˌskrɪm.ɪˈneɪ.ʃən/ sự phân biệt đối xử 13 division (n) /dɪˈvɪʒ.ən/ sự chia rẽ 14 peer pressure (n. phr) /ˈpɪər ˌpreʃ.ər/ áp lực từ bạn bè đồng trang lứa 15 push (v) /pʊʃ/ thúc ép 16 resist (v) /rɪˈzɪst/ chống lại 17 accept (v) /əkˈsept/ chấp nhận 18 bully (v/n) /ˈbʊl.i/ bắt nạt / kẻ bắt nạt 19 body shaming (n. phr) /ˈbɒd.i ˌʃeɪ.mɪŋ/ chê bai ngoại hình 20 offensive (adj) /əˈfen.sɪv/ gây xúc phạm 21 awareness (n) /əˈweə.nəs/ nhận thức 22 face (v) /feɪs/ đối mặt 23 cyberbullying (n) /ˈsaɪ.bəˌbʊl.i.ɪŋ/ bắt nạt qua mạng 24 victim (n) /ˈvɪk.tɪm/ nạn nhân 25 affect (v) /əˈfekt/ ảnh hưởng 26 challenge (n) /ˈtʃæl.ɪndʒ/ thách thức 27 unemployment (n) /ˌʌn.ɪmˈplɔɪ.mənt/ sự thất nghiệp 28 crime rate (n. phr) /kraɪm reɪt/ tỷ lệ tội phạm 29 affordable (adj) /əˈfɔː.də.bəl/ có thể chi trả được 30 infrastructure (n) /ˈɪn.frəˌstrʌk.tʃər/ cơ sở hạ tầng [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 30. 1. Noise pollution is a(n) (1) _______ problem in many big cities. Constant traffic, loud music, and construction sounds can make it hard for people to enjoy life. Those living near airports or busy roads often suffer the most from these (2) _______ effects. Over time, this can seriously affect a person’s mental (3) _______, leading to stress, anxiety, and even depression.
Question 1.  A. growing 
B. affordable 
C. offensive 
D. life-threatening
Question 2.  A. offensive          B. negative 
C. growing 
D. affordable
Question 3.  A. racism  
B. victim  
C. health 
D. awareness

Over time, this unfair treatment can create deep (13) _______ in society and increase tension between different communities.

B. Racism 
C. Cyberbullying 
D. Unemployment
Question 11. A. affected 
B. treated 
C. faced 
D. damaged
Question 12. A. challenge 
B. discrimination 
C. infrastructure 
D. shelter
Question 13. A. victims 
B. challenges 
C. divisions 
D. shelters

5.

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương GLOSSARY No. Word Pronunciation Meaning 1 norm (n) /nɔːm/ chuẩn mực, quy tắc xã hội 2 taboo (n/ adj) /təˈbuː/ điều cấm kỵ; cấm kỵ 3 disrespectful (adj) /ˌdɪs.rɪˈspek.t.fəl/ thiếu tôn trọng 4 celebration (n) /ˌsel.əˈbreɪ.ʃən/ lễ kỷ niệm 5 ritual (n) /ˈrɪtʃ.u.əl/ nghi thức 6 religious (adj) /rɪˈlɪdʒ.əs/ thuộc về tôn giáo 7 connected (adj) /kəˈnek.tɪd/ có sự kết nối 8 multicultural (adj) /ˌmʌl.tiˈkʌl.tʃər.əl/ đa văn hóa 9 tradition (n) /trəˈdɪʃ.ən/ truyền thống 10 culture shock (n. phr) /ˈkʌl.tʃər ʃɒk/ sốc văn hóa 11 language barrier (n. phr) /ˈlæŋ.ɡwɪdʒ ˈbær.i.ər/ rào cản ngôn ngữ 12 overcome (v) /ˌəʊ.vəˈkʌm/ vượt qua 13 cultural identity (n. phr) /ˈkʌl.tʃər.əl aɪˈden.tə.ti/ bản sắc văn hóa 14 reflect (v) /rɪˈflekt/ phản ánh 15 value (n/v) /ˈvæl.juː/ giá trị; coi trọng, đề cao 16 ancestor (n) /ˈæn.ses.tər/ tổ tiên 17 rooted (adj) /ˈruː.tɪd/ bén rễ, ăn sâu 18 vary (v) /ˈveə.ri/ thay đổi, dao động 19 accepted (adj) /əkˈseptɪd/ được chấp nhận 20 impolite (adj) /ˌɪm.pəlˈaɪt/ bất lịch sự 21 expect (v) /ɪkˈspekt/ kỳ vọng, trông đợi 22 celebrate (v) /ˈsel.ə.breɪt/ ăn mừng, tổ chức 23 gather (v) /ˈɡæð.ər/ tụ họp 24 speciality (n) /ˌspeʃ.iˈæl.ə.ti/ đặc sản 25 costume (n) /ˈkɒs.tʃuːm/ trang phục 26 encounter (v) /ɪnˈkaʊn.tər/ gặp phải, đối mặt 27 embrace (v) /ɪmˈbreɪs/ đón nhận 28 international (adj) /ˌɪn.təˈnæʃ.ən.əl/ quốc tế 29 reject (v) /rɪˈdʒekt/ từ chối 30 misunderstanding (n) /ˌmɪs.ʌn.dəˈstæn.dɪŋ/ sự hiểu lầm [Page 2] PRACTICE Read the following texts and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct option that best fits each of the numbered blanks from 1 to 30. 1. Differences in how people communicate across cultures can be huge. For example, it's a(n) (1) _______ to make direct eye contact in the United States to show confidence. But in some Asian cultures, it’s a(n) (2) _______ to look directly at others' eyes. It's seen as (3) _______ or confrontational.
Question 1.  A. identity  
B. norm  
C. shock  
D. costume
Question 2.  A. ancestor 
B. barrier  
C. ritual  
D. taboo
Question 3.  A. disrespectful 
B. cultural  
C. multicultural 
D. international

cắt giảm phr. v /kʌt daʊn/ B1 reduce increase 16 packaging bao bì n /ˈpækɪdʒɪŋ/ B2 [Page 2] 17 lifespan tuổi thọ n /ˈlaɪfspæn/ C2 18 consumption sự tiêu dùng n /kənˈsʌmpʃn/ C1 use 19 natural resource tài nguyên thiên nhiên n. phr /ˈnætʃrəl rɪˈzɔːs/ B2 20 lessen làm giảm v /ˈlesn/ C1 reduce, diminish increase 21 container vật chứa, hộp đựng n /kənˈteɪnə(r)/ B2 22 extend kéo dài v /ɪkˈstend/ B2 prolong, lengthen 23 furniture đồ nội thất n /ˈfɜːnɪtʃə(r)/ A2 24 repurpose tái sử dụng cho mục đích khác v /ˌriːˈpɜːpəs/ C1 25 emission khí thải n /ɪˈmɪʃn/ C1 26 recycle tái chế v /ˌriːˈsaɪkl/ B1 27 reusable có thể tái sử dụng adj /ˌriːˈjuːzəbl/ B2 28 material vật liệu n /məˈtɪəriəl/ B2 29 raw material nguyên liệu thô n. phr /rɔː məˈtɪəriəl/ B2 30 agricultural thuộc nông nghiệp adj /ˌæɡrɪˈkʌltʃərəl/ B2 31 contribute đóng góp v /kənˈtrɪbjuːt/ B2 32 significantly một cách đáng kể adv /sɪɡˈnɪfɪkəntli/ B2 considerably slightly 33 large-scale quy mô lớn adj /ˌlɑːdʒ ˈskeɪl/ C1 small-scale 34 livestock gia súc n /ˈlaɪvstɒk/ B2 farm animals, cattle 35 digestion sự tiêu hóa n /daɪˈdʒestʃən/ C1 36 manure phân chuồng n /məˈnjʊə(r)/ C2 dung 37 grazing sự chăn thả n /ˈɡreɪzɪŋ/ C1...
Question 1. The word “rich” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by _______. 
A. important 
B. huge 
C. lucky 
D. abundant
Question 2. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the Japanese government's 
efforts towards addressing overfishing? 
A. seasonal bans 
 
B. fishing quotas 
 
C. global cooperation 
 
D. aquaculture promotion
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Japan's fish population has remained stable due to advanced fishing methods. 
B. Aquaculture has replaced traditional fishing across all of Japan. 
C. The Japanese government has done nothing to stop overfishing. 
D. Poor fishing practices have led to a sharp decline in bluefin tuna populations.
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. The first “R” in the 3Rs stands for Reduce, which means cutting down on the amount of waste we produce. This can be done by avoiding unnecessary packaging, choosing products with a longer lifespan, and limiting single-use items. Reducing consumption at the source not only saves natural resources but also lessens the burden on landfills and the environment. The second “R” is Reuse, which encourages people to use items multiple times before throwing them away. Reusable shopping bags, containers, and glass bottles are examples of how we can extend the life of everyday products. Even old clothes and furniture can be repurposed or donated. Reusing helps reduce demand for new goods and lowers production-related emissions. The third “R” refers to Recycle, which involves turning used materials into new products. Paper, plastic, metal, and glass can all be collected and recycled into reusable materials. Recycling conserves resources and reduces energy usage compared to making products from raw materials. However, it should be the last option after reducing and reusing.
Question 4. The word “lessens” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. values 
B. minimises  
C. declines 
D. increases
Question 5. The word “them” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. people  
B. items  
C. shopping bags 
D. multiple times  
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]
Question 6. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“Reusing helps reduce demand for new goods and lowers production-related emissions.” 
A. Reusing stops factories from making new products and reduces energy use. 
B. Using items more than once cuts demand and lowers emissions from production. 
C. Buying second-hand goods makes companies produce more and pollute less. 
D. Reusing things increases the number of goods made and waste produced.
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Recycling is more important than reducing and reusing in waste management. 
B. Reuse involves turning used materials into something entirely new again. 
C. Reducing waste helps save resources and ease pressure on the environment. 
D. The 3Rs promote buying more single-use products to help the economy.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 6-6 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. While many people focus on emissions from transport and industry, agricultural practices also contribute significantly to climate change. Farming, especially large-scale animal agriculture, plays a major role in global warming and the increase of greenhouse gases. It’s essential to know about this connection to make good decisions about food consumption. One key issue is that livestock farming produces large amounts of methane. It is a powerful greenhouse gas released through animal digestion and manure. In addition, deforestation for grazing land reduces the planet’s ability to absorb carbon dioxide. Fertilisers used in crop farming also emit nitrous oxide, another potent greenhouse gas. Together, these factors make agriculture a major contributor to rising global temperatures. To address the problem, some countries are promoting sustainable farming techniques. These methods include improving animal diets to reduce methane, using organic fertilisers, and rotating crops or growing trees alongside crops. New technologies are also being tested, such as special feed that helps animals release less methane and growing food in tall indoor buildings, known as vertical farms. Ultimately, individual choices matter. Reducing meat consumption – especially beef – can lower demand for resource-intensive livestock farming. Instead, choosing fish, seafood, or plant-based meals helps reduce one’s carbon footprint and supports a more climate-friendly food system.
Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“It’s essential to know about this connection to make good decisions about food consumption.” 
A. Making food choices without knowledge can lead to poor long-term decisions. 
B. Understanding nutrition is vital to eating the right amount of food each day. 
C. Knowing the link helps people make smarter choices about what they eat. 
D. People should always base their diet only on personal taste and convenience.
Question 9. The word “produces” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to_______. 
A. captures 
B. digests 
C. consumes 
D. generates
Question 10. The word “It” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. Digestion  
B. Key issue  
C. Methane 
D. Livestock farming
Question 11. The word “release” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. capture 
B. create 
C. discharge 
D. refuse 
 
 

 

[Page 6]
Question 12. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the sustainable farming 
techniques? 
A. using organic fertilisers  
B. rotating crops  
C. improving animal diets   
D. growing trees instead of crops
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Farming has little impact on greenhouse gas levels or global temperature rise. 
B. All countries now rely on vertical farming instead of traditional agriculture. 
C. Livestock and fertilisers contribute significantly to harmful greenhouse gases. 
D. Eating more beef is encouraged to support modern farming and technology.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author mention solutions from nations? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author encourage the consumption of alternative foods? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

/ˈkɒm.pɒst/ phân hữu cơ; ủ phân 4 harness (v) /ˈhɑː.nəs/ khai thác 5 generate (v) /ˈdʒen.ər.eɪt/ tạo ra 6 reliance (n) /rɪˈlaɪ.əns/ sự phụ thuộc 7 carbon neutral (adj) /ˌkɑː.bən ˈnjuː.trəl/ trung hòa carbon 8 adopt (v) /əˈdɒpt/ áp dụng 9 renewable (adj) /rɪˈnjuː.ə.bəl/ tái tạo được 10 fossil fuel (n) /ˈfɒs.əl ˌfjʊəl/ nhiên liệu hóa thạch 11 release (v) /rɪˈliːs/ thải ra 12 greenhouse gas (n. phr) /ˈɡriːn.haʊs ɡæs/ khí nhà kính 13 atmosphere (n) /ˈæt.mə.sfɪə(r)/ khí quyển 14 single-use (adj) /ˌsɪŋ.ɡəlˈjuːs/ dùng một lần 15 decompose (v) /ˌdiː.kəmˈpəʊz/ phân hủy 16 contaminate (v) /kənˈtæm.ɪ.neɪt/ làm ô nhiễm 17 reuse (v) /ˌriːˈjuːz/ tái sử dụng 18 disruption (n) /dɪsˈrʌp.ʃən/ sự gián đoạn 19 extreme weather (n. phr) /ɪkˌstriːm ˈweð.ə(r)/ thời tiết cực đoan 20 vulnerable (adj) /ˈvʌl.nər.ə.bəl/ dễ bị tổn thương 21 temperature (n) /ˈtem.prə.tʃə(r)/ nhiệt độ 22 abundant (adj) /əˈbʌn.dənt/ dồi dào, phong phú 23 deplete (v) /dɪˈpliː.t/ làm cạn kiệt 24 threaten (v) /ˈθret.ən/ đe dọa 25 sustainable (adj) /səˈsteɪ.nə.bəl/ bền vững 26 global warming (n. phr) /ˌɡləʊ.bəl ˈwɔː.mɪŋ/ sự nóng lên toàn cầu 27 forest fire (n. phr) /ˈfɒr.ɪst ˌfaɪə(r)/ cháy rừng 28 glacier (n) /ˈɡlæs.i.ə(r)/ sông băng 29 sea level (n. phr) /ˈsiː ˌlev.əl/ mực nước biển 30 carbon footprint (n. phr) /ˌkɑː.bən ˈfʊt.prɪnt/ lượng khí CO₂ thải ra [Page 2] EXTENDED VOCABULARY No. Word/ Phrase [Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 entertainment sự giải trí n /ˌentəˈteɪnmənt/ B1 amusement, recreation 2 opinion ý kiến, quan điểm n /əˈpɪnjən/ A2 view, belief fact 3 visual thuộc thị giác, trực quan adj /ˈvɪʒuəl/ B2 optical 4 powerful mạnh mẽ, có sức ảnh hưởng adj /ˈpaʊəfl/ B1 influential weak 5 transform biến đổi v /trænsˈfɔːm/ B2 change, convert 6 social network mạng xã hội n.phr /ˈsəʊʃl ˈnetwɜːk/ A2 7 instantly ngay lập tức adv /ˈɪnstəntli/ B2 immediately slowly, later 8 honest trung thực adj /ˈɒnɪst/ A2 truthful, sincere dishonest 9 reporting việc đưa tin n /rɪˈpɔːtɪŋ/ B2 10 far-reaching có ảnh hưởng sâu rộng adj /ˌfɑː ˈriːtʃɪŋ/ C1 extensive limited, insignificant 11 journalism ngành báo chí n /ˈdʒɜːnəlɪzəm/ B2 12 lie lời nói dối (n); nói dối (v) n/v /laɪ/ A2 falsehood, deceit truth 13 reputation danh tiếng n /ˌrepjuˈteɪʃn/ B2 disrepute 14 distinguish phân biệt v /dɪˈstɪŋɡwɪʃ/ B2 differentiate 15 fiction hư cấu, tiểu thuyết n /ˈfɪkʃn/ A2 non-fiction 16 instability sự bất ổn n /ˌɪnstəˈbɪləti/ C1 stability 17 unproven chưa được chứng minh adj /ˌʌnˈpruːvn/ C1 unverified proven 18 cure phương thuốc / chữa trị n/v /kjʊə(r)/ B2 remedy, treatment [Page 2] 19 long-lasting lâu dài, bền vững adj /ˌlɒŋ ˈlɑːstɪŋ/ B2 enduring, permanent short-lived 20 verify xác minh v /ˈverɪfaɪ/ C1 confirm, validate falsify 21 trustworthy đáng tin cậy adj /ˈtrʌstwɜːði/ C1 reliable, dependable untrustworthy 22 misinformation thông tin sai lệch n /ˌmɪsɪnfəˈmeɪʃn/ B2 falsehood truth BẢNG CẤU TRÚC STT Cấu trúc Nghĩa 1 allow somebody to do something cho phép ai làm gì 2 distract somebody from something làm ai sao nhãng khỏi điều gì 3 help somebody do something giúp ai làm gì 4 keep in touch giữ liên lạc 5 rely on dựa vào, tin cậy vào 6 turn something into something biến cái gì thành cái gì 7 wait for chờ đợi [Page 3] PRACTICE Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 3. Social media offers young people countless chances to connect with others and expand their knowle...
Question 1. According to paragraph 1, social media enables access to all of the following, EXCEPT 
_______. 
A. tutorials  
B. news 
C. online courses 
D. educational videos
Question 2. The word “promoting” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. allowing 
B. encouraging 
C. replacing 
D. advertising
Question 3. Which of the following is NOT true according to the passage? 
A. Social media helps young people connect with friends and communities. 
B. Social media distracts students from their studies but still offers learning benefits. 
C. Young people can showcase creativity and gain feedback through social media. 
D. Some users may find career opportunities by promoting their talents online.
vocab_context_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 4-4 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 4 to 7. In the past, mass media were mainly limited to newspapers, magazines, and radio. People waited for the morning paper to read the latest news, while radio programs entertained families and informed them about national events. Although slow compared to today’s standards, these media were trusted and respected. Later, television became the most influential type of mass media. With moving images and sound, it brought the world into people’s living rooms. It provided not only news but also entertainment, education, and advertisements. For several decades, television shaped people’s opinions, habits, and even fashion. This period marked a turning point when mass media became more visual and powerful. Today, the rise of the internet and smartphones has transformed mass media once again. Online newspapers, social networks, and streaming services allow information to travel instantly. Unlike traditional media, digital platforms are interactive, meaning users can comment, share, and create their own content. While convenient and fast, modern mass media also present challenges such as fake news and information overload.
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“Although slow compared to today’s standards, these media were trusted and respected.” 
A. Old forms of mass media delivered information quickly but were unreliable. 
B. These media were slower than today’s, but considered reliable and credible. 
C. Modern mass media are faster and more trustworthy than these forms of media. 
D. Newspapers and radio spread news instantly and earned little public respect. 

 

[Page 4]
Question 5. The word “it” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. sound 
B. television  
C. type  
D. mass media
Question 6. The word “rise” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. purpose 
B. limit 
C. trend 
D. drop
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. In the past, people received news instantly through newspapers and the radio. 
B. Television had little effect on people’s opinions, habits, or fashion. 
C. Digital platforms allow users to comment, share, and create their own content. 
D. Modern mass media are slower and less interactive than traditional media.
vocab_context_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 8 to 15. Fake news refers to false or misleading information that is created and shared as if it were true. Unlike honest mistakes in reporting, fake news is often produced on purpose to attract attention, influence opinions, or earn money through advertising. With the rapid growth of social media, people can now access, believe, and spread such stories more easily than ever before. The impact of fake news is serious and far-reaching. It confuses readers, damages trust in journalism, and sometimes creates fear or anger in society. Fake news can also harm individuals or organisations by spreading lies that destroy reputations. Furthermore, when people cannot distinguish truth from fiction, their personal choices and social decisions become less reliable, leading to instability. During elections, fake news is often used to influence voters. For instance, certain online pages may publish exaggerated or completely false claims about a candidate’s background or policy. Another common case is health-related fake news, such as unproven cures for serious diseases or dangerous diets. Many people believe and share these stories without fact-checking, and the consequences can be both harmful and long-lasting. To reduce fake news, individuals should practice critical thinking and verify information before sharing it with others. Schools should teach students media literacy so they learn to question online content. Above all, people must rely on trustworthy sources and support reliable journalism to protect society from misinformation.
Question 8. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the purposes of creating 
fake news? 
A. deceive people 
B. attract attention 
C. earn money 
D. affect opinions
Question 9. The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. reputations 
B. lies  
C. people  
D. personal choices
Question 10. The word “confuses” in paragraph 2 can be best replaced by ______. 
A. reduces 
B. simplifies 
C. bewilders 
D. fosters
Question 11. The word “publish” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. announce 
B. report 
C. launch 
D. conceal
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
“Schools should teach students media literacy so they learn to question online content.” 
A. Students should be discouraged from checking information they find online. 
B. Media literacy classes make students believe all online content without doubt. 
C. Schools must prevent students from reading news and articles on the internet. 
D. Students should be taught media literacy at schools to evaluate online information.  
 

 

[Page 5]
Question 13. Which of the following is NOT true according to the passage? 
A. Fake news is sometimes created intentionally to influence public opinion or earn money. 
B. The spread of fake news can damage reputations and cause fear in society. 
C. Fake news during elections may include false claims about candidates. 
D. Fake news is an honest mistake made by professional journalists.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author compare fake news to reporting mistakes? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention health-related problems caused by fake news? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

/dəʊˈneɪʃn/ B2 2 positive tích cực adj /ˈpɒzətɪv/ B1 negative 3 charity tổ chức từ thiện n /ˈtʃærəti/ B1 4 experience trải nghiệm v /ɪkˈspɪəriəns/ B1 undergo 5 different khác nhau adj /ˈdɪfrənt/ A1 dissimilar similar 6 respect sự tôn trọng / tôn trọng n/v /rɪˈspekt/ B1 disrespect 7 unsafe không an toàn adj /ˌʌnˈseɪf/ C1 safe dangerous, risky, hazardous, perilous 8 ensure đảm bảo v /ɪnˈʃʊə(r)/ B2 guarantee, secure 9 stability sự ổn định n /stəˈbɪləti/ C1 steadiness instability 10 non-profit phi lợi nhuận adj /ˌnɒn ˈprɒfɪt/ B2 not-for-profit profit-making 11 establish thành lập v /ɪˈstæblɪʃ/ B2 found, set up 12 homeless vô gia cư adj /ˈhəʊmləs/ C1 13 participate tham gia v /pɑːˈtɪsɪpeɪt/ B2 join, engage, take part [Page 2] 14 limited hạn chế adj /ˈlɪmɪtɪd/ B1 restricted unlimited, abundant 15 knowledge kiến thức n /ˈnɒlɪdʒ/ B1 16 exchange sự trao đổi/ trao đổi n/v /ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ/ B1 17 approach cách tiếp cận, phương pháp/ tiếp cận n/v /əˈprəʊtʃ/ B2 18 launch phát động, khởi xướng, ra mắt 22 well-being sự khỏe mạnh, hạnh phúc n /ˈwel biːɪŋ/ B2 23 assistance sự giúp đỡ n /əˈsɪstəns/ B2 aid, support hindrance 24 program chương trình n /ˈprəʊɡræm/ A2 25 fight đấu tranh v /faɪt/ B2 combat 26 privilege đặc quyền n /ˈprɪvəlɪdʒ/ C1 27 injustice sự bất công n /ɪnˈdʒʌstɪs/ C1 justice 28 equality sự bình đẳng n /iˈkwɒləti/ B2 inequality 29 division sự chia rẽ n /dɪˈvɪʒn/ B2 unity [Page 3] books and simple reading corners. These free libraries quickly become centres of learning, where children 
can read stories, (16) _______ ideas, and develop their imagination. Such efforts are changing how young 
people in the countryside (17) _______ education.
