Question 1. The word “influential” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. generous 
B. positive 
C. productive 
D. impactful
Question 2. The word “their” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. farmers  
 
B. tools 
 
C. seeds 
 
D. developing countries
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation only provides medicine to cure diseases. 
B. Education is also part of the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation’s mission. 
C. Farmers in developing countries are given food by the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. 
D. Scholarships from the foundation are limited to students in rich nations. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
In many societies, men and women have the same potential but are not always treated equally in the 
workplace. Women may face barriers such as lower pay, fewer chances of promotion, or stereotypes about 
their abilities. Men can also be pressured by traditional roles that limit their choices. Equal job 
opportunities mean removing these obstacles so that everyone can develop their skills and talents without 
discrimination. 
The benefits of equal opportunities are seen in both economic growth and social progress. When 
women have the chance to lead, companies gain new perspectives and innovative solutions. When men 
are allowed to share household duties, women can pursue careers more freely. Communities become 
stronger when all people can contribute according to their abilities, rather than being limited by outdated 
expectations. 
Governments and organisations play an important role in creating this fairness. Laws that protect 
against gender bias in hiring, salaries, and promotion are essential. Schools and universities prepare young 
people by teaching them about gender equality, so future employees and employers can approach work 
with open minds.
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“Men can also be pressured by traditional roles that limit their choices.” 
A. Traditional expectations may restrict men’s freedom to choose from different life options. 
B. Men are likely to face pressure from conventional roles that reduce their opportunities. 
C. Family duties never allow men to explore wider opportunities without social limitations. 
D. Stereotypes usually give men unlimited choices and greater freedom in their careers.
Question 5. The word “outdated” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. ancient 
 B. well-known 
C. ordinary 
D. modern
Question 6. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the aspects that can be 
biased based on gender? 
A. promotion  
B. teaching 
C. recruitment 
D. salaries
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Without government support, achieving gender fairness in workplaces is impossible. 
B. Women leaders give companies new perspectives and provide creative solutions. 
C. Only women benefit from equal job opportunities, while men remain unaffected. 
D. Laws on pay and promotion are unnecessary as gender bias is rare today. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
Small acts of kindness can often make a big difference in someone’s life. The idea of “Pay It 
Forward” encourages people to help others without expecting anything in return. Instead, the person 
who receives kindness is inspired to pass it on to someone else. This simple chain reaction has the power 
to create positive energy in families, schools, and even entire communities. 
Many campaigns have been launched to spread this spirit. For example, some restaurants invite 
customers to buy an extra meal for a stranger in need. In schools, students might share stationery, tutor a 
classmate, or simply offer kind words of encouragement. 
Social media has also played a role in expanding the reach of “Pay It Forward.” Stories and videos 
of kind acts inspire millions of people to join the movement. Seeing how one person’s generosity can lift 
another person’s spirit motivates others to take similar action. The campaigns remind society that kindness 
is contagious when people choose to care. 
Beyond individual actions, organisations and companies use these campaigns to strengthen 
connections with communities. Employees may volunteer their time, donate supplies, or organise charity 
drives. These activities not onl...
standalone_mcq | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang - | rule_high
Question 8. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“The idea of “Pay It Forward” encourages people to help others without expecting anything in 
return.” 
A. “Pay It Forward” teaches that kindness should always bring rewards to the giver. 
B. According to “Pay It Forward,” kindness must be exchanged equally between people. 
C. “Pay It Forward” advises communities not to help strangers in daily situations. 
D. Through “Pay It Forward,” people are urged to help others without expecting rewards.
Question 9. The word “spread” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. reach 
B. suppress 
C. extend 
D. pursue
Question 10. The word “their” in paragraph 4 refers to ______. 
A. connections  
B. communities 
C. organisations  
D. employees
Question 11. The word “shared” in paragraph 4 is CLOSEST in meaning to ______. 
A. curious 
B. purposeful 
C. vital 
D. mutual
Question 12. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as one of the places where "Pay 
It Forward" can take place? 
A. restaurants  
B. companies  
C. stations 
D. social media
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Social media spreads “Pay It Forward” by inspiring others through stories and videos.  
B. “Pay It Forward” discourages people from helping strangers in their communities. 
C. Companies supporting “Pay It Forward” rarely build stronger relationships with employees. 
D. Campaigns of “Pay It Forward” only focus on giving money instead of volunteering time.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore the spread of "Pay It Forward" online? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author explain "Pay It Forward"? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

sincerity dishonesty 33 kindness lòng tốt, sự tử tế n /ˈkaɪndnəs/ B1 34 integrity tính chính trực n /ɪnˈteɡrə...

With creativity and (13) _______, Jobs launched products like the iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad, which revolutionised technology and transformed daily life.

[Page 5]

 

 
5. Mahatma Gandhi became the leader of India’s independence movement through his (14) _______ in 
nonviolent resistance. He organised peaceful marches, boycotts of British goods, and (15) _______ 
encouraging Indians to make their own salt and cloth instead of buying from the colonisers. Gandhi (16) 
_______ millions of ordinary people to join protests without using weapons or violence. His methods 
showed that (17) _______ and unity could be stronger than force. In 1947, after years of struggle, India 
finally gained independence from British rule.
Question 14. A. belief  
B. education 
C. difficulty 
D. experiment
Question 15. A. campaigns  
B. difficulties 
C. experiments 
D. beliefs
Question 16. A. inspired  
B. dedicated 
C. respected 
D. founded
Question 17. A. curiosity 
B. meaning 
C. campaign 
D. determination
cloze_group | câu: 18, 19, 20, 21 | trang - | rule_high
6. In the face of danger, doctors and nurses during the COVID-19 pandemic were still (18) _______, compassionate, and responsible. They worked long hours in crowded hospitals to (19) _______ lives, often wearing heavy protective suits that made breathing and moving difficult. Many risked their own health while caring for patients struggling to (20) _______. Some even stayed away from their families to prevent spreading the virus at home. Their dedication, (21) _______, and kindness brought hope and comfort to communities in the darkest times.
Question 18. A. lasting 
B. important 
C. unusual 
D. courageous
Question 19. A. save 
B. face 
C. survive 
D. found
Question 20. A. respect 
B. survive 
C. dedicate 
D. motivate
Question 21. A. curiosity 
B. experiment 
C. difficulty 
D. sacrifice

7. Mother Teresa moved to Kolkata in 1948 and (22) _______ herself to serving the poorest communities. She opened schools for children in slums and offered basic care to those without hospitals. In 1950, she (23) _______ the Missionaries of Charity, focusing on the sick, hungry, and dying. With scarce resources, she and her sisters (24) _______ lepers, abandoned children, and the rejected. Her (25) _______ soon drew international attention, bringing volunteers and donations from many nations to support her mission.
Question 22. A. dedicated  
B. inspired 
C. motivated 
D. faced

B. struggled

B. aided

C. meaning 
D. education

8. Albert Einstein had many (26) _______ in his early school years, as his teachers thought he was slow and unable to learn like other students. He spoke late as a child, and his (27) _______ way of thinking made him seem different from his classmates. However, instead of giving up, Einstein continued to question everything around him and developed a strong (28) _______ about mathematics and physics. He spent long hours reading scientific books, solving problems, and (29) _______ with ideas that went beyond what was taught in school. His perseverance eventually helped him (30) _______ the negative opinions, and he went on to publish groundbreaking theories, including the theory of relativity.

B. campaigns 
C. experiments 
D. difficulties

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 ambitious đầy tham vọng adj /æmˈbɪʃəs/ B1 unambitious 2 commitment sự cam kết, tận tụy n /kəˈmɪtmənt/ B2 dedication, devotion apathy, indifference 3 courage lòng can đảm n /ˈkʌrɪdʒ/ B2 bravery, fearlessness cowardice 4 criticism sự phê bình, chỉ trích n /ˈkrɪtɪsɪzəm/ B2 censure praise, approval 5 curiosity sự tò mò n /ˌkjʊəriˈɒsəti/ C1 inquisitiveness apathy 6 determination sự quyết tâm n /dɪˌtɜːmɪˈneɪʃn/ B2 resolve, willpower indecision 7 devotion sự tận tâm, hết lòng n /dɪˈvəʊʃn/ C1 dedication, commitment disloyalty, apathy 8 dismantle tháo dỡ, phá bỏ v /dɪsˈmæntl/ C1 assemble 9 endurance sức chịu đựng n /ɪnˈdjʊərəns/ C1 stamina, resilience 10 experimentation sự thử nghiệm n /ɪkˌsperɪmenˈteɪʃn/ C1 testing, trial 11 filament dây tóc bóng đèn n /ˈfɪləmənt/ C1 12 ground-breaking đột phá, tiên phong adj /ˈɡraʊndˌbreɪkɪŋ/ C1 innovative 13 immense mênh mông, to lớn adj /ɪˈmens/ C1 huge, vast, enormous tiny, small 14 inequality sự bất bình đẳng n /ˌɪnɪˈkwɒləti/ C1 disparity, unfairness equality, fairness 15 inventor nhà phát minh n /ɪnˈventə(r)/ B1 16 limitless vô hạn, không giới hạn adj /ˈlɪmɪtləs/ C1 infinite, boundless limited, finite [Page 2] 17 persistence sự kiên trì n /pəˈsɪstəns/ B2 perseverance laziness 18 possibility khả năng, cơ hội n /ˌpɒsəˈbɪləti/ A2 chance, opportunity impossibility 19 presidency chức tổng thống, nhiệm kỳ tổng thống n /ˈprezɪdənsi/ C1 20 progress sự tiến bộ n /ˈprəʊɡres/ A2 advancement, development regression 21 phonograph máy hát (máy ghi âm cổ) n /ˈfəʊnəɡrɑːf/ C1 record player 22 radioactive phóng xạ adj /ˌreɪdiəʊˈæktɪv/ C1 23 reflect phản ánh, phản chiếu v /rɪˈflekt/ B1 show, indicate obscure 24 reflection sự phản chiếu; sự suy ngẫm n /rɪˈflekʃn/ C1 25 relentless không ngừng nghỉ adj /rɪˈlentləs/ C1 unrelenting 26 rigor rigour tính khắt khe; sự nghiêm ngặt n /ˈrɪɡə(r)/ C1 strictness, severity laxity, looseness 27 uncertainty sự không chắc chắn n /ʌnˈsɜːtənti/ B2 certainty 28 uplift nâng cao, thúc đẩy v /ˈʌplɪft/ C1 degrade, lower 29 vision...
Question 1. The word “ground-breaking” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. significant 
B. optimistic 
C. innovative 
D. impactful
Question 2. According to paragraph 2, Tesla's ideas spark discussions about all of the following, 
EXCEPT ________. 
A. green tech 
 
B. wireless devices 
 
C. cordless charging 
 
D. sustainable energy
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Tesla focused mainly on making profits from his inventions and ideas. 
B. Most of Tesla’s projects were completed successfully during his lifetime. 
C. Tesla’s vision of clean energy still inspires modern science and technology. 
D. Tesla believed that innovation should be limited rather than dreaming too high. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
Born in 1847 in Ohio, Thomas Edison showed curiosity from a young age. He often dismantled 
household items to see how they worked. His interest in mechanics and electricity grew stronger when he 
worked as a telegraph operator, a job that exposed him to the practical use of electrical signals and sparked 
ideas for his future inventions. 
In his laboratory in Menlo Park, Edison gathered a team of skilled assistants and devoted himself to 
experiments. He pursued improvements in the telegraph and telephone before moving on to the 
electric light. The challenge of finding the right filament for the light bulb required extraordinary 
persistence. Reports say he tested thousands of materials – cotton, bamboo, and even hair – before 
discovering a carbonised filament that glowed steadily without burning out too quickly. 
His determination extended to other fields as well. Edison developed the phonograph, allowing 
sound to be recorded and played back for the first time. He also worked on motion picture cameras, 
helping to open a new era of entertainment. By the end of his life, he held more than a thousand patents. 
Each of them was the result of countless trials and an unshaken commitment to experimentati...
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. The word “dismantled” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. constructed 
 B. opened 
C. destroyed 
D. assembled
Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“He pursued improvements in the telegraph and telephone before moving on to the electric light.” 
A. He first worked to invent the telegraph and telephone, then turned to the light bulb. 
B. Before focusing on the electric light, he tried to enhance the telegraph and telephone. 
C. He concentrated on the light bulb, ignoring devices like the telegraph and telephone. 
D. He improved the telegraph and telephone after he finished creating the electric light.
Question 6. The word “them” in paragraph 3 refers to _______. 
A. patents 
B. cameras 
 C. other fields  
D. countless trials
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. More than a thousand patents were credited to Edison by the end of his life. 
B. The first successful phonograph was created long before Edison began experimenting. 
C. Edison found the best light bulb filament after testing only a few common materials. 
D. Motion picture technology developed without any contribution from Edison’s research. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
History offers figures whose lives carry lessons of endurance and vision, and from them we can 
draw deep inspiration. The point is not to idealise them but to examine how their decisions and sacrifices 
in difficult times created possibilities for future generations. 
Abraham Lincoln stands as one such figure. Rising from poverty to the presidency, he faced the 
moral and political crisis of slavery in America. His choice to preserve the Union while issuing the 
Emancipation Proclamation required immense courage. What inspires us is not merely that he signed a 
document, but that he held steady through years of war, criticism, and personal loss. 
Another striking example is Marie Curie. At a time when women were excluded from most scientific 
circles, she worked tirelessly in small laboratories, isolating radium and polonium. The physical toll of 
handling radioactive materials and the scepticism of her peers did not deter her. Her double Nobel 
Prizes testify not just to scientific discovery but to relentless devotion. Inspiration comes from imagining 
the cold nights in her lab, the uncertainty of results, and her decision to continue regardless. 
To take inspiration seriously me...
standalone_mcq | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang - | rule_high
Question 8. The word “them” in paragraph 1 refers to ______. 
A. figures  
 
B. lives  
 
C. lessons  
 
D. endurance and vision
Question 9. The word “possibilities” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to ______. 
A. candidates 
B. purposes 
C. opportunities 
D. abilities
Question 10. The word “immense” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. approachable 
B. respective 
C. extensive 
D. inconsiderable
Question 11. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
“The physical toll of handling radioactive materials and the scepticism of her peers did not deter 
her.” 
A. She was discouraged by health risks and constant scepticism from her colleagues. 
B. She kept working despite harmful radiation and doubts expressed by fellow scientists. 
C. She abandoned her studies as radioactive experiments caused serious health problems. 
D. Despite public criticism and physical weakness, she still pursued her research.
Question 12. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 4 as one of the qualities we should 
consider? 
A. moral courage 
B. intellectual rigour 
C. sacrifice 
D. reflection
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Lincoln showed courage by preserving the Union and freeing the slaves. 
B. Curie abandoned science when her colleagues doubted her abilities. 
C. We should idealise figures without noting their struggles or sacrifices. 
D. Inspiration comes mainly from names and titles, not human qualities.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author imply gender inequality? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author highlight how inspiration can help with personal 
challenges? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 organize/ organise tổ chức, sắp xếp v /ˈɔːɡənaɪz/ B1 2 careful cẩn thận, kỹ lưỡng adj /ˈkeəfl/ A2 cautious, meticulous careless, reckless 3 individual cá nhân, riêng lẻ n/adj /ˌɪndɪˈvɪdʒuəl/ A2 person; personal group, collective 4 decide quyết định, giải quyết v /dɪˈsaɪd/ A1 determine hesitate, waver 5 maturity sự trưởng thành, chín chắn n /məˈtʃʊərəti/ /məˈtjʊərəti/ B2 adulthood immaturity 6 independent độc lập, tự chủ adj /ˌɪndɪˈpendənt/ A2 self-reliant, autonomous dependent 7 responsible có trách nhiệm, đáng tin cậy adj /rɪˈspɒnsəbl/ B1 irresponsible 8 personal cá nhân, riêng tư adj /ˈpɜːsənl/ A1 private, individual public, general 9 self-study tự học n /self ˈstʌdi/ C1 10 struggle đấu tranh, vật lộn; khó khăn v/n /ˈstrʌɡl/ B2 strive, contend; difficulty 11 support hỗ trợ; sự hỗ trợ, ủng hộ v/n /səˈpɔːt/ A2 back, aid, help oppose, hinder 12 motivation động lực n /ˌməʊtɪˈveɪʃn/ B2 incentive, drive [Page 2] 13 guidance sự hướng dẫn n /ˈɡaɪdns/ C1 direction, instruction 14 manage quản lý, xoay xở v /ˈmænɪdʒ/ B handle mismanage 15 handle xử lý, giải quyết v /ˈhændl/ B2 deal with mishandle 16 situation tình huống, hoàn cảnh n /ˌsɪtʃuˈeɪʃn/ A1 circumstance 17 prepare chuẩn bị v /prɪˈpeə(r)/ A1 get ready 18 healthy lành mạnh, khỏe mạnh adj /ˈhelθi/ A1 unhealthy 19 control kiểm soát; sự kiểm soát v/n /kənˈtrəʊl/ A2 20 basic cơ bản, nền tảng adj /ˈbeɪsɪk/ B1 fundamental advanced, complex 21 part-time bán thời gian adj/adv /ˌpɑːt ˈtaɪm/ B1 22 rent thuê nhà; tiền thuê nhà v/n /rent/ B1 lease, hire 23 confidence sự tự tin, lòng tin n /ˈkɒnfɪdəns/ B2 24 balance (sự) cân bằng n/v /ˈbæləns/ B1 equilibrium imbalance 25 life skill kỹ năng sống n /laɪf skɪl/ B2 26 budget ngân sách; dự trù n/v /ˈbʌdʒɪt/ B1 27 save tiết kiệm v /seɪv/ A2 put aside spend, waste 28 responsibility trách nhiệm n /rɪˌspɒnsəˈbɪləti/ B1 duty, obligation 29 overwhelmed adj /ˌəʊvəˈwelmd/ B2 choáng ngợp, bị áp đảo (thường bởi cảm xúc, công việc, trách nhiệm) 30 independently một cách độc lập ...
Question 1.  A. rent 
B. struggle 
C. organize  
D. save
Question 2.  A. careful  
B. personal 
C. healthy 
D. overwhelmed
Question 3.  A. budget 
B. individual  
C. motivation 
D. balance

2. A young person living independently often shows clear differences from peers who still (4) _______ on family. They wake up early to prepare breakfast, pay their own bills, and (5) _______ how to use money carefully. Weekends may be spent cooking, cleaning, or working part-time instead of lying in bed all day. These daily habits gradually shape (6) _______ and make independence visible in their actions.
Question 4.  A. decide 
B. rely 
C. save 
D. prepare
Question 5.  A. struggle 
B. rent 
C. rely  
D. decide
Question 6.  A. budget 
B. guidance 
C. maturity  
D. self-study

3. Living alone often teaches young people to be (7) _______, as no one else manages their daily needs. They also become (8) _______ when dealing with housework, cooking, or repairing problems. Management skills also grow naturally, as every bill, appointment, or mistake must be tackled themselves. Through these experiences, (9) _______ character develops step by step in ways that shared living arrangements rarely provide.
Question 7.  A. personal 
B. overwhelmed 
C. independent 
D. part-time
Question 8.  A. responsible  
B. healthy 
C. basic 
D. overwhelmed
Question 9.  A. overwhelmed 
B. personal  
C. healthy 
D. part-time
cloze_group | câu: 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
4. In university, lectures pass quickly and teachers rarely check daily work. Without (10) _______, assignments often feel heavier and deadlines seem impossible to manage. Some students only rely on slides from class, then (11) _______ when exams approach. Others avoid the library and miss useful sources that could (12) _______ their study. Without regular practice, knowledge slips away little by little. Each missed chance makes the study more tiring, and the lack of discipline gradually turns into frustration and a loss of (13) _______.
Question 10. A. maturity 
B. self-study 
C. budget 
D. situation
Question 11. A. decide 
B. control 
C. struggle  
D. support
Question 12. A. support  
B. balance 
C. rent 
D. handle
Question 13. A. individual 
B. maturity 
C. motivation 
D. guidance 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
5. Living independently means making countless decisions without parents’ (14) _______. Choosing a 
major, selecting accommodation, or even (15) _______ daily expenses requires thorough thought. A 
student may hesitate between spending money on a new phone or saving for rent. Another may wonder 
whether to join an internship while (16) _______ heavy coursework. Each choice involves weighing 
options and predicting consequences. Independent life constantly presents such (17) _______, and 
decision-making becomes a daily exercise in responsibility and judgment.
Question 14. A. self-study 
B. guidance 
C. responsibility 
D. situation
Question 15. A. managing  
B. struggling 
C. relying 
D. renting
Question 16. A. handling  
B. supporting 
C. budgeting 
D. saving
Question 17. A. budgets 
B. skills 
C. individuals 
D. situations

Yet not everyone feels confident in the kitchen. Burnt pans, undercooked rice, and wasted money remind them how difficult life can be without (21) _______ cooking skills.

C. prepare  
D. save

8. A person needs many life (26) _______ to live independently, but the following two are the most important. When they know how to (28) _______ for emergencies, they can live without financially relying on others. Equally important is time management, which allows them to balance study, work, and personal (29) _______. By setting priorities and making schedules, they can complete tasks effectively without feeling (30) _______. With these two essential skills, independence becomes both manageable and meaningful.
Question 26. A. individuals 
B. situations 
C. skills 
D. budgets

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 ability khả năng n /əˈbɪləti/ A2 capability inability 2 limited có giới hạn adj /ˈlɪmɪtɪd/ B2 restricted, finite unlimited 3 involve bao gồm, liên quan đến v /ɪnˈvɒlv/ A2 include, entail exclude 4 effectively một cách hiệu quả adv /ɪˈfektɪvli/ B1 efficiently, successfully ineffectively 5 planning sự lập kế hoạch n /ˈplænɪŋ/ B1 6 unnecessary không cần thiết adj /ʌnˈnesəsəri/ B2 needless, redundant necessary 7 habit thói quen n /ˈhæbɪt/ A2 8 independence sự độc lập n /ˌɪndɪˈpendəns/ B2 dependence 9 experience trải nghiệm, kinh nghiệm n /ɪkˈspɪəriəns/ A2 inexperience 10 duty nhiệm vụ n /ˈdjuːti/ B1 responsibility, obligation freedom 11 tidy dọn dẹp v /ˈtaɪdi/ A2 untidy, mess up 12 train rèn luyện, đào tạo v /treɪn/ A1 13 review xem lại; sự đánh giá v/n /rɪˈvjuː/ A2 evaluate, assess 14 instruction hướng dẫn n /ɪnˈstrʌkʃn/ A2 guidance, direction 15 growth sự phát triển n /ɡrəʊθ/ B1 development 16 financial pressure áp lực tài chính n. phr /faɪˈnænʃl ˈpreʃə(r)/ B2 [Page 2] 17 practical thực tế, thiết thực adj /ˈpræktɪkl/ B1 realistic impractical 18 income thu nhập n /ˈɪnkʌm/ B2 earnings, wage 19 emergency tình huống khẩn cấp n /ɪˈmɜːdʒənsi/ B1 crisis, urgency 20 valuable quý giá, có giá trị adj /ˈvæljuəbl/ B1 precious, priceless valueless, worthless 21 teamwork làm việc nhóm n /ˈtiːmwɜːk/ B1 22 full-time toàn thời gian adj /ˌfʊl ˈtaɪm/ B2 23 employment việc làm n /ɪmˈplɔɪmənt/ B1 work, job, occupation unemployment 24 graduation tốt nghiệp n /ˌɡrædʒuˈeɪʃn/ B1 25 academic (thuộc) học thuật adj /ˌækəˈdemɪk/ B1 non-academic 26 carefully một cách cẩn thận adv /ˈkeəfəli/ A2 cautiously, attentively carelessly 27 excessive quá mức, thừa thãi adj /ɪkˈsesɪv/ B2 extreme, extravagant moderate 28 burden gánh nặng n /ˈbɜːdn/ C1 load BẢNG CẤU TRÚC STT Cấu trúc Nghĩa 1 allow somebody to do something cho phép ai đó làm gì 2 avoid doing something tránh làm việc gì đó 3 decide to do something quyết định làm gì 4 encourage somebody to do something khuyến khích ai đó làm gì 5 fo...
Question 1. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the things related to time 
management? 
A. giving priorities 
B. schedule planning 
C. wasting time 
D. good use of time
Question 2. The word “challenges” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. struggles 
B. benefits 
C. conditions 
D. impacts
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Time management means focusing only on work tasks while ignoring personal needs. 
B. Students practice time management by studying continuously without taking breaks. 
C. Calendars, to-do lists, or apps can help students balance deadlines and activities. 
D. Good time management requires avoiding schedules because they reduce daily flexibility. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
Helping children become independent is an important task for both families and schools. 
Independence does not appear suddenly but develops gradually through daily habits and experiences. 
When children are encouraged to make choices and take responsibility, they grow more confident in their 
abilities. 
One useful way is giving children simple duties such as tidying their room, setting the table, or 
helping with simple cooking. These tasks train discipline and teach that every action has consequences. 
Parents can also allow children to decide on small matters like choosing clothes or planning weekend 
activities. At school, teachers may guide students to practice self-study so they know how to review 
lessons without waiting for instructions. 
Children should also learn basic skills such as handling money, organizing schedules, and solving 
simple problems. When mistakes happen, adults need to provide gentle guidance instead of doing 
everything for children. Gradually, the experience of managing responsibilities helps young people gain 
confidence and maturity, making independence a natural part of their growth.
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______. 
A. experiences 
B. daily habits 
 C. children  
D. choices
Question 5. The word “simple” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. individual 
 B. effortless 
C. complex 
D. easy
Question 6. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 3? 
“When mistakes happen, adults need to provide gentle guidance instead of doing everything for 
children.” 
A. Parents need to prevent children from making any mistakes during their learning. 
B. Adults must always correct children’s errors immediately and let them learn. 
C. Parents should ignore mistakes completely so children can solve all issues alone. 
D. Adults should guide kids kindly through mistakes rather than fixing every problem.
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Small household duties like tidying or cooking can help children build independence. 
B. Children become independent suddenly without practicing habits or daily experiences. 
C. Teachers should always give instructions so students never practice self-study alone. 
D. Adults must handle all responsibilities for children to prevent mistakes and problems. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
When students enter university, many of them decide to take a part-time job. The reason is often 
financial pressure, since they must pay for rent, meals, and study materials without relying completely on 
their families. Besides money, students also want practical experience that cannot be gained from 
lectures alone.  
Part-time jobs provide clear benefits. Students can earn income to support their basic needs and even 
save for emergencies. The workplace also teaches valuable life skills, such as communication, teamwork, 
and problem-solving. Confidence grows when they deal with customers or supervisors. In addition, these 
experiences make their résumés stronger and prepare them better for full-time employment after 
graduation. 
However, difficulties appear if students cannot maintain a proper balance. Long shifts reduce study 
time and leave them too tired for classes. Some focus more on money than on learning, which harms 
academic results. Without discipline, they may feel overwhelmed by competing demands. The lack of 
energy can also affect health, and in extreme cases, students may lose their motivation for study. 
The negative effects can be reduced if students ma...

“Besides money, students also want practical experience that cannot be gained from lectures alone.” 
A. Students often hope that part-time jobs will provide both income and professional exposure. 
B. In addition to earning income, students seek real-world skills unavailable through lectures.  
C. Besides covering expenses, many students value working alongside academic studies. 
D. Practical experience is unnecessary for students, as money remains their only true concern.

D. Part-time jobs only create financial stress and never prepare students for employment later.

D. Paragraph 4

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 landscape phong cảnh n /ˈlændskeɪp/ B2 scenery 2 historic có tính lịch sử adj /hɪˈstɒrɪk/ B1 3 monument đài tưởng niệm, công trình n /ˈmɒnjumənt/ B2 memorial 4 modernization modernisation sự hiện đại hóa n /ˌmɒdənaɪˈzeɪʃn/ B2 5 cooperation co-operation sự hợp tác n /kəʊˌɒpəˈreɪʃn/ B2 collaboration 6 unite đoàn kết, thống nhất v /juˈnaɪt/ B2 7 safeguard bảo vệ; biện pháp bảo vệ v / n /ˈseɪfɡɑːd/ B2 8 humanity nhân loại n /hjuːˈmænəti/ B2 mankind 9 global toàn cầu adj /ˈɡləʊbl/ B1 worldwide local 10 inspire truyền cảm hứng v /ɪnˈspaɪə(r)/ B2 11 ritual nghi lễ n /ˈrɪtʃuəl/ C1 12 memory ký ức n /ˈmeməri/ A2 13 illustrate minh họa v /ˈɪləstreɪt/ B2 14 context bối cảnh n /ˈkɒntekst/ A2 15 emphasize emphasise nhấn mạnh v /ˈemfəsaɪz/ B1 stress [Page 2] 16 foreigner người nước ngoài n /ˈfɒrənə(r)/ C1 17 water puppetry múa rối nước n /ˈwɔːtə ˈpʌpɪtri/ B1 18 original nguyên gốc adj /əˈrɪdʒənl/ A2 19 adaptation sự thích nghi n /ˌædæpˈteɪʃn/ B2 adaption 20 promotion sự quảng bá, xúc tiến n /prəˈməʊʃn/ B2 21 resist chống lại, kháng cự v /rɪˈzɪst/ B2 oppose 22 tangible hữu hình adj /ˈtændʒəbl/ B2 physical intangible 23 intangible phi vật thể adj /ɪnˈtændʒəbl/ B2 abstract tangible 24 civilization civilisation nền văn minh n /ˌsɪvəlaɪˈzeɪʃn/ B2 25 identify xác định v /aɪˈdentɪfaɪ/ A2 26 visible có thể nhìn thấy adj /ˈvɪzəbl/ B2 invisible 27 survive sống sót, tồn tại v /səˈvaɪv/ B1 perish 28 regulate điều chỉnh, kiểm soát v /ˈreɡjuleɪt/ B2 control 29 interact tương tác v /ˌɪntərˈækt/ B2 30 combine kết hợp v /kəmˈbaɪn/ B2 blend separate 31 diverse đa dạng adj /daɪˈvɜːs/ B2 varied BẢNG CẤU TRÚC STT Cấu trúc Nghĩa 1 adjust to something thích nghi / điều chỉnh với điều gì 2 attach something to something gắn cái gì vào cái gì 3 be made up of được tạo thành từ / bao gồm 4 fail to do something thất bại / không làm được việc gì 5 help do something giúp làm việc gì 6 refer to something đề cập đến / ám chỉ điều gì 7 rely on...
Question 1. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“Without international cooperation, many treasures might be lost forever.” 
A. Valuable heritage sites will remain safe even when nations fail to cooperate. 
B. If countries do not work together, valuable heritage sites could disappear permanently.  
C. International cooperation guarantees that all cultural and natural sites are at risk. 
D. Nations can easily protect their wonders without relying on support from others.
Question 2. The word “supporting” in paragraph 2 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. identifying 
B. denying 
C. promoting 
D. discouraging
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. UNESCO only recognizes sites but does not provide any further support. 
B. Protecting heritage is unnecessary for the future of global communities. 
C. Local communities can protect monuments without training or international aid. 
D. UNESCO helps preserve heritage through advice, funding, and education programs.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
When people hear the word “heritage,” they may imagine ancient monuments or historic ruins. Yet 
many forms of heritage are “living,” because traditions are practiced every day and continue to change 
with society. Heritage, such as customs, rituals, and performances, is not locked in the past; it adapts to 
modern needs while still carrying memories of earlier generations. 
In Japan, the tea ceremony illustrates how living heritage adjusts to new contexts. Although the 
practice still emphasizes harmony and respect, it is now taught in schools, universities, and cultural 
centers for both locals and foreigners. In Vietnam, water puppetry once belonged only to village festivals 
but is now performed in modern theaters, supported by light and sound systems. These changes expand 
audiences while keeping the original spirit alive. 
Communities make these adaptations possible. Potters in Bat Trang design vases and bowls with 
patterns that suit contemporary houses, while musicians in many countries mix folk instruments with 
popular music. Even traditional festivals adopt digital promotion and tourism programs. Such examples 
reveal that traditions endure not by resisting change but by creatively engaging with...

Question 4. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the examples of heritage? 
A. rituals 
B. customs 
C. performances 
D. memories

A. harmony  
B. Japan 
C. living heritage 
D. the tea ceremony
Question 6. The word “contemporary” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. modern 
B. permanent 
C. old-fashioned 
D. collective
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Communities play no role in adapting heritage to suit contemporary life. 
B. Water puppetry in Vietnam remains limited to small village festivals only. 
C. Traditional customs survive only when they completely resist modern changes. 
D. The Japanese tea ceremony is now taught in schools and cultural centers.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
Heritage can be understood in two main forms: tangible and intangible. Tangible heritage refers to 
physical objects or places that people can see and touch. It includes monuments, temples, landscapes, and 
works of art that tell the story of human civilization. Intangible heritage, on the other hand, is made up of 
practices, skills, and knowledge that live in people’s memories and daily activities. 
Tangible heritage is often easier to identify and preserve because it exists in visible structures. 
The Great Wall of China, the Pyramids of Egypt, and Hội An Ancient Town in Vietnam are clear examples. 
These sites represent history through architecture, design, and construction techniques that have survived 
for centuries. Governments and organizations can restore damaged buildings, protect landscapes, and 
regulate tourism to keep them safe. 
Intangible heritage requires different approaches. It includes oral traditions, performing arts, social 
customs, and rituals that may disappear if not practiced regularly. Examples are Japanese Noh theater, the 
Indian festival of Diwali, or Vietnamese ca trù singing. Unlike monuments, these traditions rely on 
communities to continue teaching and performing them for ...

Question 8. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 1 as one of the examples of tangible 
heritage? 
A. temples 
B. monuments 
C. knowledge  
D. landscapes
Question 9. The word “survived” in paragraph 2 mostly means ______. 
A. maintained well 
B. partly disappeared 
C. looked the same 
D. remained alive
Question 10. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“Tangible heritage is often easier to identify and preserve because it exists in visible structures.” 
A. Physical heritage can be recognized and protected more easily as it appears in clear forms. 
B. Tangible heritage is always simple to identify and protect, no matter the situation. 
C. Because it can be seen, tangible heritage is easier to identify but not necessarily to preserve. 
D. Monuments and sites are difficult to recognize and maintain, even though they are visible.

C. excessive 
D. abundant

C. Governments can safely ignore visible heritage because communities protect it best. 
D. A temple remains meaningful even without any rituals or beliefs attached to it.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author mention the disappearance if there is a lack of practice? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author categorise heritage? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

specialist, expert unprofessional, amateur 4 condition điều kiện (làm việc, sống…) n /kənˈdɪʃn/ B1 state 5 opportunity cơ hội n /ˌɒpəˈtjuːnəti/ A2 chance 6 child labour lao động trẻ em n.phr /ˌtʃaɪld ˈleɪbə(r)/ C1 7 personality tính cách n /ˌpɜːrs.əˈnæl.ə.ti/ A2 character 8 consider cân nhắc, xem xét v /kənˈsɪdə(r)/ B1 evaluate, contemplate disregard, ignore 9 counsellor cố vấn, tư vấn viên n /ˈkaʊn.səl.ər/ C1 mentor 10 colleague đồng nghiệp n /ˈkɒl.iːɡ/ A2 co-worker, associate 11 relationship mối quan hệ n /rɪˈleɪʃnʃɪp/ A2 connection, bond separation 12 supportive hay giúp đỡ, ủng hộ adj /səˈpɔːtɪv/ C1 13 teamwork làm việc nhóm n /ˈtiːmwɜːk/ B1 collaboration, cooperation [Page 2] 14 work-life balance cân bằng công việc & cuộc sống n.phr /wɜːk laɪf ˈbæləns/ B2 15 exhausted kiệt sức adj /ɪɡˈzɔːstɪd/ B1 worn out, fatigued energetic 16 relax thư giãn v /rɪˈlæks/ A1 unwind, rest stress, worry 17 idea ý tưởng n /aɪˈdɪə/ A1 18 machine máy móc n /məˈʃiːn/ A1 19 remain duy trì, vẫn còn v /rɪˈmeɪn/ B1 20 creative sáng tạo adj /kriˈeɪtɪv/ A2 21 develop phát triển v /dɪˈveləp/ A2 grow, evolve decline, shrink 22 employee nhân viên n /ɪmˈplɔɪiː/ A2 worker, staff employer, boss 23 flexible linh hoạt adj /ˈfleksəbl/ B2 adaptable, adjustable rigid, fixed, inflexible 24 complete hoàn thành v /kəmˈpliːt/ A1 finish 25 connect kết nối v /kəˈnekt/ A2 link disconnect, separate 26 part-time bán thời gian adj /ˌpɑːt ˈtaɪm/ B2 full-time 27 earn kiếm được (tiền, thu nhập) v /ɜːn/ A2 28 soft skill kỹ năng mềm n /ˌsɒft ˈskɪl/ B2 29 responsible có trách nhiệm adj /rɪˈspɒnsəbl/ B1 irresponsible 30 career sự nghiệp n /kəˈrɪə(r)/ B1 profession, occupation 31 valuable quý giá, có giá trị adj /ˈvæljuəbl/ B1 precious, useful worthless 32 intern thực tập sinh n /ˈɪntɜːn/ B2 33 ...

B. employee

B. professional

D. supportive

2. In many parts of the world, many children are forced to work at a very young age instead of going to school. They often work in dangerous working (4) _______ such as factories, mines, or construction sites. As a result, they suffer from poor health, lack of education, and limited future (5) _______. The main reasons for child (6) _______ are poverty and the need to support their families.
Question 4.  A. machines 
B. colleagues 
C. conditions  
D. relationships
Question 5.  A. colleagues 
B. opportunities 
C. employees 
D. professionals
Question 6.  A. skill 
B. career 
C. idea 
D. labour

Asking for advice from teachers, parents, or career (9) _______ can also be very helpful.

B. condition 
C. balance 
D. professional

B. develop 
C. complete 
D. consider

Listening carefully and respecting different opinions also shows that you are a (12) _______ team member. Joining group activities or having lunch together is another way to connect with co-workers. When people feel comfortable with each other, (13) _______ becomes easier and more effective.
Question 10. A. conditions 
B. colleagues 
C. ideas 
D. counsellors

B. opportunities 
C. machines 
D. relationships

5. Having a good work-life (14) _______ makes life more meaningful. If we spend all of our time working, 
we will soon feel (15) _______ and unhappy. But if we only enjoy free time without effort, we may lose

_______. Simple habits like having dinner with family, taking a walk, or listening to music after work 
can bring peace of mind. With enough rest, we can return to work with more energy and better (17) 
_______.

B. labour 
C. balance  
D. career

B. exhausted 
C. creative 
D. flexible

B. apply

D. relax

Question 1. The word “require” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. demand 
B. purchase 
C. obtain 
D. advertise
Question 2. The word “themselves” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. practical skills 
B. part-time jobs 
C. internships 
D. young people
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Youth unemployment is caused only by the lack of available jobs. 
B. All graduates have the necessary skills and experience that employers demand. 
C. Governments should create more employment by supporting firms and industries. 
D. Schools need to prepare themselves through internships or part-time jobs. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
After finishing university, some students decide not to begin their career immediately but to spend 
one year exploring new experiences. This break helps them relax and gives them time to think 
carefully about their career. 
During a gap year, students can do many meaningful activities. Some choose to travel abroad to 
discover different cultures and broaden their horizons. Others may join volunteer programs to help 
disadvantaged communities, which not only improves their social awareness but also develops teamwork 
and problem-solving skills. A gap year can also be a good time to take short courses or learn new skills 
that will be useful for their future jobs. 
Although a gap year requires careful planning and financial support, it can be very beneficial. Young 
people often return with more confidence, maturity, and motivation. They also have a clearer idea of what 
kind of career they want to pursue. In conclusion, taking a gap year before work is a valuable opportunity 
that helps students grow personally and professionally.
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“This break helps them relax and gives them time to think carefully about their career.” 
A. Taking a gap year allows students to rest and reflect thoroughly on their future jobs. 
B. A gap year allows students to avoid making any immediate decisions about their careers. 
C. Students do not waste time during a gap year as they can relax and reconsider their studies. 
D. Relaxation during a gap year makes students temporarily forget about their career plans.
Question 5. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the meaningful activities? 
A. do internships 
B. travel abroad  
C. volunteer  
D. learn new skills
Question 6. The word “pursue” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. suit 
 B. chase 
C. avoid 
D. follow
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. A gap year prevents students from gaining teamwork or problem-solving skills. 
B. A gap year can help students gain confidence, maturity, and clearer career goals.  
C. Taking a gap year always guarantees students a successful career. 
D. Students on a gap year usually waste time without doing meaningful activities. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
In the modern world, both hard skills and soft skills play a very important role in education and 
work. Today, employers not only look at academic degrees but also consider how candidates can work 
with others and adapt to challenges. This trend shows that knowledge alone is not enough, and they need 
a balance of technical ability and personal qualities. 
Hard skills are the technical abilities that can be measured or tested. They include things like 
computer programming, accounting, engineering, or foreign language proficiency. These skills are usually 
learned through formal education, training programs, or practice. Without hard skills, it is almost 
impossible for a person to perform the basic tasks required in their job. For example, a doctor must 
know medical procedures, and an architect must be able to design buildings. 
Soft skills, on the other hand, are more about personal qualities and communication. They include 
teamwork, leadership, creativity, time management, and problem-solving. Soft skills are harder to 
measure, but they are essential in the workplace. A person with strong soft skills can cooperate with 
colleagues, manage stress, and build good relationships. This makes them more suc...
arrangement_group | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang - | rule_high
Question 8. The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to ______. 
A. employers  
B. others 
C. candidates  
D. challenges
Question 9. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“Without hard skills, it is almost impossible for a person to perform the basic tasks required in their 
job.” 
A. Performing advanced tasks in a job depends heavily on having hard skills. 
B. Lacking hard skills makes it very difficult for people to carry out their job duties. 
C. Employers need hard skills in order to manage the basic requirements of their positions. 
D. Employees cannot complete essential tasks at work if they do not have hard skills.
Question 10. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the soft skills? 
A. creativity 
B. teamwork 
C. accounting 
D. leadership
Question 11. The word “essential” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. inquiring 
B. moderate 
C. vital 
D. insignificant
Question 12. The word “replace” in paragraph 4 can be best replaced by ______. 
A. supplant 
B. remove 
C. promote 
D. introduce
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Hard skills alone are enough for long-term success in work and life. 
B. Employers today focus more on academic degrees. 
C. Hard skills are usually learned through formal education. 
D. Hard skills and soft skills can easily replace each other.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author mention an employment trend? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author emphasise the combination of hard and soft skills? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

(v) tác động n, v /ˈɪnfluəns/ B2 affect (v), effect (n) impact (n,v) 25 insurance bảo hiểm n /ɪnˈʃʊərəns/ B2 26 landmark địa danh n /ˈlændmɑːk/ C1 27 landscape phong cảnh n /ˈlændskeɪp/ B1 scenery 28 luxury (n) sự xa xỉ; (adj) xa xỉ n, adj /ˈlʌkʃəri/ B1 29 manage quản lý v /ˈmænɪdʒ/ B1 control 30 memory ký ức, kỷ niệm n /ˈmeməri/ B1 recollection, reminiscence 31 navigate điều hướng v /ˈnævɪɡeɪt/ B2 32 passion niềm đam mê n /ˈpæʃn/ B1 ardour apathy, indifference 33 passport hộ chiếu n /ˈpɑːspɔːt/ A2 34 perform biểu diễn v /pəˈfɔːm/ B1 35 platform n /ˈplætfɔːm/ B2 36 popularity sự phổ biến n /ˌpɒpjuˈlærəti/ B2 unpopularity 37 positive tích cực adj /ˈpɒ...

Perfect for families and friends who love comfort and adventure. (13) _______ before October 30 to receive a 20% discount and create memories that last a lifetime!

5. Ecotourism is a(n) (14) _______ form of travel that protects nature and supports local communities. 
Instead of staying in crowded resorts, travelers explore natural areas, join (15) _______ activities, and 
learn about local culture. They may hike in forests, watch (16) _______, or help clean beaches. Though 
it may be less (17) _______ than luxury trips, ecotourism brings meaningful experiences and teaches 
people to travel sustainably. It’s a great way to enjoy the world while caring for the planet.
Question 14. A. unexpected 
B. breathtaking 
C. responsible  
D. solo

B. eco-friendly  
C. unexpected 
D. breathtaking

D. wildlife

D. responsible

6. Sports are one of the best forms of entertainment because they keep people (18) _______ and healthy. Playing football, swimming, or jogging helps (19) _______ stress and improves physical fitness. Watching sports also brings excitement and connects people through shared (20) _______ and teamwork. Instead of spending hours on screens, joining or following sports encourages a positive lifestyle and (21) _______. Whether you play or cheer, sports offer both fun and health benefits — a perfect balance of body and mind.

B. active  
C. unexpected

Learning basic local phrases helps you connect with people and shows respect for their culture. Most importantly, keep an open mind. (25) _______ new lifestyles may challenge your habits, but that’s what makes traveling abroad meaningful.

It also requires (28) _______ your own safety and budget without a travel companion. However, the rewards are priceless. You gain confidence, independence, and unforgettable travel experiences. Meeting locals, tasting exotic (29) _______, and exploring hidden attractions give you a deeper understanding of the world. Each journey (30) _______ your horizons and turns every challenge into a meaningful travel memory.

B. Solo

Question 1. The word “circulate” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. combine 
B. pursue 
C. renew 
D. spread
Question 2. The word “themselves” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. art shows 
 
B. visitors  
 
C. local festivals 
 
D. street performances
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Entertainment and tourism together can connect different cultures and people. 
B. Advertisements are the only factor influencing people’s travel decisions. 
C. Travelers today no longer care about cultural understanding or local traditions. 
D. Festivals and art events have no impact on the local economy or communities. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
Cultural festivals offer visitors a chance to witness local traditions, music, dance, and art that reflect 
a nation’s identity. Events such as Holi in India, the Lantern Festival in Taiwan, or Oktoberfest in 
Germany create a joyful atmosphere that draws millions every year. These celebrations allow travelers to 
see a country’s culture in action rather than just reading about it in books or online. 
One reason festivals are so appealing is that they combine culture with entertainment. Tourists 
enjoy the colorful parades, traditional performances, and local food while learning about history and 
customs. Many travelers even plan their trips around festival seasons to join the celebrations. This kind 
of travel not only provides excitement but also helps preserve cultural heritage by keeping traditional arts 
and values alive. 
Moreover, cultural festivals bring strong economic benefits. When visitors attend, they spend on 
transportation, accommodation, and souvenirs, helping local businesses grow. At the same time, festivals 
promote understanding and friendship among people from different backgrounds. They remind us that 
travel is not just about sightseeing – it’s also about connecting, sh...
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. The word “joyful” in paragraph 1 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. meaningless  
B. happy 
C. avoidable 
D. miserable
Question 5. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“One reason festivals are so appealing is that they combine culture with entertainment.” 
A. Festivals are popular since they focus more on entertainment than on traditions. 
B. Festivals are interesting mainly for their cultural meaning, not for enjoyment. 
C. Festivals attract people because they offer both cultural experiences and fun activities.  
D. Festivals appeal to visitors even though they do not include much cultural value.
Question 6. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the things visitors spend 
on? 
A. transportation 
B. sightseeing 
C. accommodation 
D. souvenirs
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Cultural festivals are mainly organized for local residents, not for travelers. 
B. Cultural festivals help preserve traditions while providing enjoyable experiences.  
C. Travelers attend festivals only to relax, not to learn about different cultures. 
D. Today's festivals focus on more economic growth rather than cultural exchange. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
Hosting the Olympic Games has long been viewed as both a challenge and an opportunity for the 
host nation. Beyond the excitement of sports, the event serves as a global stage that showcases the 
country’s image, infrastructure, and hospitality to millions of international visitors. Cities like Tokyo, 
London, and Sydney have used the Games to promote themselves as modern, safe, and welcoming 
destinations, leaving lasting impressions on tourists worldwide. 
The most direct benefit of hosting the Olympics is the surge in tourism. During the event, hotels, 
restaurants, and entertainment venues are filled with visitors from every continent. Even after the Games 
end, many travelers continue to visit the city to see the iconic stadiums and parks featured on television. 
For example, Barcelona’s successful 1992 Olympics transformed it into one of Europe’s top tourist 
destinations. 
Another advantage lies in the improvement of infrastructure. To prepare for the Games, host cities 
often upgrade airports, public transport, and urban areas. These developments make the city more 
attractive and convenient for future visitors. Cultural programs and festivals organized alongside the 
Olympics also help ...
standalone_mcq | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang - | rule_high
Question 8. The word “showcases” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______. 
A. supplants 
B. presents 
C. hides 
D. functions
Question 9. The word “it” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. Barcelona 
B. Olympics  
C. television 
D. Europe
Question 10. According to paragraph 3, countries preparing for the Games often upgrade all of the 
following, EXCEPT _______. 
A. local traditions  
B. airports 
C. public transport 
D. urban areas
Question 11. The word “strengthening” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. requiring 
B. improving 
C. weakening 
D. intensifying
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
“Without clear strategies, the massive investments required can lead to debt or unused facilities.” 
A. Without good planning, the Olympics will always bring financial success to the host city. 
B. If plans are unclear, large spending on investment may cause debt or unused infrastructure.  
C. Clear strategies can make Olympic investments unnecessary or too expensive. 
D. Planning means the Olympics will not need much investment in facilities and urban areas.
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Organizing the Olympics brings improvement to global infrastructure and image. 
B. The Olympics only benefit athletes and the organizers and have no effect on tourism. 
C. Host cities always gain only short-term advantages from the Olympics. 
D. Hosting the Olympic Games boosts tourism and promotes the host city’s image.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore the benefits to a specific industry? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author discuss some risks of hosting the Olympics? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

C. commitment 
D. poverty
cloze_group | câu: 7, 8, 9 | trang - | rule_high
3. Global organisations such as UNESCO and UNICEF have helped Vietnam (7) _______ its education system through training programs, digital initiatives, and financial support. They work with the government to (8) _______ new curricula that encourage creativity and critical thinking. In remote areas, these organisations fund projects that build schools, train teachers, and provide learning materials for (9) _______ children.

B. join 
C. reduce 
D. modernise
Question 8.  A. join 
B. develop  
C. promote 
D. fund

D. economic

Both sides aim to create a society where all children can grow up healthy, confident, and free from (13) _______.

B. vision 
C. lead 
D. goal

B. economy 
C. poverty 
D. cooperation

B. equality  
C. message 
D. vision

B. security 
C. cooperation

[Page 6]

their healthcare systems. It provides technical guidance, training, and research support to improve disease 
(15) _______ and treatment. During health emergencies, the WHO coordinates international responses 
and ensures that medical supplies reach (16) _______ areas. The organization also promotes fair access 
to vaccines and medicines, especially in developing nations. By setting global health standards and 
encouraging (17) _______, the WHO plays a vital role in protecting public health and building stronger, 
more resilient healthcare systems.

B. vulnerable 
C. global  
D. disadvantaged

B. commitment

D. dispute

B. social

B. cooperation

/əˈpriːʃieɪt/ B1 value, respect 2 collective tập thể, chung adj /kəˈlektɪv/ C1 joint, shared individual 3 competitiveness khả năng cạnh tranh n /kəmˈpetɪtɪvnəs/ C1 rivalry 4 conflict xung đột, mâu thuẫn n, v /ˈkɒnflɪkt/ B2 clash, dispute agreement 5 crisis khủng hoảng n /ˈkraɪsɪs/ B2 6 division sự chia rẽ n /dɪˈvɪʒn/ B2 separation, split unity 7 empathy sự thấu cảm n /ˈempəθi/ C1 8 enterprise doanh nghiệp n /ˈentəpraɪz/ B2 business 9 establish thành lập v /ɪˈstæblɪʃ/ B2 found, set up 10 exchange trao đổi, sự trao đổi n, v /ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ/ B1 11 fast-changing thay đổi nhanh chóng adj /ˌfɑːst mang tính hội nhập adj /ɪnˈkluːsɪv/ C1 exclusive 16 innovation sự đổi mới n /ˌɪnəˈveɪʃn/ C1 17 milestone cột mốc quan trọng n /ˈmaɪlstəʊn/ B2 landmark [Page 2] 18 negotiate đàm phán v /nɪˈɡəʊʃieɪt/ C1 19 open-minded cởi mở adj /ˌəʊpən ˈmaɪndɪd/ C1 narrow-minded 20 peacefully một cách hòa bình adv /ˈpiːsfəli/ B1 peaceably violently 21 region khu vực n /ˈriːdʒən/ A2 22 scholarship học bổng n B2 26 trade agreement hiệp định thương mại n phr /treɪd əˈɡriːmənt/ B2 27 transformation sự thay đổi, chuyển đổi n /ˌtrænsfəˈmeɪʃn/ B2 28 trust niềm tin n /trʌst/ B2 doubt 29 understanding sự thấu hiểu n /ˌʌndəˈstændɪŋ/ A2 30 visibility mức độ được chú ý, độ nhận diện n /ˌvɪzəˈbɪləti/ C1 exposure, prominence [Page 3] PRACTICE Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of the questions from 1 to 3. Education helps ASEAN students understand each other better and feel...
Question 1. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 1? 
“Scholarships give more young people a chance to study abroad, even if their families cannot afford 
it.” 
A. Wealthy students can easily travel abroad to explore new learning opportunities. 
B. Financial support allows students from even poor families to study in other countries.  
C. Students who work hard can afford to pay for overseas education themselves. 
D. Only a few privileged students receive funding for international university programs.
Question 2. The word “they” in paragraph 2 refers to _______. 
A. scholarships  
B. their countries 
C. ASEAN students 
D. local problems
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Cultural exchange programs often make students less interested in cooperation. 
B. Scholarships are only offered to students who can afford to study abroad. 
C. ASEAN students mainly study overseas to enjoy different lifestyles. 
D. Education encourages ASEAN students to connect and understand one another better.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
In today’s globalised and fast-changing world, no country can face every challenge alone. Problems 
such as climate change, health crises, poverty, and war have impacts that cross borders and affect millions 
of lives. International cooperation allows nations to share knowledge, technology, and experience, 
creating stronger and faster responses to global issues. By working together, countries build trust and 
develop collective solutions that bring long-term stability. 
Cooperation also promotes peace, dialogue, and cultural understanding. Through organisations like 
the United Nations, ASEAN, and WHO, countries can negotiate peacefully and prevent conflicts before 
they grow worse. Educational and cultural exchanges help citizens appreciate diversity and develop 
empathy for others. When people understand one another, they are more likely to support harmony instead 
of division. 
Beyond solving problems, working together brings opportunities for growth. Trade agreements, 
exchange programs, and research partnerships open new paths for development and innovation. By 
cooperating instead of competing, countries can create a fairer, safer, and more sustainable world for 
future generations.
standalone_mcq | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. According to paragraph 1, international cooperation allows countries to share all of the 
following, EXCEPT _______. 
A. knowledge 
B. poverty 
C. technology 
D. experience
Question 5. The word “promotes” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. requires 
B. decides 
C. discourages 
D. fosters
Question 6. The word “development” in paragraph 3 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. evolution 
B. degradation 
C. prevention 
D. consideration
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Countries can easily solve crises like wars and poverty without outside help. 
B. International cooperation enables nations to handle global problems more effectively.  
C. Global challenges are limited to specific regions and rarely affect other nations. 
D. Cooperation often leads to more conflicts rather than peace and understanding. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
The Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) is a regional organisation established in 1989 to 
promote free trade, investment, and economic cooperation among countries in the Asia–Pacific region. 
Its main goal is to support sustainable growth, enhance regional integration, and strengthen connections 
between member economies. APEC also focuses on human resource development, digital transformation, 
and inclusive innovation to ensure that all members benefit from globalisation. 
Vietnam officially joined APEC in 1998, a time when the country was deepening its international 
integration after years of economic reform under the Đổi Mới policy. Joining APEC marked an important 
milestone, allowing Vietnam to expand trade relations, attract foreign investment, and participate in 
discussions shaping the region’s economic future. It was also a strategic step to enhance its international 
reputation and strengthen partnerships with developed economies. 
Through APEC, Vietnam has gained access to larger export markets, advanced technologies, and 
professional training opportunities. The organisation has helped the country improve its business 
environment, promote tourism, and enhance the c...
standalone_mcq | câu: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 | trang 6-6 | rule_high
Question 8. The word “established” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______. 
A. recognised 
B. demolished 
C. set up 
D. laid off
Question 9. The word “its” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. Vietnam  
 
B. a strategic step  
 
C. APEC  
 
D. the Đổi Mới policy
Question 10. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 3 as one of the benefits Vietnam 
has gained from joining APEC? 
A. professional training opportunities 
B. access to larger export markets 
C. improved business environment 
D. better competitiveness globally
Question 11. The word “enhance” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. inspire 
B. lower 
C. visualise 
D. promote
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
“Strengthening education and sustainable industries will help Vietnam make full use of APEC 
cooperation.” 
A. By investing in education and sustainable sectors, Vietnam can maximise the benefits of APEC 
cooperation. 
B. Vietnam depends mainly on natural resources instead of education to join APEC successfully. 
C. Expanding heavy industries will reduce Vietnam’s need for further cooperation with APEC members. 
D. APEC countries must avoid sharing educational or industrial strategies to protect national development.
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Hosting APEC events has had little impact on Vietnam’s international image. 
B. APEC focuses only on trade liberalisation and ignores other development areas. 
C. Vietnam joined APEC mainly to compete with other Asian economies. 
D. Vietnam still struggles to meet APEC’s high standards of trade liberalisation.  

 

[Page 6]
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author list the main and other goals of APEC? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention some challenges still facing Vietnam? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4

(v) mặc cả n, v /ˈbɑːɡən/ A2 19 cash tiền mặt n /kæʃ/ A2 20 worth đáng giá adj /wɜːθ/ B1 21 economical tiết kiệm adj /ˌiːkəˈnɒmɪkl/ B2 22 stable ổn định adj /ˈsteɪbl/ C1 unstable 23 interest tiền lãi n /ˈɪntrəst/ B1 24 retirement sự nghỉ hưu n /rɪˈtaɪəmənt/ B2 25 financial thuộc tài chính adj /faɪˈnænʃl/ B1 26 charge (v) tính phí; (n) khoản phí v, n /tʃɑːdʒ/ B1 27 order (n) đơn đặt hàng; (v) đặt mua n, v /ˈɔːdə(r)/ A2 28 business doanh nghiệp; việc kinh doanh n /ˈbɪznəs/ A2 company, firm; commerce, trade 29 profit lợi nhuận n /ˈprɒfɪt/ B2 loss 30 retail bán lẻ n /ˈriːteɪl/ C1 31 manage quản lý v /ˈmænɪdʒ/ B1 32 income thu nhập n /ˈɪnkʌm/ B2 earnings 33 extra (adj) thêm, phụ; (n) phần thêm adj, n /ˈekstrə/ A2/B1 additional (adj) 34 afford có đủ khả năng chi trả v /əˈfɔːd/ B1 35 earn kiếm được (tiền) v /ɜːn/ A2 36 invest đầu tư v /ɪnˈvest/ B2 37 value giá trị n /ˈvæljuː/ B1 worth 38 saving tiền tiết kiệm (thường ở dạng số nhiều) n /ˈseɪvɪ...

During these times, they give attractive (12) _______, making items even cheaper than usual. D. item

D. orders

_______, sellers sometimes give fruit or vegetables instead of cash. These creative ways make shopping 
more friendly and fun. They also show respect for buyers and keep local traditions alive.

B. change 
C. charge 
D. sale

[Page 1] TỪ VỰNG & ĐỌC ĐIỀN – ĐỌC HIỂU CƠ BẢN Cô Vũ Thị Mai Phương BẢNG TỪ VỰNG STT Từ vựng Nghĩa Từ loại Phiên âm Cấp độ Từ đồng nghĩa Từ trái nghĩa 1 aisle lối đi giữa các dãy (trong siêu thị, nhà thờ, máy bay...) n /aɪl/ B2 2 barter (v) trao đổi hàng đổi hàng; (n) sự trao đổi v, n /ˈbɑːtə(r)/ C1 3 browse xem qua, lướt v /braʊz/ B2 4 compare so sánh v /kəmˈpeə(r)/ B1 5 cosmetics mỹ phẩm n /kɒzˈmetɪk/ B2 6 delivery giao hàng n /dɪˈlɪvəri/ B1 7 description mô tả n /dɪˈskrɪpʃn/ B1 8 digital kỹ thuật số adj /ˈdɪdʒɪtl/ B1 9 directly trực tiếp adv /dəˈrektli/ B1 in person indirectly 10 display trưng bày v /dɪˈspleɪ/ B1 exhibit 11 evolve phát triển, tiến hóa v /ɪˈvɒlv/ C1 develop 12 exchange (n) việc đổi trả; (v) đổi, trả lại n, v /ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ/ B1 13 face-to-face trực tiếp, gặp mặt adj, adv /ˌfeɪs tə ˈfeɪs/ B1 in-person, direct virtual, online 14 fiercely một cách mãnh liệt, dữ dội adv /ˈfɪəsli/ C1 intensely gently 15 huge rất lớn, khổng lồ adj /hjuːdʒ/ B1 enormous, massive tiny, small 16 living standard mức sống n. phr /ˈlɪvɪŋ ˈstændəd/ B2 17 loyalty lòng trung thành, sự gắn bó n /ˈlɔɪəlti/ B2 [Page 2] 18 medium phương tiện n /ˈmiːdiəm/ C2 19 membership thành viên, quyền thành viên n /ˈmembəʃɪp/ B1 20 organization sự tổ chức, sắp xếp n /ˌɔːɡənaɪˈzeɪʃn/ C1 arrangement 21 original nguyên bản, ban đầu adj, n /əˈrɪdʒənl/ B1 22 payment khoản trả tiền, thanh toán n /ˈpeɪmənt/ B1 23 promotion khuyến mãi, quảng cáo n /prəˈməʊʃn/ C1 24 refund (n) sự hoàn tiền; (v) hoàn tiền n, v /ˈriːfʌnd/ B1/C1 25 reliable đáng tin cậy adj /rɪˈlaɪəbl/ B1 trustworthy, dependable unreliable, untrustworthy 26 represent đại diện, tượng trưng v /ˌreprɪˈzent/ B2 stand for, symbolize 27 review đánh giá n, v /rɪˈvjuː/ B1 28 serve phục vụ v /sɜːv/ A2 29 trade giao dịch n /treɪd/ B1 30 ultimate cuối cùng, tối thượng adj /ˈʌltɪmət/ B2 final, supreme initial 31 unsuitable không phù hợp adj /ʌnˈsuːtəbl/ B2 suitable 32 wealth sự giàu có n /welθ/ B2 prosperity, affluence poverty, destitution [Page 3] PRACTICE Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indic...
Question 1. The word “they” in paragraph 1 refers to _______. 
A. reviews  
 
B. buyers  
 
C. choices 
 
D. home delivery services
Question 2. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 2? 
“Although it takes more time, many people still prefer in-store shopping for the sense of trust it 
brings.” 
A. Many buyers like traditional stores, though they require a longer time to make payments. 
B. Customers save time by avoiding stores and relying only on online product reviews. 
C. Despite being slower, many shoppers choose direct shopping as it feels reliable.  
D. People prefer offline shopping because they cannot trust digital platforms completely.
Question 3. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. In-store customers cannot touch or try the products before making payment. 
B. Online shoppers often rely on pictures and descriptions instead of seeing real products.  
C. Online shopping rarely includes reviews or detailed information for buyers. 
D. Visiting physical stores saves both time and effort compared to online shopping. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 4]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 4 to 7. 
Modern supermarkets have become an essential part of city life, offering customers a convenient 
and comfortable shopping experience. With wide aisles, clear organization, and air conditioning, 
shoppers can conveniently find everything they need in one place – from food and household goods to 
cosmetics and clothing. Most supermarkets display clear price tags, making it easy to compare costs and 
choose products that fit each customer’s budget. 
Another advantage is the variety of sales and discounts offered regularly. During weekends or 
special holidays, supermarkets launch big promotions that attract hundreds of buyers. Customers can save 
money while enjoying the excitement of finding great deals. Loyalty programs and membership cards 
also give frequent shoppers extra points or rewards, encouraging them to return. 
Finally, supermarkets provide a reliable refund and exchange policy, giving customers more 
confidence when buying products. If an item is broken or unsuitable, it can easily be returned or replaced. 
This professional service, combined with high product quality, makes modern supermarkets a trusted and 
enjoyable place for shopping.
arrangement_group | câu: 4, 5, 6, 7 | trang 5-5 | rule_high
Question 4. The word “organization” in paragraph 1 is CLOSEST in meaning to _______. 
A. requirement 
B. company 
C. appointment 
D. arrangement
Question 5. The word “frequent” in paragraph 2 is OPPOSITE in meaning to _______. 
A. rare 
B. regular 
C. current 
D. reusable
Question 6. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the passage as one of the advantages of 
shopping in supermarkets? 
A. free home delivery 
B. clear prices and organised layout 
C. discounts and membership rewards 
D. easy refund and exchange policy
Question 7. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Refund policies make customers feel less confident about their purchases. 
B. Customers cannot exchange items at supermarkets once they have been paid for. 
C. Shoppers never compare prices when buying in modern supermarkets. 
D. Supermarkets attract buyers through discounts, promotions, and reward programs.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

[Page 5]

 

 
Read the following passage and mark the letter A, B, C, or D to indicate the correct answer to each of 
the questions from 8 to 15. 
In today’s world, many people see money as the ultimate goal of life. They work hard, compete 
fiercely, and even measure success by how much they earn. However, money was never meant to define 
a person’s worth or happiness. To understand it correctly, we need to look back at its history and original 
purpose to see what it truly represents. 
In ancient times, people didn’t use money at all. They exchanged products directly—rice for salt, 
clothes for tools, or animals for food. This system, called barter, worked only when both sides needed 
what the other had. Later, people began using objects like shells, metal coins, or rare stones as a medium 
of exchange. These early forms of money made trading simpler, fairer, and more efficient. 
When real money appeared, it brought huge advantages. It allowed people to store value, measure 
wealth, and build more complex economies. Over time, money evolved into many forms, such as cash, 
credit cards, and now digital payments or online wallets. Unfortunately, as it became more powerful, 
people started treating it as the center of life rather than a helpful tool to improve living standards. 
As we can see, money itself is neither good nor bad – it’s just a means to achi...

Question 8. The word “original” in paragraph 1 can be best replaced by ______. 
A. fake 
B. honest 
C. authentic 
D. initial
Question 9. The word “They” in paragraph 2 refers to ______. 
A. people  
B. ancient times 
C. products  
D. animals
Question 10. Which of the following is NOT mentioned in paragraph 2 as one of the media of exchange 
in the past? 
A. rare stones  
B. shells 
C. rice 
D. metal coins
Question 11. The word “wealth” in paragraph 3 is OPPOSITE in meaning to ______. 
A. motivation 
B. abundance 
C. richness 
D. poverty
Question 12. Which of the following best paraphrases the underlined sentence in paragraph 4? 
“Don’t see money as everything, but as something that helps create and exchange values.” 
A. People should earn as much money as possible to generate value for society. 
B. We should treat money as a tool for value creation and exchange, not as all needed for life.  
C. Money becomes meaningful only when it is used for daily trading activities. 
D. Lacking money proves that a person focuses more on moral rather than material values.
Question 13. Which of the following is TRUE according to the passage? 
A. Early forms of money, such as shells and metal coins, made trading simpler and fairer. 
B. People in ancient times used money mainly to show their social position and power. 
C. The barter system allowed trade even when only one side needed the other’s goods. 
D. Digital payments have replaced all other types of money in today’s world.
Question 14. In which paragraph does the author explore the history of money? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
Question 15. In which paragraph does the author mention various forms of modern money? 
A. Paragraph 1 
B. Paragraph 2 
C. Paragraph 3 
D. Paragraph 4
